reviews.text:
Really good experience, well worth the money

Mook our guide was lovely and ensured we were looked after during the excursion.

Canoing was amazing a gentle amble through the little amazon river lots of wildlife and soectacular views.

The off road section was spectacular, with frequent stops to look at flora and fauna.

The restaurant was in the middle of a fish lagoon and food was delicious.

We didnt realise there was a temple visit and werent prepared as women need to ensure they are wearing trousers and shoulders, fortunately one of the other guests lianed my wife a towel so we could enjoy the experience in full.

All in all a great trip, its a long day and rather tiring as it was 35 degrees celcius all day!! 

Well done team 😊
reviews.title: Amazing Day Out

reviews.text:
The temples were very memorable the history and religion behind them.
Unfortunately, our Guid was too unresponsive to serious questions about the female side. Although he meant if he didn't know something he would say it too that he didn't. Also as far as wildlife is concerned, the statement that they are lower life forms and have no standing in religion but you are punished more if you kill an elephant than a human. In this day and age a no go sorry
reviews.title: Temple tour in German

reviews.text: hellish two and a half hour drive from Khao Lak with awful traffic. We first stopped at a temple which was disappointing..having passed some stunning temples we ended up at this one which was more tourist trap than anything else. Our driver didn't make it clear to the group the time we had to be back...in fact 50 minutes, which meant we got stuck in a stuffy hot minibus for 35 minutes while we waited for the ones who though he had said 5pm. On to the market which was endless tourist tat, hot and crowded, then another hellish drive back.
reviews.title: disappointing

reviews.text: We booked the tour through booking.com. On the drive to the boat we were with four adults and 5 children. That was a good group size. Our guide - Ivan - not a local himself, has been at our side - especially the participating children - in German at any time with advice and deed and gave some tips for the further holiday. That was very good. The whole day was very well plated, the individual spots well selected. Especially the rich and very good food in the stilt village should be highlighted here. We can recommend the tour - with our guide.
reviews.title: All-round successful day
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
We selected this trip from the hotel brochure and were not dissapointed.
We were picked up from the hotel by the aircon minibus and was greeted by "Volker" our tour guide. What a nice guy he is, very knowledgable, laid back, friendly and led us on an amazing tour through the day. He also took the time to take photographs for us all and made sure we had great memories of the day.
Lunch was served at the elephant sancturary and was a nice surprise in both quantity and quality.
This is a must do tour that offers excellent value with great experiences at a nice steady pace.
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari

reviews.text:
Very ambivalent!
We have booked the "3 days Khao Sok tree house and lake" (not listed here) for the price of 12,500BHT per person. Sounded very good in the prospectus and also in the advice. Unfortunately, the facts were quite different. First day with market visit to Takua Pa, elephant camp, visit to Wat Tam Panturat Temple, canoe trip in the wilderness of Sok River and overnight stay at Rock an Treehouse Resort with lunch and dinner just PERFECT! 
On the second day we continued to Cheow Land Stau-See. As the journey on the lake towards the Floss House grew, the impressive surrounding scenery became more and more positively impressed. The control of the raft house was still visually beautiful. Then followed the disillusionments:
- 
The accommodations were simple (no objection to that), but if these "chambers" are then clearly dirty, in the sanitary area also mold was still present, the "enjoyment" stops suddenly.
-
The meals at lunch and dinner were largely identical, but in the evening there was another, let's call it "fried" fish, which was on its way to the Dörr fish in the processing by the chef (?).
In the morning there was a frugal breakfast consisting of a slice of toast, a cold fried egg and 3 pineapple particles and two small melon slices. IT WAS NO LONGER THERE!
-
And for the use of the canoes in the lake "resort" we had to pay 200 BHT on site, which was not mentioned in the brochure and also the personal advice was not mentioned.
-
The stay was complemented by EXTREMELY unfriendly staff.

We and also our 2 fellow travellers were happy when we went back to the starting point and we were able to establish for ourselves: 
- beautiful panorama
- and having seen 3 wild elephants at dinner on one of the boat game drives,

and were now away from the accommodation.

The program of the third day was, not necessarily, to remember it. 
All points, such as simple accommodation on the lake and additional payment for the use of the canoes were then the subject of a letter to the agency / organizer. I was not concerned with any refunds due to poor performance(s). I just wanted information about how it could have happened,

I received an answer which my questions answered only rudimentarily, at least with the realisation that the author of the answer (including our consultant) admitted that he had perhaps, possibly, not argued quite objectively in the tender. I then wrote to the author again with the request to go into the exact points and how the topic with the 200BHT is to watch. I'll wait until today for the answer.

We did not realize our reaction in the form of "planned other additional booking" due to the experience and behavior of the agency at the agency.

Worth mentioning is the good tour guide, the equally good driver and the vehicle used.
reviews.title: At / with the agency not again

reviews.text: We had already booked the tour through Khaolak-Adventures.de in Germany. At the hotel in Khao Lak we were picked up on time. As a guide we had Anton?, he called himself the Flying Dutchman as he ran from shadow to shadow. He was able to give us very good insights into Buddhism, as he himself lived as a monk for 12 years. This tour was completely successful. Whether it was about the history of temples, Buddhism or the life of Thais, he could tell us a lot and entertainingly about it. The food was also very good.
reviews.title: It was an all-round successful day

reviews.text: We really enjoyed our trip. Philipp was our host who was fantastic. The drive was around 1 hr to rhe port where we got a long tail boat through the mangroves to James bond island. This was a great experience on the boat. At james bond island it was fairly quiet, no big crowds which was good. We then vixited panyee floating island which we had read about before goinh to thailand so it was good to experience this. We also had food here which was nice. After returning to the port we visited and fed the monkeys and a temple cave with a lying down Buddha. Greay day, we woykd recommend this tour as you get to visit some great cultural island/places.
reviews.title: Great day out with no crowds
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: Beautiful early day trip to do snorkeling on three different spots incl. delicious lunch, awesome beaches and impressive nature! Highlight is the visit of the Moken!
reviews.title: Amazing trip with the family!

reviews.text: Unfortunately, neither the offroad ride, nor the waterfall were really great... the waterfall was just a little trickle in the bushes and the offroad ride were 10 minutes over an agricultural gravel road.... Apart from potholes, there was nothing with off-road... and I must also say that we were really unlucky with our guide. There were 4 children who actually only disturbed him and questions from them did not answer. He was very disrespectful to them. He was also not motivated to take children's food requests into account. But we had already done some tours with khao lak Discovery, and knew that there is food for children. only on several requests, he ordered it... even the guides from the raft trip were not bothered to show us anything... so for us the trip was a real shame for the money...
reviews.title: Only partially recommended

reviews.text:
We had a very good tour with a very good guide named Heiko. He showed us a lot and explained, you could tell that he loved the country and the people and liked to pass on his knowledge. We booked the tour Khao Sok 2 days with an overnight stay at Rock&treehouse Resort. We saw a market, were allowed to feed and bathe elephants, saw a temple, visited the dam, hiked through the jungle, visited a cave and still received information about the land, people, wildlife and plants in between. 
Highly recommended.
reviews.title: Wonderful tour Khao Sok with overnight stay

reviews.text: The tour Khao Sok Jungle Trekking was a wonderful experience thanks to our tour guide Aris. She planned the process so that we could discover the Jungle away from the other visitors. Your information was very interesting. For me as a hobby photographer, she was always looking for the best possible locations for my photos. She also paid attention to our well-being. The pace was adjusted, breathers were taken and the bathing opportunity has cooled and wonderful. She chose the paths off the beaten track so that even less experienced people could still keep up. I can fully recommend this tour and especially tour guide Aris.
reviews.title: An eventful day in the jungle with many exotic animals

reviews.text: This was a good day trip to the beautiful Surin Islands but there is room for improvement. Our guide was not the best - only really focussed on timekeeping for the day and not on giving us information about the places we visited or even talking with the passengers. For a tour at this price point, I expect a more engaged guide. It is a very long, noisy and bumpy boat trip (90 mins each way). This cannot be helped but something to keep in mind when booking this trip. The location was beautiful and snorkelling was very good. We did 3 snorkelling stops, a visit to the Moken village and a visit to one of the islands where we stopped for lunch for about 90 mins. Pick up for this tour was early (5.30am) but I recommend this as we were ahead of the crowds.
reviews.title: Beautiful location, average tour guide

reviews.text: This tour and another was picked at the office with the support of the team there, we explained exactly what we wanted and they advised us on what they could do as a best match. When returning to Khao Lak I will definitely use their services again as on both tours everything was as we wanted , expected and more besides.
reviews.title: Great advice and tours
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
We use this company for our tours 
Wow can’t moan it was amazing 
Our tour guides were fantastic 
It’s a must when you get there 
Patty you are a fantastic person 
The cheow lan lake it’s a must the views are out of this world
reviews.title: Discover Thailand

reviews.text:
We booked this day trip through our hotel “ Flora Khao Lak “
Our guide was Anne , who had an excellent knowledge of the places we were going to visit . 
The trip is called the Khao Lak Safari . We bathed Asian elephants, rafted down a river on a bamboo raft,visited the Royal Navy Turtle Conservation Centre, lunched by the river and swam under a waterfall.
We visited the Police boat , which was carried several kilometres by the Tsunami  , in 2004.
Last but not least, we visited a traditional Buddhist temple . 
The cost for this full day trip was : Adult - 3000. Child - (ages4-12) 2450 . Children three and under , free. 

Bathing the elephants was a real treat. Rafting , a first for me, was fun . My only negative was that there could have been a seat back to lean against and give some support . I have problems with my back , so this would have been helpful .
Lunch was excellent, a lovely choice of food and flavours.
The Turtle Centre , was originally an idea from the Queen of Thailand , a rescue centre , where the turtles could grow and be released when ready .
 To sum up our day : Anne was a gem of a guide, full of fun and knowledge ( Thank you Anne ) 
Well worth the 3000 THB we paid . 
Well done 🦋
reviews.title: An excellent day out .

reviews.text: Wonderful guide gift and our driver aek who navigated horrendous traffic. Could have left earlier to avoid worst of crowd at Sunday market thought I would faint at one point managed to duck into a side street literally for air. Shopping centre was lovely would rather spend more time there
reviews.title: No room to breathe

reviews.text:
I did the Khao sok lake tour day trip with Keaw and it was truly amazing! She was our guide and she was very knowledgeable about the jungle, plants and it's uses, animals etc! It was amazing! Our driver was also funny and very accommodating. 

I have motion sickness, and they saved a front seat for me 🤩. 5 stars!! 

The only thing is that I think the tour was quite expensive for what we saw/did/ eat.
I mean it was a day tour and 4 hours driving round trip to and from Khao lak, the boat trip in the lake and the lunch at raft house, plus a very small jungle trek and very nice cave exploration. 
However, it was a long tail boat and the lunch was very basic... So in my opinion quite expensive tour, if you ask me

Otherwise it was awesome!!
reviews.title: Khao sok lake day trip from Khao lak - awesome tour but a bit pricey

reviews.text:
We did the 3-temple tour with Volker and were absolutely thrilled. Volker was mega entertaining and at the same time very informative – he was just incredibly happy to be listened to.

The tour was super planned, there were always chilled drinks and refreshing towels, and we felt in good hands at all times. It was an excursion that we not only enjoyed a lot, but also had the opportunity to learn a lot about the country, people and culture.

Thank you for this great experience!
Elisabeth & Mario 🌺
reviews.title: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Khao Lak Discovery – Highly recommended!

reviews.text:
Nice trip. Varied and engaging. 
The canoeing in the mangrove forest was nice. Driving to the falls was peppered with animal discovery. 
Our guide Anne had a good eye for finding animals. 
The lunch was very tasty and in a super nice location.
Finally visited a temple.
Unfortunately it was rainy season, which didn't really help us.
reviews.title: Varied and fascinating for a nice introduction to Thai nature and culture.

reviews.text:
We did a tour to the elephants, it was just breathtaking to see the animal, feed them and end up bathing with them. 
Many thanks to our guide Ivan who explained everything to us great, explained a lot of interesting things about the elephants and took beautiful pictures of us!
reviews.title: Stunning close to the elephants

reviews.text:
We had the best day with our guide Gift & our driver Mr Benz. 

Gift was very knowledgeable and you could tell she enjoyed her job and wanted us to have a great experience. 

She passed on her own life experience with Buddhism and the Thai culture. A tour is always better with a great guide who has a story to share. 

The temples were magnificent, and we got to see Monks too! Lots of stairs on the first one, probably wouldn’t recommend it if you’re not physically fit. 

Thank you for a wonderful day ❤️
reviews.title: Temple Trip

reviews.text:
We discovered the Sea Turtle Preservation Center which is really interesting and unmissable. 
The day ended with a visit to the Tsunami Memorial. Very poignant and impressive visit.
Thanks to the guides for their thoughtful comments and professionalism.
reviews.title: Experience and reflection.

reviews.text:
Beautiful discovery of 3 really different temples. The first temple built in caves is really impressive. Take your courage to climb the 245 steps and you will discover an incredible place. 
The other 2 are really beautiful and very interesting to discover through their stories. Must-see visit in my opinion.
reviews.title: Spiritual discovery

reviews.text: An excellently organized tour with a knowledgeable guide. It gave a little insight into everyday religious life in Thailand.
reviews.title: Worth seeing

reviews.text: Join this trip to experience all the 3 amazing Temples I've heard about and saw online, I done this trip and wasn't disappointed as the very good English speaking guy help me understand the Buddhism and what the history was, Was well taking care of as the driver made his way round the Temples, nice lunch on the way aswell
reviews.title: Amazing experience

reviews.text:
Beautiful Experience at James Bond Island with Khaolak Planner

We visited this island with Khaolak Planner. The tour started with a scenic boat ride through the mangroves, followed by the journey to James Bond Island. It’s a stunning place, even aside from its fame from the movie. You can take beautiful photos and enjoy the breathtaking views of the surrounding area.

It can get a bit crowded, but the atmosphere remains pleasant. There are several small stalls run by locals from Panyee Village, adding a nice cultural touch. Park rangers are present to keep things organized, which is necessary given the number of boats docking.

Be sure to walk all the way around the island to fully appreciate its beauty. After the visit, we also went canoeing nearby – a unique and memorable experience. Finally, we visited Panyee Village, the floating village, which is an absolute must-see when you're in the area.
reviews.title: Beautiful Experience at James Bond Island with Khaolak Planner
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: The tour well organised and planned. Our Guide, Jasmina was very knowledgeable, friendly and good. We had Australian and German guests on tour, she spoke English, Thai and German well. My favourite part of the day was the canoe around the islands, mangroves and caves. Panyee island 👎🏻, I would like to see the visit to Panyee more immersive in the culture or way of life of the village. Perhaps you watch the football team train or play a match, you visit the school to see how children learn, or you visit the mosque. A walk from one end to the other to sit and eat and then go again is a waste, one as well not stop there. The stop at the cave temple. It felt very touristy, almost as if every tour operator or taxi go there to tick a box. There are much nicer temples to visit. Overall good but not excellent!
reviews.title: James Bond Island Phang Nga Bay Tour.
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: You will feel that you have seen heaven. The most beautiful hike of my life to date. We started from the good kaho all the name of the agency with which the good one leans I don t know remember
reviews.title: Heavenly. Pure Wonder

reviews.text:
Everything worked perfectly and we had a sensational day.
Picked up at the hotel in 7:15. We were a small group together with another family. After an hour of driving in a very comfortable MiniVan we switched to the longtail. It was a very nice drive directly to James Bond Island. Depending on the water level, the canoe tour can also take place beforehand. Since low season and good planning of the tour guide it was nowhere full and very pleasant. Afterwards we had a nice canoe trip through the mango forests and hollowed out limestone cliffs. For lunch we then stopped for the floating village. Finally, we looked at a famous temple in the mountain and arrived at the hotel after 9h really happy. Jasmine we make a great travel companion. Thanks again.
reviews.title: A very nice Tsg
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: Great place to shop and eat. Fantastic Thai vendor food. Lots of clothes and gifts, herbal and good quality brand-name items. There is also music at various points around where you can sit and have a cool beer or drink. Family friendly venue.
reviews.title: Large food and shopping market

reviews.text:
We were greeted by our guide mook and driver bird, whom we're very welcoming and friendly. 
They took us on a fabulous tour of the khoa lak area.
First it was the tsunami memorial, a very moving experience. With the guide giving a comprehensive amount of information about the event.
Next we visited the temple. Again our guide was extremely informative about this and answered many questions that came from our group without hesitation.
Then on to the elephant sanctuary. Our guide informed us of the itinerary for this location. This was an amazing experience. We were fed at this place and the food was beautiful and diverse. Eating in beautiful surroundings.
Next to the turtle sanctuary again our guide showing us around and guiding us to distinctive areas.
To finish it was some bamboo rafting through the national park. Again a very memorable experience.
This by far has been one of the best , informative and value for money excursions I've been on, all helped by mook and bird.
Thank you for a fantastic day.
reviews.title: Safari

reviews.text: We got pick up from our hotel (in a nice air-conditioning mini bus ) meet bye our guide (gift) drive to our first temple which was about one away good journey. The first temple was good but a lot of steps 240 then 40 then another 50 but was ok lots of nice statues and lots of information given by the guide was there about 50 minutes .the we were driven to the next temple about 30 minutes which was the best lots of colour stunning to look at there about 40 minutes. Then we were driven for lunch about 20 minutes away lunch was great very tasty after lunch we drove for 40 minutes to the last temple which was another nice temple lots of great statue and a wax museum which was interesting then we met one of the monks and he blessed us we stayed there about 40 minutes the we got driven back to our hotels about one hour. Great day out. The driver was(mr teerapong montha ) he was great kept us hydrated all day and gave us cold cloths to refresh our selves.
reviews.title: Good day out

reviews.text:
Picked up from hotel in air conditioned mini bus our guide Poh outlined the day and we got started 
First was a temple high in the rocks, 300 steps, at the start they are steps but the higher up you go the smaller they get, three quarters of the way to the cave temple there is a flat area where the temple start , you have to remove your foot ware, the steps after this are no bigger than a computer keyboard attached to what looks like rebar used in building work. You climb to the next level no more than 30 steps at max the cave temple is small but full of items and the viewing area gets some great pictures well worth the climb

The other two temples were a lot more relaxed but there are steps in each one and if damp they can be slippy, it says to cover your knees and I had on knee length shorts and was ok, ladies need to cover their shoulders and knees 

They have you back at your hotel around five , they provided water or coke to drink, with refreshing wet wipes as well, a very informative day great guide and a great trip
reviews.title: Good day trip

reviews.text:
Did this tour on the 6th May 2025.  Fantastic tour, worth every penny.   Mike was a funny and knowledgeable guide.  The driver was also great.  The food and location was stunning. 
Highly recommend.
reviews.title: Great day out

reviews.text: The tour was well organised and did miss some of the crowds. The snorkelling spots were ok. Got to see some clown fish and other fish.
reviews.title: Well organised tour.

reviews.text:
Extremely Disappointed – Unreliable and Unprofessional Service

I had a terrible experience with Khao Lak Land Discovery on their Phuket DIY trip and would strongly advise others to look elsewhere. I was promised a professional driver who would take me to a gun range in Patong, but instead, I was dropped off in the middle of town with no explanation and unable to speak the language and told I’d be picked up five hours later. When I asked the driver to take me to my actual destination, he simply refused and said he was spending the day at the beach.

I contacted the company, hoping for a resolution, and was told someone would get back to me. They never did. The lack of communication, disregard for customer expectations, and completely unprofessional behaviour were shocking.

This was a complete waste of time and money. I would NEVER use this company again.
reviews.title: Extremely Disappointed – Unreliable and Unprofessional Service

reviews.text:
Also booked with Khao Lak Land Discovery a very nice and long day trip "Amazing 3 Temple Tour Khao Lak" with German speaking tour guide. Pick up at the hotel in Khao Lak at around 8am in a mini-bus.

We first went to the temple Wat Manee Sri Mahathat, which is located on a main road, then we had the extensive lunch in the group in a beautiful Thai restaurant on a lake with typical Thai dishes and rice, as well as fruit for dessert. The drinks are exclusive.

In the early afternoon we drove to the royal Wat Bang Riang temple with extensive park and other interesting Buddha statues, as well as free-living monkeys. Since this temple is located on a mountain and in the hilly surroundings, you had to make some effort. But it was still feasible.

The last temple was the Naga cave temple Wat Tham Phaya Nakarad, where you have to climb the open and closed caves a bit via simpler stairs and metal ladders. This is a bit more strenuous because partly without shoes. Once at the top there is a very great view of the surrounding area.

There was extensive information about each temple. Between the slightly longer bus rides there was additional information from the tour guide. At each stop there were soft drinks or water from the driver.
Driver and tour guide were very nice and friendly.

We stayed at the hotel again in Khao Lak in the late afternoon.

We thought it was a very interesting trip to get to know the (south) Thai religion and culture.
reviews.title: Very nice day trip to 3 different temples in Phang Nga region

reviews.text:
We booked this somewhat expensive but very nice and professionally conducted day trip to the Surin Islands via Khao Lak Land Discovery. The actual boat trip was carried out by another renowned company.

After a very early pick-up at the hotel in Khao Lak about 5am you were on time for sunrise at the pier in Khao Lak, had to check in there, there was a quick breakfast and the first information about the day trip in our local language in German.

After about an hour it started. They went barefoot on the speedboat catamaran. The crew and tour guides introduced themselves.

There was mind. 2 rounds of snorkeling on great reefs, as well as 2 shore excursions, once at the See-Nomaden village and a beach and bathing stop on the neighboring island with additional late lunch buffet. On board were constantly soft drinks and water, as well as fresh fruit or cookies.
Highlights on this island were the wild animals such as monkeys, birds or warblers.
In the See-Nomaden-Village and during the bathing stop, things and souvenirs could also be bought if necessary.

The snorkeling equipment was provided. The crew and guides were nice and friendly and happy to help. So this trip was a great successful experience.

After the return journey, we stopped briefly at the pier where we could change our clothes and use the toilet facilities again if necessary before being taken back to the hotels.

Except for getting up early, it was a great day.
reviews.title: Very nice boat trip with snorkeling and visits to the Surin Islands

reviews.text:
A fabulous and interesting day  to get a brief insight of culture and religion.  The first temple can be a little challenging with +200 steep steps to take - but well worth all the efforts. 
The tour guide is extremely knowledgable / experienced and provides plenty of answers one might have. Lunch break was delicious and diverse.
reviews.title: Any time good insights and different gems

reviews.text:
You must have experienced. Were very impressed. Our guide Oli explained everything great. We were in good hands with Him. Thanks for that. 

Roland and Renate
reviews.title: Khao Lak elephant bathing. Were tie impressed.

reviews.text:
This was a fantastic trip with Khao Lak Land Discovery. A small group (8 people) and we were well looked after by Gift and our driver Run (?).  Each temple was very different and Gift brought everything to life by sharing her own experiences and explaining how religion and Buddhist philosophy work together in Thailand.

It was a long (and hot!) day but well worth it.
reviews.title: A great way to learn about Thai religion, culture and philosophy

reviews.text:
The excursions are well organized and the tour guides are all very good.
We have booked 6 excursions in 3 years through Khao Lak Land Discovery and are already planning for next year
reviews.title: Again and again

reviews.text: This was a great tour for my first day in Thailand.  Gift was a a very friendly, knowledgeable guide and I learned so much. It is a long day so be prepared, but very good value for the cost. I enjoyed seeing the beautiful countryside on the drives between temples. My only complaint was the food at lunch. They took us a to a restaurant and we were given pre-selected "European spicy" food, which apparently means bland and zero spice.  Also it wasn't quite enough food for the group size.
reviews.title: Really great tour, lunch could be better

reviews.text: It was a very well organised tour right from the start and enough time to enjoy the snorkelling and a joy to see the beautiful coral fish life
reviews.title: Fantastic well worth seeing

reviews.text:
We both had a fantastic experience, well worth the money all staff were great. Saw lots of wildlife & some beautiful scenery, plenty of drinks supplied throughout the day coke/water lunch is also included. 

We also liked that it wasn't very busy at the locations like many other tourist places.
reviews.title: Amazing day out!

reviews.text: a long day, but worth it the snorkelling was very good, food good and plenty of cold drinks, friendly staff, the only minor down side and that is all it is:- tipping is a personal matter yet instead of giving people the relaxed choice they came around the catamaran in front of everyone with a tip box and went up to everyone individually, which I thought was wrong as some looked uncomfortable about it.
reviews.title: if you love to snorkel this is for you

reviews.text:
We booked 2 days Khao Sok Wildlife from Switzerland. Already then we were advised by Lars very competently and patiently.

The trip was awesome. Transport went smoothly (and on time). Our two-day travel guidine Ariz, a Thai woman with super knowledge of German accompanied us attentively, competently, reliably, kindly and with humor throughout the tour. She has great knowledge of flora and fauna. With the longtail boat we went “to the end of the world”. The boatmaster grew up there and knows the lake and the area like his pocket. He was also top.

Our accommodation was really very modest (which we wanted). No running water, no electricity, no iPhone reception, no bath. But pure nature and tranquility or x-different nature and animal sounds. Everything was clean and the crew in the lodge spoiled us culinary. We slept in an overwater bamboo cabin (only mattress, blanket and pillow).

The tours that Ariz did with us were impressive and fully decelerating. We felt safe and secure and like we were alone in this world.

In summary: Ingenious, unforgettable, impressive, unique, beautiful and highly recommended!!!!

Many thanks to ALL!!!!

Silvia & Reto
reviews.title: Once beautiful! ̈

reviews.text:
Transfer from Phuket to Khao Lak and back.
Warmest recommendation from here.
Punctual and skilled drivers.
This was our second time choosing Khao Lak Land Discovery.
Would definitely use them again 🙏🏻👍
Friendly greetings 
Lisbeth and Ole from Denmark
reviews.title: Transportation

reviews.text:
We did the Khao Lak safari - full day tour. Highly recommend. Had the most fantastic day with our guide Por and drivers. Worth every penny. One of our best days ever. 
Only thing I'd make a point of is that there was no toilet paper at any of the loos so ladies take some tissues with you!
reviews.title: Fantastic day!

reviews.text: Best trip we've ever been on Awsome. Going through the mangrove area on a longtail boat was brilliant. The canoeing then onto Bond island was unforgettable very quiet on the island with was good took so many photos. Then onto Panyee island the floating Muslim village food there was excellent which is in the price of the day trip. Onto the monkey temple which was good then back to the hotel. Can't recommend this company enough from start to finish thank you.
reviews.title: Awsome
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: Visiting 3 beautiful and atypical temples, the guide extra, smiling , he picked us up from our hotel respecting the schedules, gave us a lot of explanations and knew how to tell us the stories of each temple. The temples are all beautiful.
reviews.title: Excursion to 3 beautiful temples

reviews.text: The trip was a dream and the highlight of my Thailand trip. The excursion was very well organized.  The pickup was super punctual. First I went to the Rock Tree House Resort where I came across 1 couple and 1 family who became my travelling companions for 2 days. On the way to Cheow Lan Lake we continued to a temple cave with bats and flying dogs. Also a non-venomous snake and large silk spider crossed our path. Once at the lake, we took a longtail boat for almost 2 hours through incredible nature with imposing granite cliffs and rainforest. The raft cabins were simple but much better than I expected and in an incomparably idyllic location. There was a double bed mattress with mosquito net and a bench as a shelf. Electricity was available from 6pm to about 9pm. After a swim break we went on the first boat safari and after the delicious meal again. The night was accompanied by jungle sounds. In the morning at 6am we set off on a heretical safari, which our fabulous guide Rolo has made a unique experience. We've seen so many animals: Spectacled lemurs and gibbons, a Malaysian bear, warblers, sambar deer, eagles, kingfishers, rhinoceros and other birds. The soundscape alone was magnificent.  Unfortunately, we only heard elephants, but that's the case with wildlife.  Thanks Rolo, if I am in Thailand again, I will definitely repeat the tour.
reviews.title: Incredibly beautiful

reviews.text:
Everything perfectly organized, our guide of the pigeon Dutchman was great. 

Very nice and unforgettable trip. 

Looking forward to the next tour already booked
reviews.title: Khao Sok day trip

reviews.text:
Thank you for the excellent service
The drivers were at time and very correct 
We will use définnively your services next time we will be in this area.
William
reviews.title: Transfert aéroport de Phuket vers Khao Lak et Khao Lak vers aéroport de Phuket

reviews.text: We have been ordering our airport transfer for years and also some of our tours at Khao Lak Landdiscovery and it always works out great. The Highligt this year was the 2 day trip to the reservoir, these are experiences for life, just 1a. A big thank you to our tour guide Rolo, he knows his stuff and gives the guests great experiences. We are already looking forward to the next tour.
reviews.title: Unforgettable experiences

reviews.text: Just great! We had booked Surin Island. super organized from hotel pickup to drop off. We would recommend Surin to anyone. I just upload the pictures that say more than 1000 words🤗🤗
reviews.title: Surin Island

reviews.text:
We've just returned back to our hotel following a fantastic day out with Khao Lak Land Discovery and their 3 Temple Tour. Out guide, Jib was extremely enthusiastic, knowledgeable, and eager to assist us and teach us about the sites.

I really can't say just how much we enjoyed this trip. It was just an all-around joy to have done. The lunch was sublime, freshly cooked food, and that view from the restaurant is amazing!there were plenty of cold drinks and cold wipes available during the course of the day.

There are many steps to walk in order to gain access to the Dragon Cave Temple, however; please don't let this put you off. The group we were with consisted of English and French holidaymakers with an average age, I'd guess, of around 65 years old. We all managed it easily. 

I wouldn't hesitate to recommend this trip to anyone. It was great. The camaraderie between the other tourists made it a joy, and we'd like to thank KLLD for making this a memorable final trip before we head back home to the UK.

Thank you, Jib, and also our driver. You are a credit to the company. Blessings to you all.
reviews.title: Amazing 3 Temple Tour and Lunch

reviews.text: We just finished two tours with Oli this week.   The first was a Kaolak full day tour and the second was the early bird Hong Island tour.  Both tours were superbly well organised - all the people involved were very caring and looked after us at all times. Our highlight was the early bird tour to Hong island.  Setting off at 4:30am meant that we could meet the exact tide times allowing us to canoe into some amazing hidden lagoons.  We also visited islands where we were the first visitors and as such had a very private viewing of the islands.  Was a fantastic experience being out there on a  traditional long boat watching the sunrise.   Lunches were great and the team always had bottles of water on hand for us whenever we needed refreshments.   Thanks very much to Oli and the whole team involved in making our experience so memorable.
reviews.title: Thanks to Oli and all the team

reviews.text:
We booked this trip through our hotel as it was highly recommended and we were told a maximum of 30 people plus crew. We arrived at the port and spent about 1 hour waiting (breakfast was offered) while they sorted hundreds of people to allocate them into all of their boats. Eventually, we were loaded, and all given one size fits all very bulky full life jackets. And then 33 of us were instructed to sit on the bench seats, which would have been great if we had all fitted, The weather unfortunately wasn't great so it made for a very uncomfortable 1.5 hour boat trip. Obviously, the weather is out of the operators control, but if they had reduced the number of people on the baot, it would have made a world of difference to the experience. We arrived at Surin Island to the first of 2 snorkling swims, not the 3 advertised.
At this point, for me, it all went wrong. No life jacket, no snorkling. I have been lucky enough to have dived and snorkeld some of the best spots around the world, and snorkling with a life jacket is not snorkling it's floating. For inexperienced swimmers, I appreciate this could still be enjoyable . We were floating in an exposed site, so with life jackets on a lot of people were getting blown away from the boat, which has no form of tender on board in case they had to perform a rescue. Visibility was good, but unfortunately, a very average float around not a lot of coral or fish varieties. Island visit with lunch was good, second island stop for tourists shopping was interesting then our second float around different spot but unfortunately more of the same. Interestingly, now that the life jackets were wet, we didn't have to wear them on the ride home, which actually created a lot more space.
reviews.title: It's not a real snorkling trip and an overloaded boat.

reviews.text:
We did the 3-day trip as a family with our 5-year-old son. We found out in advance what to expect on this tour. Unfortunately, the description on the homepage is not clear and the information from the office does not reflect everything. It was only when we were picked up by our guide Aris (I hope the name is spelled correctly) that it really became clear what we were in for. I thought this was absolutely the right thing to do so that you could decide whether you trust your child with this tour. Our son is very experienced in traveling and Aris took excellent care of him. We are very happy to have done this tour with her, which was the highlight of our stay in terms of experience. 

Nina, Tim & Marlo
reviews.title: Khao Sok 3 Day Discovery

reviews.text:
Sadly not enjoyable.
The key reason a way too crowded boat. We were really crammed in rough boat trip over. Awful sore back  Had to sit in a gap between two cushions.
reviews.title: Way too crowded on the boat

reviews.text: Fantastic day with canoing and bathing elephants in amazing nature. Excellent friendly and experienced guide Somchai
reviews.title: Khao Sok Day Trip

reviews.text:
The drive across the lake with the landscape is already fantastic! Shortly before the accommodation we were surprised with heavy rain, rainforest stop! We were served a simple but delicious lunch and then we had some free time. Swimming in the lake or kayaking, just relax in nature! The accommodation was simple but did it. The jetty to the huts cost me a lot to overcome at first but after a few times it was ok. It's not for foot weak people! Our guide Rolo was great, he lives for this national park! Unfortunately, we didn't want to see elephants, but it's not his fault. We could still see some animals! The return trip the next day would be a bit damp from above but did not bother us! 
For nature lovers it is the right tour!
reviews.title: Unforgettable excursion

reviews.text: We did the James Bond Island tour with canoeing, floating market, reclining Buddha. Very friendly, knowledgeable and caring guide - Ann.  A good range of activities in the day. Plenty of food and drink provided.
reviews.title: A great day out
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: In 2023, during our first vacation in Thailand, we booked some excursions through Khao Lak Land Discovery, all of which were just wonderful. We were particularly impressed by the tour “Khao Sok Wildlife 2 days” with our guide Rolo. This experience was so impressive that we decided to travel to Khao Lak again in November 2024 to repeat the tour. This year we booked the tour “Khao Sok National Park 3 Days Wildlife and Treehouse Tour” and thanks to our guide Rolo we had unforgettable experiences again.
reviews.title: Mega experience

reviews.text:
Our two-day trip to Khao Sok National Park was an absolute dream! Thanks to our guide Rolo, who was not only incredibly nice but also very experienced, we learned a lot about nature and felt comfortable at all times.

The highlight, in addition to spending the night in the idyllic bamboo huts on the lake, was the encounter with an elephant – a magical moment! We were also able to observe many more animals and enjoy the spectacular scenery.

A perfectly organized tour that we can highly recommend to any nature lover. Thank you, Rolo!
reviews.title: Unforgettable excursion

reviews.text: A truly unique experience. The excursion was super organized and everything worked out fine - from the pickup, to the rides, the overnight stay, the excursions and the return trip. The national park is incredibly beautiful and you can see a lot - in our case: Samba deer, Javanese monkeys, gaur, warblers, eagles, rhino birds, spectacled langurs, an elephant and even a tapir. Rolo Our guide always wanted to get the best possible for us and was full of enthusiasm. We would really recommend this trip to anyone!
reviews.title: A unique experience

reviews.text:
It was a really eventful trip with Rolo. We saw a lot and felt like we had arrived in the deepest Thailand for 3 days!
Rolo took very good care of everyone and made an extremely effort to ensure that we see animals on the safari. Afterwards he provided us with his photos free of charge. Overall, it was a mega experience that we will never forget. Thank you!
reviews.title: Mega experience

reviews.text: The overnight stay in the raft huts was unique, pure nature. Our guide went to great lengths to discover the animals.
reviews.title: Wildlife pur

reviews.text:
We’ve already done several tours at Khao Lak Land Discovery in 2023. We liked it so much that we flew back to Khao Lak especially in 2024 for the Khao Sok tour of several days. So we went through this tour more or less twice.
Everything went smoothly. Our guide was super friendly and explained a lot! Very happy to come back. We look forward to our next vacation in Khao Lak in the future.
reviews.title: Simply recommended!

reviews.text: An unforgettable trip with Rolo. We've seen a hell of a lot of animals. You can tell that Rolo doesn't see his tour as work....he is with heart ❤️ & soul....always happy. We will come back, but then we will not only book the 2-day tour!
reviews.title: We will be back - We promised

reviews.text:
We did the tour in April as a family with our 2 sons (8&9). 
We stayed one night in the tree house. It was a great experience. 
At noon we took another tour on the raft. 
The next day we went for a swim with elephants and then went to bamboo huts in khao Sok National Park. On the way there Rolo took a lot of time on our boat tour to observe wild animals. 
Very worthwhile tour!
reviews.title: Great excursion

reviews.text:
Our tour guide Volker was simply outstanding! From start to finish, the tour was perfectly organized. He inspired us with his extensive knowledge of the culture, history and impressive temple complexes. 
Especially impressive was his warm and friendly nature, which made us feel immediately at ease.

The stops at the temples and the viewpoints were well planned, and we had enough time to explore everything in peace and take great photos. 

Even small details, such as cold drinks and refreshing towels, have made the trip even more enjoyable. 

Volker always responded to our questions and explained everything understandably and with a lot of enthusiasm.

Lunch at a typical Thai restaurant with unique views was also a special experience.

This day trip was one of the highlights of our trip to Khao Lak, and we owe this mainly to our fantastic guide Volker. 

We can highly recommend the tour with Volker!

Thank you for that!

Kristina and Ante
reviews.title: An unforgettable day trip!

reviews.text:
We are just thrilled! 
We booked 3 tours at Khao Lak Discovery and it was just great. 
It started when I booked. The people in the company took the time to sit down with us and find the right tours for us. Everyone was mega friendly. 
For each tour we had perfectly coordinated guides who you noticed that they do the tours with heart. 
Value for money was really good. 
The organization was top! 
We can really recommend Khao Lak Safari, Similan Island and the two day tour still Khao Sok. 
Here you can collect incredibly beautiful memories for your holiday.
reviews.title: Just thrilled

reviews.text:
We took the 2 days 1 night trip in Khao Sok with Discovery Land Khao Lak. The company took care of everything which was very good. I will start with saying how helpful was the booking process with David from Siam Advanture. 
We stayed the night in Rock and Treehouse resort that was very comfortable and beautiful. Our the tour guide was amazing and exceptional. She has taken care of each and every individual in the group. For example (one out of many), some people came with flip flops to the jungle walk and she gave one of them her personal shoes. Nuna have a lot of knowledge and she made effort to verify that everyone understands her. It was a rainy day in Khao Sok with very limited view but Nuna was trying to do whatever it is possible to ease the situation and the inconvenient. I really appreciate Nuna’s tremendous  effort and care.
reviews.title: Nuna our the tour guide was amazing!

reviews.text: We had a great guide, we experienced exciting, entertaining insights into Buddhism and the organization was perfect.
reviews.title: Here you can get to know the culture very closely

reviews.text:
The organization was great. 
The guide ( Volker ) and the driver are very nice. There was always something to drink and wet wipes. 
Volker explained everything great, you can tell he's there with his heart.
reviews.title: Great trip!

reviews.text:
We booked the tour at the hotel and were picked up punctually at 7:20 am. After about 1 hour we arrived at the port and got on a long tail boat. From there we went through the mangrove swamps to another boat dock where a paddle boat was waiting for us. With the paddle guide we went through various caves and along the rocks, which was an absolute dream on its own, despite the start of the high season hardly any people on the road. The fact that there were so few people on the road was because we did the paddle tour first and then went to James Bond Rock. All other tours do it the other way around.
From there, to the James Bond Rock, there was much more going on here, nothing, the more despite that we had enough time to take a close look at the rock and the surrounding area and take beautiful photos. For us an absolute dream and highly recommended. Afterwards we went to the floating city for lunch, which had a great variety and actually tasted great. Afterwards we had enough time to shop in the floating city. Afterwards we went on to a temple in a stalactite cave, also worth seeing. Afterwards we went back to the hotels.
For us absolutely top.
reviews.title: An absolute dream trip for us
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
We were at Wat Chalong Temple as the Big Buddha was closed.
Subsequently, we were on the market for a long time and were able to move freely.
reviews.title: Weekly market

reviews.text: A beautiful day, a variety of different temples. Our guide, Jenny, couldn’t have been better. She was so passionate, knowledgeable and funny. She went above and beyond, showing us not only the temples, but teaching us about Buddhism, answering all of our questions, and taking us to sample some Thai street food along the way! 10/10

Our tour guide Kaew was so friendly and great at her job. 

We done the full day experience 
Feed and bathed elephants 🧼 🐘 
Turtle conservation,
Bamboo rafting,
Stopped for lunch by the riverside, 
Visited a waterfall,
Had a tour around the Palace then finished off with the tsunami museum.
reviews.title: All day Khao lak safari was amazing 🐘

reviews.text:
It was very unpleasant to anyone who didn't come wrong, who didn't stand up in his own time, as if we were in the German army. The guide talked to me rude because I didn't realize I had to get to the lobby at a certain time, said the whole group was suffering from me before the whole group, yes in what words. And when I wanted to take another bottle of water he said that I already had three bottles in my bag. 
  After I complained, I didn't get an apology phone call, despite the company's manager promising to talk to me. Brothers, I don't recommend.
reviews.title: An impatient guide that insults Traveler

reviews.text:
Khoa Sok National Park Overnight treehouse trip.

Our guide Mike, was excellent, made us feel welcome from the moment we were picked up. Although English is not his 1st language, he was excellent and very informative. 
The Elephant sanctuary was excellent, not rushed and plenty of time with the animals. The treehouse hotel was also excellent. We thoroughly enjoyed it, we did unfortunately have a very "awkward" guest who refused to listen to any of the information given by Mike and complained about everything from where she sat in the van, being really tired because she had to take sickness pills to go on a boat, and no vegetarian food, although she clearly said she had no food issues. 
We were all told at the evening meal we should be ready to leave at 8.30 in the morning, she asked what was the latest she could have breakfast as 8.30 was very early. She wasn't ready and then said she hadn't been told. She then didn't want to go on the hike. Made it uncomfortable for all.
Mike dealt with it very well and continued to be professional.
reviews.title: Khoa Sok National Park Overnight treehouse trip.

reviews.text: We went on the elephant bathing tour with a guide called Izzy who was amazing! She shared interesting information on the way, and it was evident she had lots of love for the elephants. The Phang nga elephant park was such a great experience, being able to feed and bath with these wonderful animals was second to none. The carers had great connections with their elephant friends and you could tell the animals were cared for. Would recommend this to everyone, including children! This is a smaller private tour and worth the money. They offer guides that speak different languages also.
reviews.title: Amazing elephant experience

reviews.text: We went on a tour to feed and bathe with the elephants. Our guide Jenny spoke excellent English, was very informative and friendly. The whole tour was run really well, we were very impressed. The elephant sanctuary looked after its elephants and was a wonderful experience. Highly recommend!
reviews.title: Half day tour to the elephant sanctuary

reviews.text: We were on our way to a full day trip in Phang Nga Bay. The very early rise was worth it! The speedboat took us from Phuket Harbour to various destinations, including James Bond Rock. The staff of the boat were very friendly and extremely helpful in getting off and boarding, but also in providing drinks and food. An unforgettable day!
reviews.title: Full day excursion, recommended
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
Far too long drive over 2 hours. to the temple to Phuket , which is actually superfluous. No drinks (water) in the car , which is included in other excursions. Arrived at the weekend market after almost 4 hours, and about 4 hours walking around there, sometimes too much. 
Wouldn't really recommend such a ride.
reviews.title: Weekend market Phuket

reviews.text:
I did the trip via tui. Program does not match the description 
Long ride in the jeep of more than an hour than the rainforest in delicious because the rain came with buckets from heaven
The road to the waterfall is hard especially if it had rained
Then eating,
That was okay and afternoon canoe trip
But skipped because we were already soaking wet from the rain.
Temple we did not see and were quickly taken to hotel
All in all not recommended with bad weather would have liked the opportunity 
From more info about the weather and then make a choice
Money not worth
Description of the day is incorrect
Drinking bar poorly cared for
reviews.title: Money not worth

reviews.text:
What amazing trip , our Guide was fantastic and couldn’t do enough to make sure we were happy . Our accommodations was a bamboo hut on the lake which wasn’t what we expected but which added to the experience . The driver of the longboat was amazing knowing all the nooks and crannies of the lake navigated perfectly even in the dark with only head torch . 
We saw lots of monkeys , otters deer , a few iguanas, one of which ran up the beach as we approached which was just like watching a David Attenborough documentary. We thought the big animals had avoided us but on way back on day 2 we spotted an elephant. Wow! What a treat and he wasn’t bothered by us watching him take a mud bath so we sat and watched quietly taking lots of pictures. I cannot express how magical this trip was and would definitely recommend to anyone who wants to see the real Thailand and its habitants.
U will definitely need long trousers or long socks and shorts and a really good mosquito and leach spray  for your trek in the jungle
reviews.title: Wow

reviews.text: We did the Khao Lak Adventure. White water rafting, waterfall, quad biking and zip line. It was incredible. A real experience in the depths of the jungle. Nares was an incredible tour guide. So much fun, we were very well looked after. Thank you!
reviews.title: An unforgettable day

reviews.text:
We did the visit to Elephant Welfare Centre through our hotel with this company. It was 2900 bht to go and I love elephants so was very much hoping it would be a good place for them.

I've written a more extensive review but TL;DR it was okay. The centre is (currently) Phang Nga Elephant Park which I wouldn't exactly call a sanctuary but they are taking decent care of the elephants. You get to feed them some fruit snacks and take a bath with them with their Mahout accompanying them the whole time (the handler who is the only person they really trust and listen to).

That place is currently under construction and we were only there for 90 mins (which was quite short for the price tbh) so it's a bit hard to make a good judgement but overall they seem happy enough, well fed and no chains or hitting or riding. Mahout usually gives verbal instructions, uses the rope or maybe tugs the ear (not as keen on this but I can't suggest what else they'd do to be fair and it's much better than the alternatives people use). Seems they have been trained somewhat to follow instructions and listen to their Mahour. Not sure about the small enclosures but we were told it's  for sleep and could also be to protect them from wandering into the construction and roads. Big park with lots of natural scenery so hopefully they get to walk around that

Was sceptical from reading reviews, from what I gather it was formerly not good for the elephants with rising etc. HOWEVER they have taken the feedback from customers about ethical treatment and have responded correctly by stopping these practices and caring for the elephants they had better with plenty of food, baths and pets. Still work to be done but they seem to be continuing to work to improve which is good.
reviews.title: Elephant welfare centre visit (morning)

reviews.text:
Did three separate tours with this company.
 Prices were competitive, pick up prompt, decent guides with knowledge and information.
 Everything seemed organised
reviews.title: Decent tours

reviews.text:
Elephant safari with lunch
this was so much better than it already sounds, packed day full of great experiences, friendly, funny knowledgeable guide ( mike) and great driver, plenty of water and soft drink provided throughout, great meal, rafting was fun with beautiful scenery, temples where great too, mike answers any questions you have about anything you see, feeding and bathing elephants was amazing, turtles where good too, and learning about the tsunami was a great insight of a horrible past which thankfully this fantastic place has recovered from. if I could give the tour 10 stars I would .
reviews.title: Great great fun amazing and interesting day

reviews.text: We went on the Khao Lak Safari tour and had an amazing time, making new friends and enjoying some wonderful experiences. Jennie our guide was a star, who made the whole day a pleasure. We really enjoyed bathing with the elephants and the bamboo rafting.
reviews.title: A great way to experience the wonderful local culture.

reviews.text:
We booked this All Day Khao Lak Expedition tour through the tour desk at our resort and was not disappointed.  We were picked up on time by our tour guide Red and driver Bird.  We were picked up in a comfortbale clean van and taken out to the Wat Lak Kaen Buddhist Temple , Then we headed to the Sea Turtle Conservation and rehabilitation centre, Lam Kaen. 

From there we headed to Poseidon Beach for a walk or swim then refreshments at By Leo Beach Cafe. From here we then went to Bamboo Rafting & ATV Somnuek Travel khaolak Phang Nga for a river raft ride along the Lam Ru Yai River  where we passed under several snakes sunning themselves in the trees.  

It wasn't until we were almost finished that our River guide told us "No green one Python No venomous Black & Yellow very venomous bite hospital very quick or no good!!"  Gee thanks could you not take us directly under them🤣🤣. 

After the river ride it was lunchtime at a local restaurant for a variety of Thai dishes, chicken, beef and prawn.   

After lunch we headed to the Tsunami Police Boat Memorial site.  This is where a police boat that was anchored a nautical mile offshore was picked up by the Tsunami wave and dumped 2 km inland where when the water started to subside and show the absolute destruction it had caused the boat was left on a pile of rubble and mud.  Too big and too damaged to extract and get back to water it has been turned into a memorial and a reminder of that fateful day.  Very moving and eye opening experience.  

After this we got into a 4WD and headed into the mountain jungles.  The 4WD was not the one in the tour pictures when booking and was not exactly comfortable on the lumpy bumpy tracks.  we then commenced a short walk 25mins up a steep path to a magnificent waterfall which you could swim at the bottom of and take some fantastic photos.  the same walk back to 4WD then transferred back into the comfortable van for a nice trip back to the resort for another swim 5pm 32° and 95% humidity!!  All in all a great day.  Thanks Red &  Bird.

***Make sure you take wet shoes and bathers.
reviews.title: From Buddhist temple to Sea Turtles, from Bamboo River Raft To Tsunami Memorial and beautiful Waterfalls. We saw it all!

reviews.text:
We enjoyed a relaxed day visiting a variety of temples and learning about Thai culture and (recent) history, and a little about Buddhism and practicing religion in Thailand.

The roads up to the mountain temples are windy, but our driver was very careful. The van was air conditioned and comfortable, and we were supplied with water, soft drinks and wet wipes.

Lunch at the river restaurant was amazing. A great location and good food. 

Our guide, Gift, was friendly and very knowledgeable, and also took fantastic family photos for our tour group.

We are a family with two teens (11 and 14) but there were older people on our tour (60s?) and the terrain and stairs were suitable for them as well.
reviews.title: Beautiful temples and lunch

reviews.text: We had our 3. Excursion with Arisara as a guide, she was wonderfully friendly and warm again. She speaks excellent German and English. It started with a longtail boat trip to the famous James Bond Rock where Arisara explained a lot of detailed information about the island and the backgrounds of Thailand. Afterwards we went paddling in the mangroves which was a fantastic experience. Afterwards we were on a floating village which was slightly jarring for our eyes as there next to newly opened coffee shops the old inhabitants live in the poorest conditions (Kulturschok). The food in the restaurant was ok. Finally we went back with the longtail boat and still looked at the monkey temple also here Arisara was detailed in explaining and always considered that nothing happens to anyone. She makes every trip special, if you can ask for her, you will not regret it.
reviews.title: With Arisara as Bond Girl in Phangnga Bay
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
Great tour. Picked up on time at the resort. A long drive but in Phuket it is very busy with all the traffic. After a short stop at the Chalong temples on to The Big Buddha. An hour here. Then to Naka weekend market. Here, more than 3.5 hours, time to browse all the food and stuff stalls, eat and shop. Driven back to the resort in an hour and a half. 
It was definitely worth it.
reviews.title: Nice tour to the Naka weekend market.

reviews.text: Great trip with guide Volker, who explained everything very well and provided us with lots of interesting background information. Everything was perfect, and the food in the chosen restaurant with a great view and very good food was also highly recommended. We were also always provided with plenty of drinks.
reviews.title: Very nice cultural excursion with the top guide Volker!

reviews.text: Wow! What an amazing experience to see these magestical creatures up close. GENTLE GIANTS! Beautiful place. Well looked after.
reviews.title: Elephant discovery Khao lak land

reviews.text:
Unfortunately we were unable to go on our vacation in June 2024 due to FTI insolvency.
We had to cancel the trip... hence my review.
Super quick email response in advance.
The trip even took my gluten intolerance into account.
We had made a deposit via Paypal which was immediately refunded after I canceled it.
If we book Thailand again I will do the trip.
reviews.title: Nice contact

reviews.text: We were picked up on time, in fact 10 minutes early which was good. The guide Aris was very informative throughout the whole trip, and very friendly. Our driver Mr Biy was a good driver and was as careful as he could be. The off road part is a bit bumpy, but I have 2 titanium rods on my spine and survived, so couldn't have been that bad. ( they do ask anyone if they have back issues before the start).The waterfall part is quite demanding with a steep walk down an uneven track, and the return trip will test the unfit. But the waterfall is cool and refreshing to have a dip in. The stop for food was good also, you even get an ice cream. Drinks were freely available all day coke or water which was needed frequently.  After a stop at the temple, we were taken canoeing, and each canoe had a guide. They took us through the mangroves, and we saw a troop of monkeys which was great, us watching them watching us. This trip is well worth the money. We have been on quite a lot and this is up near the top for value for money.
reviews.title: Great day out

reviews.text: If you want primarily to go to the James Bond island, do not book this trip. We spent 30 minutes of the one hour stated, this trip is mainly about the floating market. Being dragged through from the back of the market along through unsanitary conditions along the whole length. Where lunch is concerned, so much of the food was left by the other 9 travellers. Then we hung around for an hour waiting to be taken back doing nothing. The canoeing was fun but that was for 40 minutes only out of the 9 hour day. The driver on the way back, drove far too fast finding every bump and pot hole and the email that we should have received the previous evening arrived an hour and a half after the revised pickup, a true shame really, we had been looking forward to it. There was only 2 small bottles of water on offer and a glass of sprite for the whole days agenda. We were very dehydrated.
reviews.title: Poor customer service
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: The trekking tour in Khao Sok National Park with our guide Isi was super exciting and very informative. We saw lots of animals and plants. A highlight was the canoe trip.
reviews.title: Trekking tour in Khao Sok National Park with Isi

reviews.text:
Great trip with lots of information about nature, the country and the people.
The team, consisting of Michael (guide) and Tawat (driver), work well together and make the tour a great experience!
reviews.title: If off-road doesn't scare you and you like the jungle... do it!!

reviews.text:
We booked the Khao Sok National Park Trip. 
We had such an amazing day. Highly recommend 

Our tour guide Aris was the best! Definitely made the trip, lots of information, super friendly and made sure we all had the most perfect day. 

Lovely aircon mini bus for the 2:30 hour journey, stops for toilet when needed 

Highly recommend 

Thank you Aris
reviews.title: Great trip!

reviews.text: This was easily the best day of our holiday. The tour guide was awesome, he was passionate about his country, knowledgeable and had a great sense of humour which absolutely made the day. We stopped by the morning market, did the off road safari, walked down to the beautiful waterfall, visited a buddhist temple, had a lovely lunch and did a relaxing canoe trip. Unlike the boat trip we booked through a different company where there were loads of us packed into a boat like sardines. This felt like a truly authentic relaxed trip with only 6 of us in the group. Lunch was freshly prepared for us with quite a few choices and everything was really tasty. Highly recommend this trip. If we return to Khao Lak we'll be doing it again.
reviews.title: Amazing day out

reviews.text: Hello, we booked a tour through the hotel with Khao Lak Land Discovery and were more than satisfied. We were with the guide Dominik, who comes from Germany and currently lives in Thailand. We were a mixed group of Germans, Austrians, English, French and Australians. Dominik made an effort in all languages to ensure that everyone understood and he had a lot of background knowledge. The tour was very well organized and there were always enough cold drinks and a delicious lunch. Would recommend it again and again. Thank you and see you soon
reviews.title: Great tour with Khao Lak Land Discovery
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: Booked DIY driver who arrived stoned off his head!!!  Smoking out front of hotel to collect us and wobbling around. Had to request another driver which took another 90 mins to arrive seemingly from another tour company Tropical Touristik this driver was great even though there was a significant language barrier.
reviews.title: DIY disappointment - Driver stoned!!!!!!!

reviews.text: JayJay and her colleagues were so helpful. Smoothest and safest snorkeling trip we've done yet inThailand. Flippers masks and pipes provided for everyone if needed, hot drink and snack very welcome after first snorkeling stop. Beautiful beach stop for lunch. Waters were so clear and sealife abundant! Also the best coral we've seen so far. Grey reef sharks seen. Cannot recommend enough.
reviews.title: Superb company to snorkel with!

reviews.text: We went on the Khao Lak Expedition, the snorkelling trip to the Similan Islands Early bird and the Little Amazon & Takua Pa old town half day trip through Khoa Lak Land Discovery. Lars was so helpful in helping us to choose which excursions would suit our family and help us to make the most of our stay. The information we received before the trip was first rate. All 3 trips had excellent guides and we felt safe the whole time. They had so much information to share with us and kept us all informed and entertained. Thank you so much 😊
reviews.title: A wonderful company for adventuring with

reviews.text: Wow wow wow, fabulous day. Thanks to ollie and the team for an amazing day, an unforgettable day. So we'll organised and just magical
reviews.title: Fabulous fun, highly recommend

reviews.text: Great tour through the rainforest, lots of nature. Vehicles in order, with sun protection. Drinks were also available. Would gladly do it again
reviews.title: We had 2 great tours

reviews.text: All I can say is that I have done this before, was picked up on time and was in a small group and as promised, everything was in German. There was a lot of information and the day flew by and the lunch at the nomad village was really good, I will book here again next time, keep it up. Greetings Andreas
reviews.title: Finally got to know the island where Bond was filmed.
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
Worst excursion in years. First of all, the guide was weird. Touching everybody en forcing them to do silly things. 

We booked an excursion: Police Boat, Temple, Swimming in a waterfall, safari in the jungle, bamboo rafting and beach time.

Police boat was interesting, so was the temple. Then we went to the waterfall. It was a difficult hike and when we got there, no water at all to swim in. Just with your ankles in the water… 

We only got 1 roover for 10 people + driver + guide so everybody was pushed together. It was horrible. 

Then we visited the turtles, it was fine but a little bit sad because the different types of turtles were kept to show the tourist. They breed and set turtles free but they also keep turtles in very little spaces just to entertain the tourists. Some turtles will never set free. When we asked about it the guide said “no it’s fine, it doesn’t matter”… 

Then we went to the bamboo rafting area. Same as in the waterfall, barely water so the guide had to walk with a rope to get the raft forwards. Sometimes we had to get off to help the guy pushing the raft. 

Conclusion, no recommendation at all. Save your money!
reviews.title: Bad experience

reviews.text:
It was a wonderful experience to see the Mangrove from a canoe, just like the temple and the waterfall were worth the trip. But we spent more than half the time driving on large country roads - in the back of a four-wheel drive. The only off road that was in the trip was on a 45 minute trip up the mountain to the trip we had to go, down to the waterfall.
In return, we had the best guide, who vividly told us about all the plants and animals we passed.
reviews.title: Not much off-road.

reviews.text: Well worth the early start to great snorkels and lunch on the beach. Smooth boat ride after breakfast on the dockside. Plenty of drinks well worth the money and Noon was super friendly.
reviews.title: Great day out with our Guide Noon

reviews.text:
We were a bit wary of booking the tour as it was 8 hours long, which included quite a lot of time travelling between the temples. 
Having said that, our guide, who's nickname was Oil, ( a really lovely, knowledgeable guide), used the time travelling to explain the significances of each temple, the monks and the history of the religions , which made the time between temples seem a lot shorter.
The temples were very interesting, for us the Dragon Cave temple was the highlight, the climb up through each section was a journey in itself, up to the top cave where we met the bats, what a great experience. 
  
I would recommend this tour but be aware there is a lot of walking.
reviews.title: Thailand Temples

reviews.text: Jenny our guide was absolutely amazing. She told us so much about Thai culture, Buddhism and the temples themselves that you didn’t want the day to end. What was also a nice touch was that she told us a little about her family and daily life which made the information that she shared with us more personal and relevant. There is quite a bit of walking, and at times it’s quite steep with a lot of steps, but you get plenty of rest breaks and they make sure you are well hydrated. The food stop was also very good, with great views over the lake. I would definitely recommend and if you get Jenny you won’t be disappointed.
reviews.title: A great guide to Thai culture

reviews.text: The tour guide lady was really friendly and passionate about explaining the history of the temples. It was very informative and the templates where so visual beautiful and interesting to see
reviews.title: Educational and memorising.

reviews.text: Booked direct via the land discovery website and got a prompt confirmation from Lars. Pickup on the day bang on time and the day ran to clockwork thereafter. Would recommend the early bird, the lunch area had gotten a bit busier by the time we moved on to our second snorkel. Everything as advertised, great day, great team, great service 😎
reviews.title: Just a great day …

reviews.text: Do not arrive in Khao Lak until later this month but we have had great service via email from Lars helping me to choose the best tours . Really looking forward to some great experiences
reviews.title: Great email service from Lars

reviews.text: A tour guide with in-depth knowledge of Buddhism, with a lot of joy and dedication. A day I will never forget. Competent and good work.
reviews.title: I would do it again

reviews.text: We spent the day in a small group (11 people) with our guide Jake and what an amazing day. Jake was very professional, very smiling, he has enormous knowledge about Thailand and the customs, but also about the animals that we were able to encounter. He took us to untouristy places which allowed us to enjoy without being disturbed. Everything was perfect, the temple, the memorial, the waterfall, the bamboo rafting, the turtles, the lunch and especially the elephants where he took the time to take a photo of each group. We recommend 100%!
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari

reviews.text:
This is the most amazing experience I ever had. In the middle of National Park, in unspoiled wild nature with amazing guide - Rolo. We watched the wildlife for hours, patiently, and all of the sudden - wild Elephant appeared. We watched it twice - in the evening and early in the morning. Rolo knows the habits of animals, talks about their life in the Park. The trip is very well organized, the meeting with the wild elephants in the natural environment is truly an unforgettable once in a life time experience. I recommend this tour to all sensitive people who want to discover the nature give it a respect and who are patient and keen to see the animals and untouched sacred jungle form the distance.
The best is to watch the animals from the boat, to listed to this ancient forest and not even see the exact genre but just listen. The night watch is also amazing under the stars. You can catch a sens of raw nature. You sleep in a bamboo tiny house, on the water, unconnected with the internet, with only wild around.
reviews.title: Khao So expedition with Rolo

reviews.text: Great experience with beautiful landscapes, lots of animals and numerous hotspots. Lots of pure nature in one day.
reviews.title: Great experience

reviews.text: We were on a temple trip 12 years ago in Thailand and now we had our daughter with us and thought she should also see temples, so we booked this trip. The guide Gift was really nice and very informative about Thailand's religion and answered all the questions we had (the child had quite a few too). First temple, Naga Temple, was probably the most impressive, but they were all really nice. There was also a nice lunch included. At the last temple we got a bracelet which was blessed by a monk.
reviews.title: Memorable trip

reviews.text: perfect organized trip and great guides! always the first at the spots and good tips from the guides! i can highly recomend xtra holiday 👍
reviews.title: perfect choice

reviews.text:
Very poorly organized trip!!!
Pick up at the hotel at 5.20am! (Slogan "The early bird...")
Arrival at the "harbor" at 5.50 a.m. "Harbor" is one big makeshift! Not enough seats for breakfast, everything is very basic.
Because the tides were not taken into account, all the boats are aground and cannot start. Wait until the boats are finally towed free at 8.15 a.m. - so almost 2 hours of waiting!
Access to the boat through mud with sharp-edged stones - no jetties!!!
On the way to the islands, we stopped twice because of engine problems - according to the guide, we had known about it the day before, but we still set out with the same boat.
Due to all the delays, we were much too late at completely overcrowded beaches and snorkeling spots.
Due to the delays, a snorkeling trip had to be canceled entirely!
After the lunch break, we continued on with the supposedly repaired boat. Total breakdown after half an hour. Daring and dangerous transfer to two other boats on the open sea. Luckily, no one was hurt and we were late but at least we were healthy. arrived again.
Exiting the boat was the same as getting on - mud and stones!
The boat crew and guides "tried very hard", but were also visibly frustrated.
