I don't want to experience a trip like that again!
The islands are great, and if the organization is good, it's definitely a worthwhile trip. But not recommended with this travel organization at the moment!
reviews.title: Similan Island - not a nice trip!

reviews.text: Great Tour, bamboo rafting, elephant conservation, turtle conservation and more... shout out to the best tour guide Mr. Folker aka Johnnie Walker aka Somchay absolutely brilliant 👏 👌
reviews.title: Unforgettable family adventure

reviews.text: We wanted to travel on the catamaran as reading the other reviews the speedboats were noisy and uncomfortable, as we were near the back it was still noisy, it was also a 0500 pick-up time, despite this it was a fantastic day, staff, food and snorkeling was amazing, I tried using the supplied fins but they gave me leg cramps so swam without them after, I have some excellent Gopro footage and hopefully my underwater camera will have pictures to be proud of, a must do trip.
reviews.title: a must do trip

reviews.text: Enjoyable and educational, staff were amazing and informative, the first temple had some seriously dodgy stairs to climb/descend but otherwise would recommend.
reviews.title: Good, but stairs are dodgy!

reviews.text: I went on the Amazing 3 Temples Tour yesterday. A really lovely day with tons of great photo opportunities, lots of information about Buddhism and the culture of Thailand and a delicious lunch with a view. Our small group of 4 guests was provided with cold towels and drinks all day and the driver drove carefully. Thanks to the funny tour guide Sonchay who made this day very special with his friendly, open manner.
reviews.title: Amazing 3 Temples Tour

reviews.text: Unique experience! Thanks to Rolo - he does this tour with passion! We can 100% recommend this tour!
reviews.title: Unique

reviews.text:
We did the tour in mid-December.
The pick-up was great (7am), the driver was on time and there was a German tour guide (Rolo).
As we hadn't had breakfast yet, we were kindly offered the opportunity to have breakfast in the "tree house".
The trip to the huts on the longtail boat was very interesting, and we saw numerous animals on the way there. The accommodations are simple bamboo huts with mattresses and mosquito nets. Not luxury but certainly sufficient for one night.
The actual tour:
After lunch, we went on an exploration tour with our tour guide Rolo and our longtail boat driver Liam. We saw various species of monkeys, deer, various species of birds and elephant tracks. A short hike through the jungle following the elephants' tracks was also included. After that, we went back to the boat to keep looking for elephants and other animals.
After dusk, we drove back to the bamboo huts, where we had dinner.
The next morning we set off early (6am) to look for elephants. We saw a number of tracks on the shore again, but no elephants. Instead, we saw many other animal species, including a gaur, which is only seen very rarely.
After the exploration tour, we returned to the huts at around 10 a.m. and were greeted with breakfast. After that, we had some time to swim in the lake or explore the jungle on our own by canoe. After lunch, we went back with the longtail boat and the van, which dropped us off at our hotel.

In short, we can only recommend this trip to anyone who really wants to observe animals without being disturbed by the crowds of tourists.

A big thank you also goes to Rolo, who is always up for a laugh - a really great guy. He also took pictures of the animals with a camera throughout the tour, which he then sent to us.
5 out of 5 stars!
reviews.title: Two-day trip into the wilderness

reviews.text: Many thanks to our guide. He made these two days an unforgettable experience.
reviews.title: Unique experience

reviews.text:
Great trip and I learned so much about Thai culture & religions. Everything worked smoothly. 

Our guide Jenny was a total pro and I felt very safe while our driver Rwn (??) was driving the minivan. 

I recommend this trip if you are interested to see different kinds of temples & learn more.
reviews.title: Great trip, totally recommend!

reviews.text: Gift was an amazing guide to the amazing temples, her knowledge and enthusiasm was clear to see. Tat our driver made us all feel safe and the lunch was excellent. Onto the Temples the Naga cave was extraordinary and well worth the many steps one can only imagine the first monk climbing to find this cave. Walk the extra 55 steps for the view you will regret it if you don’t. The Pagoda temple again is Amazing and the views and serenity cannot be described. Lunch consisted of a range of Thai dishes all delightful with a view of the river which adds to the experience, there is no way you would find this restaurant on your own. The Third temple was not as wow as the first 2 but I was blessed by a monk and saw some amazing Waxworks. Thank you Gift it truly was an “Amazing 3 Temple tour
reviews.title: Gift made the day

reviews.text: The trip to Phang Nga Bay was run by Khao Lak Land Discovery. The tour was very well organized, we were always at the attractions before the other tour groups arrived. The trip on the Longtai boat, the canoe trip in the mangroves and caves, the James Bond Rock, the sea nomad village of Ko Panyi and the cave temple with the monkey feeding were all very worth seeing. The funny tour guide Tim did a pretty good job, but we would have liked a bit more information here and there. But all in all it was a very nice trip that we can definitely recommend.
reviews.title: Worth seeing trip to Phang Nga Bay
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: Would have been nice to have known that we were going to sit in a van for 4 hours before we got anywhere can’t fault the driver he was excellent
reviews.title: Poor time management

reviews.text: Excellent tour from start to finish. Lots of fun and a great guide. Full day to experience the environment around Khao Lak
reviews.title: Great experience

reviews.text:
We were lucky to time our visit that co-incided with the annual Vegetarian Festival.

We booked an overnight stay in Phuket Old Town and witnessed the Night Market which was amazing but very tiring with the humidity, but the following day, we were fortunate enough to see the Vegetarian Parade.
reviews.title: Phuket Old Town Worth a Weekend Visit to see the Night Market

reviews.text:
We booked the full day tour, mainly because it included elephant feeding and bathing.
We were not disappointed. The visit to the Elephant Camp was the highlight, but the other tour inclusions were also well worth visiting. 
These included the Turtle Conservation Sanctuary,  a traditional Thai Temple, a very tasty Thai lunch, bamboo rafting, the Tsunami Memorial and a waterfall visit.
Our guide for the day, Gift, and driver Nong were both great, and ensured that we had the best experience possible. 
I would highly recommend this company for any tours in the Khao Lak area.
reviews.title: Amazing Great Value Tour

reviews.text: Today we went to the Elephant Park with Somchay and Bird to bathe with the elephants. It was a wonderful experience that we wouldn't want to miss. Our guide was in a great mood, very friendly, humorous and had a lot of information for us and answers to all of our questions - we absolutely recommend the tour. Thank you for the experience :)
reviews.title: Unforgettable experience with the elephants

reviews.text:
We made the Off-road Safari-Tour and visited the Rainforest with the Jeep, saw a beautiful Temple and have been paddled with a Kayak though the Mangroves …. And: It was SUPERB 👌🏻

Everything was well organised, and Michael (the Guide) and the Driver Tawat (hopefully correctly written) made a SUPER JOB!
Michael knows a lot about the Rainforest and the local Plantages (Kautschuk + Palm Oil) so that you get new insights about Thailand. Tawat is a routined driver so that you never - even in the Rainforest - feel insecure. 

Both of them are really friendly and caring, and Michael is even more a fun guy to travel with. So: MANY THANKS 🙏🏻 to Michael and Tawat! 🤗 … we will book again for sure
reviews.title: Wonderful Experience in the Rainforest and Mangroves

reviews.text:
Our trip with Khao Lak Land Discovery to the off-road safari in Khao Sak National Park, led by Michael and accompanied by driver Jun, was simply fantastic! Although the weather was not on our side and the boat tour literally fell into the water, this only made our day even more exciting.

Michael was an excellent guide who was not only very knowledgeable about the flora and fauna of the national park, but also always provided a good dose of humor. His knowledge and enthusiasm for nature was infectious, and he made us feel comfortable from the start.

Driver Jun also did a great job guiding us safely and skillfully through the off-road paths of the national park. The canoe tour through the “Little Amazon” was a real highlight despite the rain. The rippling water and lush vegetation created an enchanting backdrop.

The unexpected rain made the day an unforgettable adventure, and we laughed heartily as we paddled through the tropical rainforest. Khao Lak Land Discovery managed to turn what seemed like a mishap into a unique experience.

Overall, this trip was fantastic and highly recommended. We would book it again without hesitation and can recommend Khao Lak Land Discovery, Michael and Jun to anyone who loves the beauty of nature and exciting adventures. Thank you for this unforgettable day!
reviews.title: An adventure in the rain – Unforgettable day with Khao Lak Land Discovery (Michael and Jun)

reviews.text: We had the 2nd excursion with Land Discovery today and both were incredibly breathtaking already. Super great organized, great guides (thanks again to Ray), with delicious snacks and lunch. The guides were very attentive and always considerate of our young son as well. We would book Land Discovery again in a heartbeat.
reviews.title: Great Discovery

reviews.text: Traveled a lot in Southeast Asia as my wife is Thai. Won't  forget this day. A stop at the police boat, then a beautiful waterfall. After that a 30 min. ride to one of the highlights on this tour. A welfare camp for elephants. We were allowed to feed and bath with these wonderful animals. What an experience. The elefants are obviously doing well and they had fun with us in the water.  Afterwards a sea turtle conservation center, followed by a delicious lunch.  Second highlight was bamboo rafting in the former neighborhood of our fantastic tour guide, who always looked after us and was a wonderfully open and approachable person. Somchai told us a lot of interesting things about the country, the people, Thai cuisine, animism, Hinduism, Buddhism and belief in spirits, showed us various plantsand animals, fruits and seeds and how you can use different plants to heal yourself. This man is a treasure trove of knowledge and he is not afraid to pass on his knowledge. My wife and I are still very impressed. A visit to a local temple finished that beautiful day. We will come back to Khao Lak and book excursions with Khao Lak Land Discovery again. Can only recommend everyone to do same.  Don’t be afraid to ask for Somchai -   with this guy your trip will for sure become the absolute highlight of your holiday.
reviews.title: Best day ever with fantastic tour guide Somchai on a trip called Khao Lak Safari

reviews.text: It was a great experience!  I've been on many ATV tours all over Thailand and some were hit or miss.  This place still has challenges and since our group was small and seemed like the riders has some experience, we rode through various courses which was fun! Definitely a great activity to take a break from the beach and hotel!
reviews.title: Great riding experience

reviews.text: One of the best tours I've ever done. Jenny and Aeck are fantastic tour guides and so nice. I did the Khao Lak Adventure tour (white water rafting, ATVs, zip lining, and a waterfall trip) and the ATVs and white water rafting were amazing. It rained hard when I went but honestly, that made it far more fun especially riding ATVs when the water went up to your calf!
reviews.title: One of the best tours

reviews.text: Had an interesting day visiting the 3 temples, Sonchay was very knowledgable and was a great tour guide. Was quite a long day but we had a lovely lunch and had plenty of water throughout the day. Definitely worth the visit to get more of an insight of the temples and Buddhism
reviews.title: Really interesting and worthy day spent

reviews.text: What a great day guided by Somchoi seeing elephants, turtles, a Buddhist temple, bamboo rafting down a river, seeing the tsunami museum and a lovely waterfall. The highlight was feeding and bathing (scrubbing) the beautiful elephants. But the day was made particularly special as our tour guide was one of the best guides I’ve ever been on a tour with. He was super interesting and knowledgeable about Thai culture and customs and generous in sharing this information. I learnt so much in one day! He was also very helpful and caring. I highly recommend this tour.
reviews.title: The best Khao Lak Safari

reviews.text: We had a fantastic day out at Khao Sok Lake. The scenery was spectacular and even the torrential rain during our jungle trek didn't dampen our spirits. Oil our tour guide was very friendly, knowledgeable and made sure everyone had a fun day.
reviews.title: Khao Sok Lake

reviews.text: Our first time to Thailand done a few trips but the one we done to James Bond island was amazing our guide (Jenny ) was amazing would totally recommend gave us so much information and always made sure we were okay , the group we were in was amazing we all got on so good would deffo get Jenny again if we come bk to visit xxx
reviews.title: James Bond island
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
We have checked many tour companies at the end we have chosen Khao Lak land discovery and we never regret it . 
We are very satisfied with itinerary it is very informative and exciting . 
We would to thank our driver Bee and tour guide “ Johnny Walker “ they are very knowledgeable , friendly and always smiling . 
Definitely will recommend to it friends and family. 
Best regards 
Yanina Radionova
reviews.title: Wonderful Khao Lak safari tour

reviews.text: Fantastic from start to finish, really relaxing and informative trip. The rain forest was spectacular which was described to us by the rep in a passionate way. Well worth the trip!
reviews.title: Amazing experience

reviews.text: Beautiful tour past three different temples. Very interesting and our guide "Johnny Walker" was very enthusiastic and provided a lot of information. Really worth it.
reviews.title: Nice trip with flour information.

reviews.text:
We arrived early and were pleasantly surprised that is was clean and well organised. We found the stall holders friendly and helpful 
The goods on offer were varied and not all same same and well priced 
The food court had a great selection
reviews.title: Clean mostly undercover weekend market.

reviews.text: The safari tour was amazing! Tour guide Ming was the best tour guide, very knowledgable and took lots of photos for us at each stop of the tour! Would highly recommend!
reviews.title: Safari tour was unforgettable

reviews.text: Tour guide rolo spoke mostly german instead of english (while we booked an english tour), the tour does not include jungle trekking as they mention, it's just a 1.3 km slow walk on touristic constructed paths, guide can not give any information about the nature. We went on boat to see wildlife, but the guide needed to pee a lot so the boat trip had to be discontinued several times so he could pee. Guide smoked a lot, so instead of nature you smell cigarettes, disgusting. In summary, not worth the money at all. The biggest tourist was the guide himself. Not recommended!!!
reviews.title: Not worth the money

reviews.text: We took a day trip to the PhiPhi Islands. The driver arrived promptly at 4 a.m. During the 1.5 hours to the port in Phuket, the driver kept collapsing, microsleep = life-threatening! I couldn't relax for a moment. Then we went out on the boat in high waves, the tour should have been cancelled. The tour guide seemed drunk. The rest of the crew were very nice and we saw beautiful places. At Maya Bay, 45 minutes is enough for a photo, because you're not allowed to swim there anyway. I would have preferred to have stayed longer on Krabi, rather than on Bamboo Island where an ice cream costs €4. There are no showers on the island to freshen up for the long journey back.
reviews.title: Dangerous tour. But beautiful places

reviews.text:
If you are getting picked up from the Pullman Khao Lak Resort, then this trip is a waste of time. 3.5hours travel time to the Big Buddha as we were first to get picked up. Last to be dropped off; 3hr travel time back.  Overall, we have spent close to 7hrs travelling to only be out of the van for under 4hrs. 

40 mins allocated at Big Buddha
3hrs allocated at Naka Weekend Markets

No tour or commentary was provided during the journey. This is literally a taxi service.

We called the tour operator an hour prior asking about cancelling, and they obviously lied to us when they told us that there was 0.1mm rain in Phuket.

All in all, it's not worth it, very disappointing.
reviews.title: Think twice doing this from Khao Lak

reviews.text: We visited three temples where there were no other tourists, very quiet and serene. The guide gave a lot of explanation, both at the temples and on the way.In the afternoon we got a delicious lunch with a fantastic view.
reviews.title: Wonderful experience

reviews.text: My husband and I and our 2 teen boys enjoyed the day with Gift (tour guide) and Sayan (driver). Gift spoke very clear English and blessed us with her personal experiences with Buddhism as she explained the religion, beliefs, and practices. She was friendly, and it was easy to ask her questions. The three temples were each very different: the first in a rather remote cave; the second, a vibrant temple and pagoda atop a hill overlooking enormous statues; the third temple was next to a giant Buddha and museum of wax replicas of monks (the museum was the only place we could not take photos). One of our boys claims he was a bit bored with so much sitting in the van, the other welcomed a nap during the time between temples. It was a full day that we enjoyed. People with mobility challenges would not enjoy the cave -- there were hundreds of steps to get into it and explore it, and, when wet, some of the rock surfaces were slippery. There were handles at all the stairs. Note, you will need to take off shoes in all the temples, and socks come off in the wax museum. Wet tiles could be very slick, and there were mats and walkways set up for those. In the cave, some took socks off, I kept mine on (yes, they got a bit dirty). The cave temple seemed to be swept and clean, unlike most rocky caves.  The provided lunch was delicious and, at the start of the day, Gift asked about food restrictions and allergies and made sure that there was an assortment of dishes available for everyone.
reviews.title: Full day with a variety of settings

reviews.text: What an amazing day out. The Three Temples tour was one of the highlights of the Phuket holiday. The first two temples were visually stunning, while the third one required climbing into a cave - the view from there was amazing. The lunch stop was at an authentic local restaurant where the food was beautifully spiced. The guide and driver were fabulous, however special mention to the driver who had a constabt supply of icy cold water, soft drinks and wet wipes for us to cool down. The pickup and delivery back to the hotel went very smoothly. Totally recommend Khao Lak Land Discovery.
reviews.title: Three Temples Tour

reviews.text: Interesting information about the history of Buddha, pleasant and competent guide. Visited 3 temples, all of a different style accompanied by a good meal at noon.
reviews.title: Nice experience

reviews.text:
My husband and I were were absolutely blown away by this tour! 

Gift was such a wonderful guide with personal and in-depth knowledge of each temple location. We finished the tour knowing so much more about the culture of Thai people and the history of Buddhism.

Each temple was quite different, the first one located in a cave, the second one was quite mind blowingly beautiful and grand, and the final one was a local temple where we had the opportunity to learn about prayer ritual if we wanted to (no pressure to do this if you don't want to), and also be blessed by the Monk. The wax museum at the last temple was pretty interesting too.

Between the 2nd and 3rd temples we had a lunch stop at a local restaurant with a spectacular view over the river. The food was delicious - so much food and with great options for people with dietary restrictions.

I would highly recommend this tour to anyone that would like to learn about the culture, religion and also enjoy some pretty spectacular sights, with a knowledgeable and very sweet guide. 

I recommend taking shoes that are easy to get on and off as you will be removing them quite a bit. There is a little bit of steep climbing up steps to get to the first temple cave so if you are afraid of heights or unable to climb stairs, you might have to sit this temple out. But the other two temples are easy enough to access.
reviews.title: A breathtaking tour with an incredible guide!

reviews.text:
Highly recommend. Very professional driver. Picked up from hotel in Khao Lak right on time. Van was clean and comfortable. Supplied cold drinks and towelettes.
Excellent, thanks again.
reviews.title: Highly recommend

reviews.text:
My wife and I booked the Khao Lak Land Discovery Khao Sok Day Trip. This was a whole day tour and was fantastic. Our guide was Gift, who spoke perfect English and was absolutely delightful. Nothing was too hard and she was incredibly knowledgeable. We were picked up at our resort and first went to Takua Pa’s morning market. This was mainly a food market and Gift explained the different fruits and vegetables and then we had time to meander. Gift bought some sticky rice delicacies for us to have later. We then stopped at a waterfall which, unfortunately had no water. Not Gift’s fault! Then to an amazing viewpoint where you see across the valley to the mountains. Really spectacular.

We then had one of the highlights. We visited en elephant sanctuary and went down to the river where we washed and scrubbed the elephant and fed the elephant bananas. It was interesting to see how such a large animal was so gentle, We had cold drinks and fresh fruit there as well.

We stopped at the monkey temple. This was really interesting. The original temple was built in a massive cave and there were monkeys everywhere.

We had lunch at the Rock and Treehouse Resort. Lunch was Thai style with Tom Yum Soup (a little milder for us westerners), Pad Thai and dishes with chicken and vegetables. Clearly freshly made and absolutely delicious. My wife is vegetarian and they made special food for her, including tofu.

After lunch another highlight. We took a canoe trip down the Sok river. There were two people plus the guide/paddler in each canoe. The scenery was spectacular, with huge cliffs and wild  jungle. We saw 2 snakes on the way. Half way we stopped for afternoon tea. The guides cut coffee cups from bamboo, including stirring spoons. They built a fire and made the coffee. We enjoyed the sticky rice delights which were - interesting.

We finally returned to our resort at about 4.30pm. It was an amazing day and I strongly recommend this tour to anyone. The elephants and the canoe trip were amazing.
reviews.title: An amazing day out in Khao Sok - not to be missed

reviews.text:
Firstly, Gift was truly an amazing tour host. She was knowledgeable, friendly and very warm. 

From the moment we were picked up from our hotel, to the minute we were dropped off - we felt happy and content.

The temples were stunning, and took our breath away. Each of the 3 were so unique and made for beautiful viewing.

It felt like an honour to be guided through these sacred spaces by a guide who clearly loves her faith. Truly special. 

Highlights of the day - we had a wonderful chance to meet and be blessed by a monk, we climbed up to the viewing point of the first temple and the view was just amazing, and we got to enjoy the day with a group of like minded strangers who had also tagged along for the tour! 

Be mindful, there are a fair few stairs and the day was very warm - so dress appropriately but be prepared to sweat! 

Overall, was one of the best experiences we’ve ever had. 10/10 would recommend.
reviews.title: Truly unforgettable, gorgeous views & amazing experience.

reviews.text:
We have now made the Surin Island Snorkel Tour on 25.04.23 for the 2nd time after 5 years. We were negatively surprised by the implementation. Already booked from Germany. Online you can advertise with 3 snorkel stops and with your own speedboats. Unfortunately we had to find after the pick up at the hotel that the good was changed and we were not informed about it, a pity, the port for departure with the speedboat was moved from the north port to the south port thereby increasing the journey time from about 75 minutes to a whopping 105 minutes but a snorkel trip was cancelled. On arrival in the port were 2 speedboats planned unfortunately by a third party Kaholak Adventures because 4 people canceled one has quickly painted a boat and stuffed 34 people on a boat, sitting like the herrings next to each other ,absolutely outrageous. Furthermore, there was virtually no information to both excursions . 2. Snorkeling gear was conducted in dirt water statement is at this time always here Dirty should dive what not everyone can .
We didn’t think you could destroy an actual beautiful tour like this.
Sorry Land Discovery if you want to save but not at the expense of tourists your living from you. We are disappointed.
Can't recommend the tour provider anymore
reviews.title: 2. Surin snorkel tour unfortunately not worth the money -disappointed by Discovery

reviews.text:
Thanks to Aris, who not only provided a lot of interesting information in good German, but also incorporated her view of things. It made her look very authentic.
3 temples that contain different currents. The cave temple inhabited by monks (dragon cave temple) , Bang Riang sponsored by the royal family and finally a temple where Hinduism and Buddhism flow in from the north (China) and south (kaew manee Si mahathat). At the first temple you should be good on foot, 240 steps to the first cave. It is also in the description (countless levels), yet some were surprised.
Lunch at the mangroves.
Anyone interested in land, people, culture and religion is in good hands here.
reviews.title: Culture, culture, culture, learning a lot about Buddhism, its manifestations, as well as country and people.

reviews.text: It was a great tour, with a great tour guide, we did other tours, everything worked wonderfully.  Price performance absolute class. With this institution can book with peace of mind Lars makes everything possible if what does not fit in time. Greetings to Khao Lak it was fantastic
reviews.title: Dreamlike tour

reviews.text: Exhausting, since approach from Khao Lak does not manage under 2 hours. And despite the typical Thai driving style, some would say risky. The Big Buddha is worth a visit, even if heavily visited. You can also be blessed by a monk. The Naka Weekend Market is huge, m. M. about 5 times the size of Bang Niang Market. See and eat fish and crustaceans. Bake half the fresh lobster, delicious. But also in meat, vegetables and fruit everything your heart desires. Plus life music. Even the non food area offers more than Khaolak, everything the tourist needs, or not. One could discuss the stay. 40 minutes at Buddha is relatively short (based on the mass of people). We personally don't need 4 hours in the market for food and to stroll through the aisles once. But others may see things differently.
reviews.title: Good new impressions, huge selection of food, but exhausting.

reviews.text:
This tour was fantastic from start to finish. We were collected from our hotel promptly, and first visited the Police boat and Tsunami memorial. Although not the happiest of subjects to start the trip with, and some of the photos are pretty harrowing and moving, it's an important topic to learn about. 

The tour also included stops at a local temple, a turtle conservation centre, bamboo rafting, lunch and elephant feeding & washing at a local sanctuary. At each stop soft drinks were provided, traditional coffee was provided at the bamboo rafting and freshly-cooked lunch also provided, and all this was included in the price of the tour. We had plenty of time at each activity and were provided with lots of local information and insight throughout by our guide.

The guide was very friendly, patient and informative throughout. We always felt that we had plenty of time at each location and were never rushed, but he was also always on-hand whenever we wanted to ask questions or to provide information. Also, the lunch that he arranged at a local restaurant was very tasty indeed!

Overall, a great tour with so many brilliant experiences packed into one day. Well worth doing and, if we visit Khao Lak again, will definitely look to Khao Lak Land Discovery to book our next trip :)
reviews.title: Khao Lak Land Discovery - Khao Lak Safari

reviews.text:
Fantastic tour guide, very good with English and believe me it is not obvious. He gave us the beauty of their culture and religion. 
Magnificent places to take your breath away, I would do it again a thousand times.
reviews.title: TO BE REMADE 300 TIMES

reviews.text:
Wow wow wow! Khao Sok day trip - AMAZING!
What an amazing day from start to finish.
Our guide Ming was fabulous, very informative and couldn’t do enough for us. Really recommend this trip. We had 3 generations of our family so ages from 70 down to 6 and was perfect for everyone! The canoe down the river was out of this world, monkeys fab and the elephants just topped off a perfect day! Could not have asked for more so thank you very very much 🙏🏻
reviews.title: Khao Sok Day Trip - WOW WOW WOW!

reviews.text:
Fair cancellation option needed, because you never get back your money if you have to cancel.

We had to cancel because sickness and we cancel very early. But the company don't pay back the money. 5 times communication with the traval guit ISI an nice words from him. But since 16.02 no money back from the company. Bad customer support.
reviews.title: Cancellation impossible

reviews.text: The ride was interesting and entertaining. Two temples were also located in a very beautiful landscape setting. The cornerstones of Buddhism have been explained to us very vividly and entertainingly by our guide Ming. In addition, fresh drinks and a good lunch were always taken care of.
reviews.title: Culture in scenic surroundings

reviews.text: What a wonderful experience this day trip was. Our guide ‘Gift’ is a practising Buddhist and therefore was. so passionate and knowledgeable about the faith and facts at each temple. We were a family of 4 and joined by 2 other couples and so it felt like a very intimate and special day, even praying together and meeting a resident monk at the final temple. Gift and the wonderful driver ‘Bird’ looked after us so well throughout the day with lots of cold drink and snacks and we had a wonderful Thai lunch at a restaurant as well. Highly recommended… thank you Gift and Bird for a special day!
reviews.title: Very special day

reviews.text:
The guide was super reliable, kind and helpful at everything. A beautiful experience in nature.  It was nice to visit so much in one day.
I recommend it to everyone.
reviews.title: Nature - Beautiful view
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
A very well organized tour .
The destinations were visited very well in time as there was no overcrowding at the spots .
reviews.title: Pure organisation

reviews.text:
Our guide was very good .
He has brought the country closer to us with a lot of detailed information.
Lunch or meals also fit
reviews.title: Information in abundance

reviews.text: Our trip was very nice, our guide Ming (speaking super German) was very friendly, helpful and explained everything to us well. He was also able to answer all questions very competently. Lunch during the trip was also very good. This trip is highly recommended.
reviews.title: Great excursion
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: I had fairly low expectations but my wife and I agree that this was the highlight of our visit to Phuket. The excursion we booked was amazing and totally worth it. The pictures tell the story. Get a package deal for the boat, stop at the Buddhist Island for lunch, canoe trip and the whole deal. Well worth the time and the price.
reviews.title: Don’t miss this
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: Certainly the bottoms are beautiful and populated by fish, however in the description of the excursion it is indicated "a total of three snorkel stops of about 45 minutes each". Actually there are only two stops. Neither the weather nor the timing justified not applying the number of dives. It's a shame and borderline dishonest.
reviews.title: 3 stops snorkeling, two actually!

reviews.text:
We were picked up by Camille and driver (sorry I can’t remember his name) on time and after collection from one more hotel we were off. We did all the activities on the list and all were fun. The rafting was gentle and pleasant and the lunch before was very tasty. 

Thailand is a beautiful country and the people seem so happy and courteous. This trip is, by definition, very touristy but you get to see some of the hidden gems and I wouldn’t want anything cut from this. The Buddhist temple was eye catching. We were well looked after. Thank you.
reviews.title: Khao Lak expedition

reviews.text:
Minibus arrived punctually and we were escorted by Jake. The group were a mix of English and French and, though he was French, Jake spoke well and informatively in both languages. The trip down to the bay was reasonably comfy and then we got on to our longtail boat. Powered by a truck engine these are not quiet but we had an interesting trip round the mangroves before heading to James Bond island via a stop for a canoe trip. We got our touristy pictures and headed for lunch at the floating village. The lunch was superb. 

Back by boat to where the minibus was parked and then to a nature reserve to see the monkeys. They all seemed to be asleep but a little food enticed them out.  We then moved to the temple. We weren’t allowed in as previous tourists had ruined it for everyone by visiting in bikinis etc, so a bit of a let down but hey…

The day was hot and tiring but well worth it. Yes it is touristy but we got to see some of Thailand and some beautiful scenery.  Jake and the driver (sorry I forget his name) were very attentive and looked after us well. Thank you.  Back in time for tea and medals.
reviews.title: Excellent day
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
Our guide, Som Chay, a German with a Thai name, impressed us with his knowledge, especially about Buddhism. But he has also been able to give a lot of information about Thailand and life in Thailand in general. This made the temple tours super interesting and very informative. Although I have been to different places in Thailand several times, I have learned a lot of new things because every question was addressed. 
The driver provided us with soft drinks regularly. Lunch at a beautiful vantage point with a view of Phang-nga Bay also rounded off the trip, although unfortunately it also rained a bit.
This excursion can be recommended without restriction
reviews.title: A very informative, completely successful excursion

reviews.text: The tour was easy to book and everything was included, including return hotel transfers, a light breakfast, lunch, drinks and snacks. The tour guides were very helpful and knowledgeable.
reviews.title: Surin Islands daytrip by speedboat

reviews.text: Driver was solid, Eris the guide was wonderful, Buddhist temple grounds were interesting, trip into the mountains was very cool. Got to swim by a waterfall and that was refreshing. We could hear gibbons in the trees; would have made my day to see them but at least we got to see a large troop of monkeys on the boat ride later. Highly recommend this look at mother nature away from the beaches. Sorry, can't upload a pic because I left my phone at the car rental place in Phuket! D'OH!
reviews.title: Khao Lak away from the beach

reviews.text: Took this tour Jan 2023 with Gift as our guide, lovely small group & comfortable minivan. Learned more from Gift about the temples & Buddhism in general than we had picked up on several visits to Thailand previously. Really enjoyed the contrast between the 3 temples, what a stunning view from the second. Great lunch, plenty to eat & soft drinks included. Totally recommend.
reviews.title: Three temple tour

reviews.text:
Volker was in a very good mood and gave a lot of information about country/religion/temple - who wants to know more about the country + excellent photo motifs is right here.
The tour is estimated to take about 8 h.
reviews.title: very informative, good mood guaranteed

reviews.text:
The write up is a bit misleading. It is not a bad tour despite my 2 star rating, if it was what you were expecting. For our family it was just too much driving and too little activity.
It went something like this:
8.45 pick up
13.00 lunch
And between these times there was one 40 break of walking to a waterfall. Some of that driving was in a nature reserve but it's a very long drive for a waterfall that, although nice, was not impressive. 
The afternoon had 40 min canoe in a mangrove Forrest. This was great, different scenery and very peaceful. And 25 min at a temple.
Drop off was after 4.30 so again a lot of driving.

The driving was all in the back of an open air safari truck in the wind and baking sun. Definitely  apply your sun block before starting the tour
reviews.title: Too much driving and too little activity

reviews.text: This was our excellent service from Khao lake land discovery everything was on time nice staffs I really like it we will come gain here .
reviews.title: Excellent service

reviews.text: We have booked a 2-day Safari Tour tree house. The activities were basically fine, but unfortunately our German-speaking guide with Dutch descent made a dent in our minds. He was unmotivated, disinterested and gave us only terse answers when we had questions. During the activities he usually never said anything about it, we had to discover for ourselves. Also, he was never with our group when we had lunch or dinner, he preferred to be alone and roll his cigarettes. The highlight was then at the reservoir. At the landmark with the 3 stones we wanted to take a person photo from the boat, which unfortunately could not because it was too cumbersome for the boat captain. Everyone else around could. We are very disappointed by the 2 days. You could have made more of it, in that sense you could have saved the guide. So we cannot recommend Khao Lak Land Discovery!
reviews.title: Unmotivated guide put a line in our face

reviews.text: We went on a guided adventure which included zip line, white water rafting and ATV. From the moment we were picked up from our hotel, Oil, our tour guide was warm, wonderful and enthusiastic. She gave us a full briefing of what to expect on each activity and lots of other information about the local area. Each activity was exhilarating and loads of fun. We were also provided a delicious cooked lunch. We would highly recommend the tour and our lasting family memory will be for the great, fun day out and also for Oil taking such great care of us, well above and beyond what we could have expected.
reviews.title: Fantastic day out (zip line rafting ATV) with fantastic tour guide

reviews.text: Really lovely trip. Oi was very caring and gave us a lot of information. We even had the chance to pray with a Buddha. It was a bit sad that the last temple was closed as this one opens only for monk celebrations so we went to the ordination hall. I would recommend this trip to everyone who is interested in Buddhism and Thai culture. Lunch was a lot and very good. I liked the place we stayed. The trip can easy be done by flip flops. The stairs even with rain shouldn’t be a problem. We were only 4 on this group so it also felt like a private tour. Very nice and happy I booked it.
reviews.title: Highly recommended

reviews.text: The tour and specially the guide was really interesting and we learnt and discovered a lot about Thai Buddhism
reviews.title: Explore and discover Thai Buddhism

reviews.text:
Our tour guide (Oil) and driver (Phong) were both fantastic. The tour was well planned, and very enjoyable throughout to learn more about the culture.
We really enjoyed the second temple and the views up to the Pagoda were stunning!
reviews.title: Great day out!

reviews.text:
Thanks to Camille, French, a great day to discover the history of the country and religions
We will not forget the diversity of temple colors down to the white of elephants and their tusks, the Buddah tooth or tooth sits not far.
reviews.title: Super 3 amazing temple

reviews.text: Beautiful day of discovery. The whole service is perfect: the care, the guide (francophone, thank you Camille!), the variety of activities.Really a great day
reviews.title: To do!

reviews.text: A great trip! The off-road track was a real challenge for the driver, which he mastered superbly. The guide answered all the questions. From the canoe we saw monkeys and snakes. But be sure to wear closed shoes and take mosquito spray with you!
reviews.title: Is worth the money

reviews.text:
Prepare to be out all day. 8-17.00. Take something to cover shoulders and knees (both men and women). Take mosquito spray and sun tan cream. Wear sensible shoes but be aware you have to keep taking them off and putting back on. Be prepared to sweat. 
This tour is brilliant- You are kept well informed, throughout all the temples. Our tour guide Gift was fantastic, she explained everything so well and related it to her life, so it was easily understood. The driver kept us well hydrated, as well as safe as he was driving. The food stop wasn’t good- which was a shame as it was a lovely setting. 
But this should not detract from this being an  informative and interesting day.  Highly recommended.
reviews.title: Fantastic and Informative.

reviews.text: Our guide Volker (som chay) impressed us with his knowledge of the Thai religion as well as its history, which he conveyed to us in a very understandable way. Haven't been through such good leadership. High praise to him and his driver who drove us so safely through an impressive landscape. I'm sure this wasn't the last outing with her organization.
reviews.title: An excursion we don't want to miss

reviews.text:
A really worthwhile experience. I’d booked this via my hotel to do something on my first full day (as the two other day outs I really wanted to do weren’t available) and I’m so glad I did this.

The first temple is within a mountain deep in the jungle. What an experience that was - pictures really don’t do it justice.

The second was a Royal temple - arms and legs need to be covered for both men and women so be prepared.

The third was the Wat Suwan Kuha. This temple is in a cave. It had rained in the afternoon so power was intermittent within the temple itself. Again, a fab experience. Be careful around the monkeys! You can also walk up to the top of the cave but make sure you’ve got proper footwear, especially if it has been raining. 

Our tour guide, Boy from Eco Lak, was brilliant. As a former monk himself, he was also able to bring a lot of his personal experience in, which was a perfect touch.

So, what started as a “that sounds ok to spend a day” turned out to be a brilliant day!
reviews.title: Both stunning and peaceful!

reviews.text: Excellent tour with a very friendly and informative guide. Our guide, Gift, keep the tour interesting with many facts and it was enjoyable despite the torrential rain
reviews.title: Amazing Temple Tour

reviews.text:
This was our second day trip with Khao Lak Land Discovery after the 3 temple tour.
Again, we were picked up from our hotel on time and everything else had worked out perfectly again.
We drove our minibus to the jetty for the longtail boat ride. A beautiful drive that took us first through mangrove forests, then to Phang Nga Bay, incl. "James Bond Rocks." Later we changed to paddle boats and went on a great trip through mangroves and caves. Afterwards we took the longtail boat trip to the sea gypsy village at noon (drinks incl.) to eat.
In the afternoon we took the minibus back to a monkey rock temple, which was also very interesting and beautiful.
All in all, the compiled tour was absolutely top! There is no doubt about that at all! Also we were always provided with drinks everywhere. 
Which is why I didn't give a 5 star rating, is a bit due to the tour guide.
Tim was very nice and his laughter is also really infectious, but we were all in the minibus together for over an hour and only when we reached our destination (the dock for the longtail boat) did he introduce himself to us and talk to us for the first time.
The driver was driving okay, too. However, we were thrilled by the other combination on the 3 temple tour, tour guide + extremely attentive driver (kept pushing drinks into our hands, always had a smile on our face and always gave us, after each sight, a refreshing towel, much more.
But as already described, the tour itself was great! and after the tour guide was "a bit thawed?" also absolutely recommended.
reviews.title: Very great tour with many highlights
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
We had booked the 3 temple tour and it had all worked out great!
We were picked up on time at our hotel in Khaolak. Our guide Sonjay /Volker was really great!
A German, who is married to a Thai woman, told us soooo much great and interesting in the temples and during the trips. Also we could ask anything, great!
On the whole tour we were superbly looked after. Our nice driver drove very prudently and offered us a cool drink of our choice after each sight, as well as a wet wipe for hands or to refresh.
The lunch with great views, was also very tasty and also here the drinks were incl. 
We booked 3 more day trips through Khaolak Land Discovery. We hope that these will be just as interesting and that everything will continue to go so smoothly....
reviews.title: 3 temples - tour where everything worked out great and you learn a lot

reviews.text: Many thanks to Sonjay (People) for his detailed and interesting travel guide to these three extremely interesting as well as different temples. Through Sonjay, the essence of Buddhism was brought closer to us and perfectly explained for the time frame. It was a pleasure to meet him and experience this tour! Unrestrictedly recommendable!
reviews.title: Impressive and very interesting!

reviews.text:
As part of Khao Lak Land Discovery tours we did the bamboo rafting which was an exceptional experience, and one we did not think would be so good.

Our guide was excellent in his navigation of the river and it was great for him to point out a number of animals within the environment.

Whilst I presumed that this was going to be a rather boring activity, I must say that it was anything but.

Highly recommended activity for all tourists who haven’t experienced bamboo rafting before.
reviews.title: Bamboo Rafting - A Must Do!!!

reviews.text:
I would like to thank Khao Lak Land Discovery and especially Rolo for the awesome experiences which we made within your trips in Khao Lak and Khao Sok. 
We booked 3 Trips within this Organisation (3 Temples, DIY Phuket and 3 Days Khao Sok) and every trip was well organized, especially the 3 days in Khao Sok. 
Within these 3 Days we got to know the wildlife quite through the eyes of Rolo who is the living motto of the Organisation „Water is live - Nature is home“.
reviews.title: „Water is live - Nature is home“

reviews.text: Super interesting tour. Guide (Volker) has described everything in detail and with a high entertainment factor.
reviews.title: Top overall package

reviews.text: Although I am sure this tour would be better on a sunny day, for us it rained non stop and we still loved it. The temples are all different and beautiful/interesting. Our guide, Gift, was very professional, friendly, knowledgeable, and spoke perfect English. Everything was as, if not better, than advertised. I’ve traveled many times in Thailand and seen many temples, I still found this very interesting and worth while. Highly recommended!
reviews.title: Great Tour, Even in Rain!

reviews.text: It was our third outing with KHO LAK LAND DISCOVERY - both done before went well so it is with confidence that we participated in koa lak wildlife 2 j - frankly the guide (German nickname role ) is bad , he is in his world taking pictures not even to offer them at the end of the excursion (except of course to the Germans) - the extension to the temple and none because he explains nothing on the site (perhaps he explained it to the group of Germans who were with us - majority 6 people) - fortunately there was the guide in French training to give a little lightness to the experience and explain to us certain aspects of Thailand - pay BTH 15 000 for this experiment where we are ill accompanied with explanations in the drip it is a scandal - The landscapes are beautiful certainly, it is a real digital detoxification but finally we see very few animals except the guide who has telephoto lens - we do hours of traditional boat frankly uncomfortable (2 hours to get to the site) then directly after arrival 3 h for exploration where we see only 4 types of animals (bird , deer and buffalo, monkey ) -> you will not have more - any other nature reserve in any place in the world will be better in the sight of the deplorable organization made by this guide and moreover during the boat hours you are told to close because your voices may frighten animals - NO but frankly animals are used to the presence of man in view of the number of excursions organized on this site and motor boats that pass through the site and which make a lot of noise - the guide is so lame that he does not know how to apprehend wild animals - he does » he does « he does » Khao LAK LAND DISCOVERY I beg you to go and visit the site to ask for an improvement the infrastructure where tourists from the world will spend 1 night: cleaning and securing access to the cabins - I do not thank ROLO for its organization - otherwise we thank the boat operator, the locals on site who made us food and the French apprentice guide
reviews.title: Guide 0/10 and dirty site

reviews.text: Great tour company providing quality tours (cultural, landscapes etc) special thanks to our guides: Oil and Tim who made this experience memorable
reviews.title: Great tour company

reviews.text:
Very good tour overall with a nice selection of temples, a very good guide who obviously enjoys his work (Volker) and a good (amount) of food.
The time at the temples themselves could be just a little bit shorter to make the trip a little less strenuous and it was a bit of a shame that we didn't get to see the two big statues at the second location close up which we assumed we could've based on the pictures.
Nevertheless we can recommend this to anyone interested in Buddhism, Thai culture and beautiful viewpoints.
Again big thanks to Holger and our really great driver Noangor (we're so sorry we probably misspelled that!) who took us safely and smoothly from location to location.

Please wear sandals or shoes that can be taken off easily as you'll be doing that a lot!
reviews.title: Totally recommend!

reviews.text:
It was from khao lak.
We were with the tour guide Gift (that's her nickname). She was charming, funny and full of knowledge and interesting stories everywhere we went. She made sure to give us the full experience in the best possible way together with the professional driver, who drove both in nature with us and on the road - we felt safe and enjoyed the ride . They took us to both touristy places and local hidden gems. All of our group are vegetarians and they took care of us for a varied and delicious lunch. The trip was very enjoyable. Thank you for an unforgettable experience! :)
reviews.title: Professional, considerate, great teamwork and synchronization

reviews.text:
We had such a fantastic day on the Khao Lak Expedition tour. Oil and Tan were fantastic guides and gave us lots of specific insights and first had experiences. Particular highlight for us was the bamboo rafting and the waterfall where we were the only group there! Oil was very helpful throughout and took time to explain specific parts of the tour and the history whilst also pointing out local wildlife and other attractions whilst travelling. 
Thank you!
reviews.title: Great trip, even better guide!

reviews.text: Great day with Hugues, very complete and pleasant excursion. A very good guide, everything corresponded to our expectations... we recommend them.
reviews.title: Great day
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: This was our best day out in Khao Lak! We are so glad that we met Nok in our hotel  - she advised us on the best things to do and recommended the Khao Lak Day Safari. She even rearranged the tour, later that day when the forecast changed to heavy rain. We were picked up the next day in a fabulously clean and comfortable mini-bus which took us on our day out. James, our tour guide was amazing and not only kept us informed throughout the whole tour, but kept us entertained with his personal stories. The highlights were definitely the Elephant experience and the bamboo rafting. The lunch was plentiful and delicious. I would certainly recommend Khao Lak Land Discovery, but especially James as he is so personable & amiable.
reviews.title: Professional & Experienced - James is a star!

reviews.text:
We did the Half day elephant visit, which we really enjoyed. Kids loved seeing the elephants so close and having the opportunity to swim/bath with them was amazing.

Company also kindly took us too the Sea turtle conservation project in Khao Lak at no extra charge (aside from the entrance fee). Kids loved this too. 

Only negative was that we were in the bus for 1.5hours, picking up other guests and again to drop off guests. With small kids this was a long time, especially as we were only at the elephants for 1.5hrs.
reviews.title: Great trip with kids

reviews.text:
Great day 
Everything is very well organized and the activities follow perfectly. 
We were fortunate to be accompanied by Hugues who guided us, informed us, advised us all day and who never hesitated to answer our multiple questions 
The landscapes admired on this day are sumptuous and the sites chosen are perfect. I highly recommend!
reviews.title: James Bond, perfect visit
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text: We did a 2-day trip with overnight stay. It was very impressive and despite the rain still be able to spot wildlife. The guide (Rolo) was very enthusiastic and experienced. Very happy that we chose the overnight stay as we could visit the national park deeper. The stay is primitive but incredibly nice and a great view. Highly recommended!
reviews.title: Highlight of the holiday in Thailand!

reviews.text: Unfortunately, the difficulty level was not specified. Not suitable for sensitive people. A whole road full of leeches...
reviews.title: Attention leeches

reviews.text: They don't really care about elephants. They used them only to get tourists. Leave this company please
reviews.title: No good treatments with elephants!

reviews.text: It was great! Guide “Gift” was very kind and pleasant. We did the private tour with our kids to James Bond and Next day the elephants and canoeing, it was incredible. Great for Couples or families.
reviews.title: Must do in Khao Lak

reviews.text:
We booked the Amazing 3 Temples Tour for early June. Jenny was our tour guide and she was fantastic. She was really informative and friendly.

We wanted the tour for a Tuesday which is not a day the tour normally happens on. We messaged the operator and they were able to accom date a private tour for us. Thank you.
reviews.title: Fantastic tour

reviews.text:
I was enjoying this lovely tour last Sunday June 5th with Oil as guide and Moo (both from the agency in Khao Lak) as driver. They made the experience beyond amazing, providing great and useful information about the temples and their history which is actually what I was looking for. They are really kind and friendly and either the tours agency as well as the tour deserve a 5 star rating.

Fran
reviews.title: Unforgettable Experience

reviews.text:
The tour of the three temples was amazing. 2 temples were located in caves/ the mountains and the other was a rich and royal temple. The guide, Aris, was amazing. Aris was very knowledgeable, helpful and would do anything to make you comfortable. Aris also gave us a back story on Thailand which was very helpful and good to know. 
The food served waas brilliant and nice too. Definately worth the money.
reviews.title: Absolutely beautiful

reviews.text:
We booked the Khao Sok Paddle and Elephant Tour as well as the Island Snorkel Tour. 
Both guides were really great. Reacts flexibly to events, so we had plenty of time. 
And of course you are not alone on the snorkel tour and we were actually the first anywhere as promised. But doesn't want to grind me out what it's like at times when there are normally many tourists in the region. 
There are much cheaper providers, but organization, planning, execution and friendliness of the guides were great.
reviews.title: Planning and guides top !!

reviews.text:
We visited Surin Islands six years ago. We had forgotten how nice this trip was. 
All you need is suncream and a swimming costume, everything else is provided including towels. Grab some breakfast before you go as what’s provided isn’t great. Lunch was really nice and some food is provided when you return to the pier at around 17,00.
The snorkelling is fantastic with a great array of fish and coral to be been seen over the three stops that we did.
A great trip to do.
We would definitely visit again.
Thank you Seastar, the crew and Latte our very informative tour guide.
reviews.title: Fab trip to do

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful Surin islands trip! The best snorkeling of our Thailand time.

I'm not sure how the different tour companies collaborate (or if it's different because of COVID and each place isn't running separate trips right now), but we booked through Khao Lak Land Discovery although a different company was actually the one running the trip. 

Khao Lak Land Discovery was very prompt in responsiveness and answering our questions. We booked a week or so before we got to Thailand.

This trip included great snorkeling, some hiking, lunch, etc. and all of that was great. This trip actually had a lot of Thai people as opposed to our other trips that were clearly all tourists so it was cool to go where the locals go.

What I didn't love was the speedboat trip to the islands, and this goes for all of the island trips we took - just consider this part to be transit to the islands, not a "boat ride" because they cram everyone inside a speed boat that doesn't have windows at eye level, so you can't see anything and you may as well be on a bus for 90 minutes each way.
reviews.title: Great day at the Surin Islands

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful Phi Phi islands trip!

I'm not sure how the different tour companies collaborate (or if it's different because of COVID and each place isn't running separate trips right now), but we booked through Khao Lak Land Discovery although a different company was actually the one running the trip. 

Khao Lak Land Discovery was very prompt in responsiveness and answering our questions. We booked a week or so before we got to Thailand.

This trip included great snorkeling, some hiking, lunch, etc. and all of that was great. The tour operators also reacted well to the weather, making slight changes throughout the day that allowed us to have excellent weather most of the day, with rain not starting until just a few minutes after we got off the boat to leave.

What I didn't love was the speedboat trip to the islands, and this goes for all of the island trips we took - just consider this part to be transit to the islands, not a "boat ride" because they cram everyone inside a speed boat that doesn't have windows at eye level, so you can't see anything and you may as well be on a bus for 90 minutes each way.
reviews.title: Excellent day on the islands

reviews.text: You will be taken good care of with inspiring an inspiring tour guide. With only a group of 6 (usually it is the double), it felt very intimate and personal - a true honest guide through three different temples. A lot of driving but you will be walking a lot and also see some really true Thai religious culture. I would say this tour is slightly strenous and make sure to bring good shoes. If you are afraid of insects such as cave grasshoppers or bats - this tour is not for you. They will be present around you at certain times, but they wont harm you. Food experience was good with nice views and very adaptable to your needs or service. Drinks, desinfectant, small cold towels was provided at all times. We were informed that this tour is usually crowded, but it felt very calm and easy-going which we know is due to the lesser amount of tourist during the covid-19 pandemic. Thank you Gift for an amazing and passionate tour of three differnet temples in the Phang Nga province.
reviews.title: Passionate and honest temple tour

reviews.text: Due to a misunderstanding when booking, our 2-day wildlife tour became a private tour. Nothing better could have happened to us. So we were able to leave out everything we didn’t want and concentrate on wildlife observations from the longtail boat for a total of 11 hours. Rolo, who after a skeptical look at my photographic equipment, provided me with his 300mm lens (which is not a given in any case) has an extraordinary view for the animals of the national park. It's very moving when it suddenly crashes into the bushes and a wild elephant makes its way to the water. We experienced this several times. We could also observe crooks, white-tailed eagles, lingurs and samba deer. Even a fish owl we were able to discover (very briefly). Due to the misunderstanding mentioned above, we did not stay at the normal camp by the lake, but even further in the national park in floating bamboo houses. All in all, these were 2 unforgettable days for us. Rolo took a lot of time for us, explained a lot about Khao Sok Park and the animals that are native to it and also the rangers who do their duty there. On our next Thailand holiday we will definitely be back with Rolo in Khao Sok National Park.
reviews.title: Unforgettable

reviews.text:
We did two tours with this provider, Similan and James Bond Island (private tour)! Both were perfectly organized, great tour guides (TIM and FABIO), from whom we also learned a lot about the country and people.
(PS: Due to Corona we were of course “lucky” that very few people were at the hotspots)!

CONCLUSION: Better to spend a little more and not sit in the crowded speedboats that arrived after us on the Similan Islands!
reviews.title: Top!!!

reviews.text:
We had a really great time exploring the national park with its beautifull landscapes and animals! 
Rolo is very experienced and the best guide you can wish for. We definitly recommend this tour!
reviews.title: Great wildlife tour!

reviews.text: A true nature experience, doesn’t get more authentic than this! We spend 2 days in beautiful Khao Sok national park and saw elephants, gaur, hornbill and lots of monkeys! The evening and morning safaris by boat was amazing and even if accommodation isn’t the best it’s definitely worth it to experience this kind of true nature life! Our guide Rollo was extremely knowledgeable and service minded, he really made sure to give us the best possible experience!
reviews.title: If you love animals and wildlife, do this!

reviews.text:
In February we did a total of three beautiful tours with this organizer. Surin Isands, Safari and Khao Sok Day Tour.
We can recommend these without restriction. The tour guides were all very friendly, courteous and humorous. At this point high praise and best regards to Michael, Sam and Thomas. 
Pick up at the hotel on time. Prices reasonable. German speaking. Informative. Fresh fruit, delicious lunch, cool drinks. But above all, memories that you never forget. Thanks for that!
We will come back and try the other offers..

Norman and Elli :)
reviews.title: Unrestricted recommendation !

reviews.text:
A great organized trip to Surin. 
It all worked out. Everything went perfectly during our trips to James Bond Island and Khao Lak Safari. Thanks also to the guides Michael and Olli. Great job! We will come back.
reviews.title: Everything great!

reviews.text:
Khao Lak Land Discovery was by far the best agency for guided tours, For these excursions we were lucky to have the best Him-Him or Hum-Hum guide.

He explained everything to us in detail, offered himself to take pictures of us, and always made sure of our well-being. We were lucky enough to have a private guide for most of the excursions.

For transfers, punctuality and Quality Service were required.
reviews.title: Jungle Tour, Cheow Lake Cave Tour, Encounter with Elephant

reviews.text: Did the Off Road Safari. Nice, entertaining guide. The tour was interesting and entertaining. Personally, I liked the paddle ride the best.
reviews.title: Interesting

reviews.text: Went on two day-long tours, and loved them both!  The James Bond Island / cave tour was our favorite, but the Elephant / rafting was a very close second!
reviews.title: Fantastic tours!

reviews.text: We saw more wild animals than expected. The guide was very good in english and were very easy-going, she made our small group work well together. The trip to the rainforest was amazing.
reviews.title: Vacation

reviews.text: We bought usual trip but in fact we (me and my wife) were alone. So this trip was individual, moreover we didn't face any more tourists. We spent our day perfectly, took a lot of emotions and super photos. Special thanks to our guide - O. She's funny and it was really pleasure to talk to hee. She made us day really special. Thanks!
reviews.title: Khao Lak Adventure

reviews.text:
I had such a good trip our with Twan the tour guide and his driver Eddie! I learnt so much and seen amazing sights! Twan and Eddie made the day enjoyable for everyone,  with jokes here and there, was great fun! Also the guide is bilingual so i was able to understand everything and ask questions! Great day out, got to see monkeys when canoeing down the jungle river and what a beautiful waterfall you get to see.
Refreshments provided all day which was a really nice touch!
Once again cant thank these guys enough 11/10 :)

All the best
Craig from England
reviews.title: What a great day out!

reviews.text:
A great day out. We visited the temple first, then made our way to the jungle. It is a steep walk down, but the ground was dry so wasn't slippery. I can imagine if it was raining would be a difficult walk. Nice waterfall at the bottom, which you can have a swim in. There is plenty of ice cold water and coke provided. You get a chance to ride on top of the jeep which is good fun. Also see tarantulas and lizards.
Our guide Dido was very good, had a good sense of humour and very informative. After lunch which was at a lovely setting we went to the mangroves on a paddle boat which was very peaceful and relaxing. Be sure to take plenty of sun screen and a good mosquito repellent.
An excellent day out.
reviews.title: Off road safari

reviews.text:
Good day; like being in Jurassic Park, leaves as large as houses. Need good walking shoes, don’t go in flip flops or you will break a leg or your neck. The day starts with a pick up in your all day jeep so no change overs. 45 mins down the day you cutoff to a river where you pick up your canoe and paddle man. 50 mins on the water through mangroves; if you are Lucky you will see monkeys.then out off the water. It takes 30 mins to walk through the forest to a place to have tea. This is where you need shoes. It is steep then down hill, drops and tree roots to climb over. It has steppingstones but they move and are covered in leaves. I lost my footing and went down saved by a pole I grabbed. Then lunch at a really lovely restaurant that served authentic Thai food, watch out for the heated dish, it will blow your brains out.

Then Jurassic park, off road experience not for the non rough and ready types. It’s great but don’t expect a huge waterfall as it’s a tiny trickle of water over rock. Huge sugar cane forms a canopy over the trail. You get the the opportunity to sit on the roof. Great fun! Back on the main road you are taken to a small temple then home.

The day was let down by the guide who spoke in German most of the time and to us in short condensed phases which on a trip advertised as in English was very bad. I could understand some but my partner was totally lost.

Go b before it’s not there anymore and destroyed by developer’s!
reviews.title: Jurassic Park in the Rain Forest

reviews.text: A wonderful day exploring Thai temples off the tourist trail. Our passionate guide accompanied this day that we will remember forever. A blessing, wonderful exchanges, stars full of eyes, THANK YOU. Discover without moderation
reviews.title: 3 temples

reviews.text:
Collected on time. There were a German couple & 4 English guests on the trip & the company provided both a German & English guide although the German speaking guide (sorry can't remember his name) was Dutch & also spoke fluent English & Thai so any language issues were well covered! He was also quite a card!!
Out guide was called "O", she spoke excellent English & her encyclopedic knowledge  of the temples we visited meant that we got an amazing insight into the Buddhist religion as well as the history & meanings of the structure & decoration of the temples.  
Cold drinks were available on tap & lunch was traditional Thai in a lovely location. Plenty for everyone with a good selection of flavours & spice levels to cater for everyone's taste.   
This is a must do if you visit Thailand. With the knowledge we gained from this day out it gave us a real understanding of the culture & values that are the foundation of the wonderful Thai people.
reviews.title: Fantastic "Must Do" Cultural Tour

reviews.text: We took this tour after seeing recommendation in Lonely Planet. The tour was excellent and worth the 2 hour drive to get to the lake. Our guide Jenny was fluent in English. Helpful and cheerful. Very caring of a lady who was not as fit as the rest of the group. We had a 2 hour jungle trek with a local guide. A cave visit and then lunch at the floating restaurant where you could also swim. The lake is absolutely vast and really stunning as is the surrounding scenery. Company should suggest you bring some extra water. They give you water before and after the trek, obviously they cannot carry extra for a group of 11 into the forest. We had a litre with us in an insulated carry bottle and were glad of it. Super day out.
reviews.title: Chiew alarm Lake, Khao Sok Day tour

reviews.text:
2 weeks ago my boyfriend and I did the Surin Tour and we are still thrilled! At the pier we had a small breakfast before we went on the speedboat. The organization was great from start to finish and you always felt very safe. With the three snorkel stops in different places it was never too crowded with other tourists and you could look at great corals and fish. For us the highlight was the beautiful beach on the island and the crystal clear, turquoise water - just a dream! Here you can really speak of paradise!
Due to the early arrival time at the island we were, as promised, one of the first boats and thus had the beach almost to ourselves. Only when we drove back again the other guests came and it became full. So here too, a big praise to Khao Lak Land Discovery. We will definitely come back to you!
reviews.title: Wonderful beach and crystal clear water!

reviews.text:
I enjoyed an amazing day trip to Surin Island with Khao Lak Land Discovery. 
The snorkeling stops were very beautiful. I saw so many different colourful fish and corals. Our guide was also very good. He gave us lots of information about the marine life of South-east Asia and with the help of pictures we could check what kind of fish and corals we have seen. 
Another highlight was the lunch at a wonderful beach. I have never seen such a crystal clear water before. 
I can definitely recommend this trip!
reviews.title: Amazing snorkeling experience

reviews.text:
Magnificent day thanks to our Francophone guide Camille who thanks to his professionalism, his endearing personality and his knowledge of the country will remain unforgettable.
Eric and Muriel
reviews.title: Thank you Camille

reviews.text: Our family including a 15yr and 10yr old did the Khao Sok day tour with Khao Lak Land Discovery. We booked last minute and they were very helpful in the office. Picked up on time with only 12 guests, our tour guide, Gift was great. Her English was excellent and she was very informative throughout. First stop was the local market, nice to have a quick look but not something that is essential to see. The canoeing down the Khao Sok river was beautiful despite the low water level. The  guides spotted snakes in trees for us and couldn't help but see the family of monkeys hanging by the river bank. The lunch stop was a lovely tree house hotel, then onto the monkey temple. Some were a bit angry but Gift was very good keeping us away from these ones. I was a bit hesitant with the elephant bathing due to their welfare. It was short time scrubbing the mud from them and feeding them, enough time though for the elephant. They seem to be well cared for and they have been born in captivity so could not be left in the  wild as they wouldn't know how to fend for themselves. Overall, the tour was great as small group and would recommend them for this type of tour.
reviews.title: Khao Sok Day Trip

reviews.text:
We (+ our two "big" children) were on the 11.02.20 for two days with Rolo. Everything super organized. Rolo is very nice and knows what he is doing.
The accommodation simple but TOP! No cell phone reception, no electricity at a later hour, no shower with its own toilet....you don't need that here. For me it was an experience as evening or even in the morning nature with the many animals came forward. At 4:30 the monkeys were doing gymnastics above our cabin in the trees ;-). You can't describe it......you just have to do it.
Big praise to Rolo....stay as you are!
reviews.title: A dream of landscape, animals, experiences and peace

reviews.text:
We went on the dolphine cruise daytrip from Khao Lak. Short transfer to the boat. 
A nice day on the sea. 
First a visit at a hidden beach, there where no other groups there, only us! 
Good lunch at the boat. 
Then snorkling and later on fishing. 
Great and frendly staff, extra thank to our guide ’Charm’!! 
We went with our soon to be 3 year old son, worked well on the boat.
reviews.title: Great daytrip from Khao Lak

reviews.text: Small group led in perfect German. At every station, you were among yourself and it was never overflowing. The staff competent and friendly, the guide scored with excellent expertise and humor. From now on ONLY Khao Lak Land Discovery!
reviews.title: Never again where Anders

reviews.text: Excellent day with our French-speaking guide Kim who masters Buddhism perfectly. We had an unforgettable day for 2 couples with teenager and young children. Discover Buddhism, their culture. We were even able, thanks to our guide, to realize a Buddhism prayer, We were also able to meet monk who gave us a blessing. Brief to recommend absolutely.
reviews.title: Visit of the 3 temples

reviews.text:
Very enticing program but very mixed once there! 

The activity with the elephant is very good, kindness, laughter, memories, landscape, finally sharing everything is part of the game!

The visit of the temple was beautiful and complete, we read a lot of information thanks to our guide.

The perfect bamboo ride right after the meal to digest well.

As for the rest I find it incomplete for the price. 

Waterfall time is half an hour (yet it costs nothing ) 

Activity with turtles! M biggest disappointment the entry is first of all 60 baht only, as much to go there by his own means or not at all since there is nothing. 4 unhappy pool with 3 different type of turtle. Visiting an aquarium would have been better.

The restaurant was good but we were rushed to eat. 
The visit of the stranded boat due to the tsunami (free) was short, uninformative, no desire we were dropped off blind and left immediately. 

Well good apartment the elephant and the bamboo ride I advise against, it is a waste of money knowing that there halves of the activities offered are free you have as much to go yourself for less. 
Guide who forces to tip and it does not make you want at all.
reviews.title: Review safari

reviews.text:
We used Khao Lak Land Discovery for both transfer to and from the airport, boat trip to Surin Islands and Off Road Safari. Everything worked really well and although the trips may be priced a bit for the expensive side, we chose Khao Lak Land Discovery due to the good level of service (and the price for airport shuttle seemed to be quite on the same level as the others).
Off road safari tour was a really good experience; nice combination of temple, jungle tour, waterfalls and canoeing in the mangrove. Not least our guide Kim was sovereign. She spoke superb English and knew a great deal about what we saw and drove past - it gave it all an extra dimension that she could tell so much along the way. It may have been a lot of road driving from one place to another (and it felt pretty strong when you sat behind the load of the car and roamed around - though with seat belts), but it was necessary to see so much on the same trip. All in all a good experience, which can definitely be recommended.
reviews.title: Good experience - can be recommended

reviews.text: We had a very nice day.  The rhythm was good with a temple in the morning and 2 in the afternoon.  Meal in accordance with excursions on the area in pleasant restaurant. Nice day.
reviews.title: Visit of temples

reviews.text: I have used this agency for pick up from Phuket airport a number of times and always found it easy to locate them outside the terminal building. This last visit the driver even furnished me with a bottle of water. I have also used them for tours once in hotel.
