reviews.title: Very Friendly

reviews.text:
We had an excellent day with Gift as our guid today on the Khao lak safari. The temple was beautiful and memorial to the tsunami was informative and interesting, The turtle conservation place was great. The bamboo raft was fun, but if you don’t like snakes make sure the guides know! 😱 the lunch was also great! 

The best part of the day for us was with the Elephants at the welfare sanctuary. We  were lucky enough to only be a party of two so had the whole experience to ourselves. Never thought we’d get that up close to Elephants. Truly Amazing!
reviews.title: Amazing day out! Beat our expectations.

reviews.text: Picked up in an air conditioned minibus and taken to the pier around 30 mins away to check in for our trip to the Surin Islands.  Very well organised processes at the pier where we were split into groups depending on our language and given wrist bands and life jackets.  Speed boat took around and 60-75 mins to the first snorkelling spot.  Enjoyed three snorkelling spots (approx 20-30 mins at each) plus lunch on the island.  Food was plentiful and of reasonable quality on the island.  Plenty of snacks and soft drinks available on the boat.
reviews.title: Well organised trip to Surin Islands

reviews.text:
We spent the day in the jungle doing 4×4 watching the animals we saw .. then we made a pose to go see a waterfall where we bathed it was beautiful all this vegetation... at lunch we ate very well in a place where the setting is just breathtaking... after canoeing..day away from the crowd..in places where nature is preserved...really to do..
With the guide Lea we got a lot of information in French. She is really at the top...thank you..
reviews.title: Wonderful day

reviews.text:
Tour was good, guide and driver very knowlegable and friendly. Did see some animals, enjoyed the trips out on the boat
Food at the lodge was good, dinner especially. Toilets and showers not great, did not want to use them. It is difficult to walk from the boat / restaurant to the toilet and huts, needs improvement as our trainers got wet all the time. Also the way into the water for swimming, ladder missing
reviews.title: Great tour, accommodation ok

reviews.text:
I made a last minute decision to visit elephants up close, and it was one of the best decisions I made on my trip. Not only was our tour guide, Volker, fluent in English he was knowledgeable about the elephants and also Thai life in general. I always have lots of questions and he had lots of answers for me.
The elephants were indeed gentle giants and we we’re warned ahead of time that they are never pressured to do anything they don’t want to do. Lucky for us, they did want to get bathed and petted.
Definitely a good way to spend half a day.
reviews.title: Wonderful bonding with the elephants

reviews.text: We were on tour with Rolo for two days. Perfect organization, competent guide and magnificent nature. Thank you, Rolo.
reviews.title: Khao Sok

reviews.text:
We went on a Day Trip from Khao Lak yesterday to Tsunami Museum, Swimming & Washing Elephants,Bamboo Rafting, Fantastic Lunch, visit to the Temple,Turtle Conservation Centre.
It was an amazing day out. We highly recommend this tour. Our Guide was very friendly, spoke English very well and explained everything in detail.
reviews.title: Fantastic Day Tour / Highly Recommended

reviews.text:
We had 2 super nice days in Khao Sok National Park. For nature lovers just the thing! 
The organization was top. 
Anyone who wants to watch wild animals will definitely see some here. Unfortunately we were a bit unlucky and could not see elephants, but monkeys, deer, owl, various birds and wild boars. 
It was still very impressive and exciting to hike through the jungle with Rolo and the Ranger. 
The cottages are great, no more than equipped with a mattress, pillows and blankets. At night you have to go to the toilet with a flashlight. 
Those who love the adventure will love it there too. 
It was a great time, thanks to Rolo and Nong (captain). I would love to come back 😊😊😊
reviews.title: Great experience

reviews.text:
Lovely day with our guide Ann. We were the only couple so a private tour. Started with visiting the Tsunami memorial which was very moving and thought provoking especially with Anne’s personal experience. Then on to a temple which was interesting but if you’re a bit squeamish you may find the dead monk in a glass case slightly disturbing. On to the Turtle sanctuary which although does really good work making sure baby turtles survive and then are taken back to the wild, I found the older, injured turtles didn’t seem to have much room to move. Following that the elephant welfare centre. It was amazing to get up close and personal with the elephants and they appear to be very well cared for. 
We were then given a great lunch followed by freshly ground and made coffee in amongst the coffee plants. Then on to the bamboo raft led by a really friendly oarsman. This was very peaceful and a great experience. When we returned there was a photo in a frame waiting for us for 300Bht. We weren’t obliged to buy but did as it was a nice photo - it did seem a bit Disney though. The last thing was a visit to a waterfall 
All in all a wonderful day. Ann was a lovely guide, very personable.
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari

reviews.text: Wow! Great experience! Perfect organisation, small group, personalised and our guide Aye was the best! Khao Sok national park, Kanoo, elephants, lunch & temple. Price seems to be high but you really get what you pay!
reviews.title: You have to try!

reviews.text:
My 16 year old daughter and I went on this trip on Christmas Day. 
There were 6 tourists and ‘Oil’ our extrovert and very knowledgable guide made sure we all get on by introducing us on the mini bus and taking group photos. 
The trip to 3 very different temples, was very interesting and gave a really insight not only into the Buddhist religion but into the ‘Thai way of life’. 
I feel I understand these charming people so much better now I understand how this way of life affects their lives.  

Lunch was amazing, fab food, fab views and (4 Brits & 2 Danes) fab company, 

You also get to see the beautiful and dramatic country Thailand is. It was a full day and the driver made sure he had plenty of ice cold cola, water and cold, refreshing wipes to keep us going.

This trip is worth every penny, we had a wonderful day and it has given me a respect for the beautiful Thai people 🙏
reviews.title: Fantastic Experience

reviews.text:
Great experience with elephants.
Visit a temple, with a great explanation.
Beautiful discovery of the turtle hospital, bamboo raft ride exoloration of fauna and flora ....waterfall and memirial tsunami...not to forget the 26.12.2004
Thanks to camille our exceptional guide who has enchanted us with the culture of this beautiful country
reviews.title: Khao lak safari

reviews.text:
Just had an amazing day being looked after by Tim at Khao Lak Land Discovery. Very early start at 4.30am but we’ll worth it to avoid the heat and the crowds. We did the Hong Island trip and with vouchers given at the hotel paid a total of 5600 baht which was great value for the activities we did. We contacted the tour company by WhatsApp the day before we wanted to go and everything was arranged within a few minutes. 
Tim was an excellent guide speaking fluent English and German. There were just 6 of us on the tour. 
The whole tour was really well thought out with lots of extra experiences thrown in along the way as well as the lucky sight of a dolphin on the way to James Bond island!
Highly recommend.
reviews.title: Fabulous day

reviews.text:
Great on every level.
Beautiful temples, our guide Kim was on top!
Enriching visit, highly recommendable.
reviews.title: 3 temples

reviews.text:
Excellent day.cute Franco-Cambodian Kim guide,discreet,knowing his subject on the tips of the nails,available.
Small group of 8 pers.
We were practically the only ones. very nice. Not to be missed.Kim:kap koun kap!
reviews.title: 3 temples contemplate you....

reviews.text:
It was 2 eventful and super organized days. Everything was well organized, from pick-up at our hotel to transfer to our next hotel in Khao Lak. Sam was a very nice, courteous tourist guide. He constantly explained nature, animals, Thailand, its culture, its people and its customs without being intrusive. We spent the night at the comfortable Rock&Tree House, also a stunning hotel set amidst trees, which exceeded our expectations for an overnight stay in the tree house. The food there was also great.
The tours (longtail boat on the Ratchaprapha incl. hike in the rainforest, lunch and visit to a cave, river trip incl. traditional coffee break, visit to a temple and a local market as well as elephant bathing) were all included (as was the overnight stay) in the price for the 2 days. Everything was very well organized and structured and that even though we arrived deviating from the given days (Tuesday).

Communication in advance also worked great in this regard.

All in all it was 2 unforgettable days. Sam was great. I can only recommend it. I wish you many more customers after us:-)
reviews.title: 2-day tour in Khao Sok National Park

reviews.text: They were brilliant, such a brilliant day. Thank you it's a day I'll never forget. It's a must if going near
reviews.title: Surin

reviews.text:
If you want to learn a lot about Buddhist culture on your Thailand trip, I can really recommend the Amazing 3 Temple Tour.
Each temple on the tour is fascinating in its own way and has its own story to tell.
In between I could ask all the questions I had and got everything answered in detail.
An absolute must for anyone who wants to get involved with culture in Khao Lak.
reviews.title: Lots of interesting information on an exciting tour

reviews.text: Peter did an excellent job explaining the history and philosophy of the temples and the walking Market was a great exposure to the local people .
reviews.title: Great information on Buddhist temples

reviews.text: Well organised trip. Great guide Oil. Attentive and looked after us impeccably. Lovely two hours on secluded beach, dinner on boat delicious and then snorkelling/fishing/swimming and sun bathing. Only 13 on trip. We was the only british aboard however included and as I said before looked after.
reviews.title: Lazy beach and boat day

reviews.text:
Did a 3 day tour with Rolo. Lots of sights and experiences, including monkeys and elephants in the wild. For those who are looking for unspoilt nature without the tourism common in many other areas of Thailand - clear recommendation!
Best regards, Matthias & Kristina
reviews.title: Kao Lak Jungle Tour - Recommendation!

reviews.text: We booked 3 trips and all were perfect! Organization, professionalism and friendliness are really excellent. Phuket private, Hong Island and just 2 days Khao Sok make our holiday unforgettable! Special thanks to the best driver in Thailand: Tan, you are the ruler of the roads and Sam: we will NEVER forget your way of showing us your country!!! Thank you very much!!!🙏👍🏻🙏
reviews.title: Not cheap, but INEXPENSIVE!!!! TOPP!!!!

reviews.text:
Very disappointed with this. 2000 baht per person for a 'half day tour' to an elephant welfare centre.

Firstly, it wasnt a half day tour. It is basically an hour there once travel is removed. We were also guided by a slightly sketchy Dutch guide that was coughing his guts up the whole time.

Secondly, and most importantly, it isn't a welfare centre. It is basically a pen, with cages hidden in the distance, owned by a Thai family that have elephants bread in captivity. I must be missing how this area benefits the animals that look dead behind the eyes. The elephants respond to command and are ordered around and then washed by groups of tourists. It isn't good to see, although the only positive I can take is that it opened my eyes and made me realise how naive i had been by expecting something different. I would never pay for anything where humans interact with animals again.
reviews.title: Half Day Elephant Welfare Centre (not really a welfare centre)

reviews.text:
We are in Khao Lak for the second time and wanted to experience jungle. With Zeta and Eddi a really successful tour. Super organized. Prepared for anything. And drew attention to many things.
The all-round carefree package. With great guides.
reviews.title: Great tour great guides

reviews.text:
We booked our airport transfer to Khao Lak on line before leaving the UK.  They had a very good deal on at the time and we decided to book the return once we arrived.  We were unsure what to expect but we needn't have worried.

The pick up at the airport was well organised and our driver who spoke limited English was excellent and drove well.  My wife is a very nevous passenger and insisted on asking for him on our return.

A very reliable company who have a office in Khao Lak Centre.
reviews.title: First Class Airport Transfer

reviews.text:
We can't find words. Mother and daughter went to discover this part of Thailand! (for the 16th birthday of my dear daughter) Enchanting trip thanks also to Kim, passionate, cultivated, so nice and so cool!
We took 3 excursions with Kim and are both scarred for life of this exceptional trip and Kim has a lot to do with it. Besides, if you read this message we kiss you very hard.
Hugues is also very good advice!
A thousand thanks 😘
Juliette and Aurélie
reviews.title: Great! Fabulous! Extraordinary!

reviews.text: We spent the day with Kim our Francophone guide, kim explained a lot about Thai culture and Buddhism, it is really exciting and very interesting, the temples are very impressive and have all their peculiarities. The driver Mr Singhanat Chumsri was very careful with us throughout the day. If you want to know more about Thailand and Buddhism this is a very nice excursion. I really recommend this agency that takes care of you from start to finish and in very small group. Ask the French guide when booking, kim was really t on top!
reviews.title: A very nice day

reviews.text:
A great day starting with a pick up at the hotel. Our guide Gift, checked we had everything to make the day as special as possible. We were joined by 2 other families - making our group a total 8. It was very comfortable in the minivan and our driver was lovely. First stop was a temple, our guide was very informative. Our 2 boys were desperate to see the elephants. Our boys were very patient at the temple.  Then we went to see the turtles, again our guide was very informative about the conservation of turtles and was happy to answer questions. After a short drive we got to the bamboo station where we boarded our bamboo raft. The boys thought this was brilliant as did we. The experience was magical and we saw several different species of butterflies, frogs and a snake. All making it perfect. The rafts go over a few tiny rapids - which get your bum wet! Lots of giggles followed! A trip highlight. Once back on the bank, our boys were surprised with birthday cake - making it a birthday to remember. A quick stop at the waterfall. A chance for a quick dip. Next stop was lunch - this was a selection of dishes. All well prepared. The crispy chicken being a favourite. Lots of water and soft drinks were provided throughout the trip. Then a 40 minute drive to the elephant welfare centre - giving everyone time for a post lunch nap. Perfectly timed. We arrived at the elephant centre - beautiful setting. Three beautiful calm and happy elephants arrived to several wows and ahhh. They were adorable and enjoyed many bananas. This was a highlight. Our guide gift continued to provide us with information about the elephants. After the elephants devoured all the bananas they went for a dip in the river and a chance for us to scrub them. A great relaxing and calm experience especially as we were the only people there. Heavy rain fell cutting this experience a little short as we took cover. Gift provided us with rain coats, drinks and sliced pineapple. The heavy rain continued as we left the elephants and we drove to the tsunami memorial and police boat this was a moving experience. This was a 
great day out enjoyed by the whole family. The calm, smooth running of the trip made it a brilliant way to spend our twins birthday. Thank you.
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari - making memories

reviews.text:
A great half day tour.  had a 4year old elephant to trek 1km with
to river where you get to scrub and swim with the animal
so much fun.  The guide of tour Robert very very good  and
he wants a wife!!!!!!!
reviews.title: washing elephants

reviews.text:
This tour is an absolute must for anyone who wants to holiday in the Khao Lak area and experience something very special. We spent 3 days with Rolo - a brilliant guide who knows the jungle and nature reserve super well and authentically lives and conveys the love for the animals and nature. Bathing and feeding the elephants on the first day was the first highlight of many! The overnight stay in the comfortable tree house was something very special, but the absolute highlight was the longtail boat ride on the reservoir through the fascinating landscape of Khao Sok National Park. On our three species of game we were lucky enough to see elephants up close several times and to observe, among other things, crooks, sambar deer, warblers and various bird species. However, the biggest adventure of this tour for us was the time we got to spend with the rangers. The unique atmosphere there, the warm people, the life in the untouched nature we will not soon forget. The rustic bamboo huts were equipped with a thick mattress, toilets and laundry facilities were also available.  

Thank you Rolo, for this great tour - it was incredibly great and impressive!
reviews.title: Khao Sok 3 Day Treehouse Reservoir - a great experience!

reviews.text: How can you come to Thailand and not do this trip? Amazing and enlightening day out. Lovely thai lunch included. Guided for the day by Ran
reviews.title: 3 temples

reviews.text: AMAZING well worth the money, Tanya our guide was brilliant! included lunch at a jungle restaurant :)
reviews.title: took part in an all day excursion visiting waterfalls, monkey temple (take bananas!) bathing elephants and river rafting

reviews.text:
We visited Khao Lak for the second time and wanted to learn about the temples and Buddhism this time. When we heard that our guide is a former monk and so we should know everything about it, we were already very pleased. It turned out, however, that he did not want to teach us the stories of Buddha, nor could he explain the philosophy behind Buddhism. It just meant he couldn't tell everything, so he told almost nothing. He also always ran ahead to the bus or temple so you couldn't talk. And last but not least, he conveyed to each of the questions feeling naive and asking inappropriate questions.
What a time and a waste of money!
reviews.title: 3 temples with former monk total failure

reviews.text:
Incredible tour from this group. We were 2 adults and 1 infant and Oil really is the star of the show as was Phu the driver (pooh bear). It was a small private tour of 6 people and we arrived at james bond island on long tail before the masses for stunning photo Ops and exploring the island before a canoe trip arpund the bay and visiting the stunning Muslim sea gypsy floating village. The shopping here was cheap and a lovely private tasty buffet provided for us. Oil and pooh bear would constantly check enough to squeeze in monkey temple for my wee girl. Oil was a fountain of knowledge and obviously very passionate. She deserves
 some serious recognition. Please don't miss this trip it's worth every penny.
reviews.title: James Bond Island, floating village, monkey temple

reviews.text: We had a really positive experience renting a motorcycle for around Khao lak and a car to visit Khao Sok Cheow Lan Lake from here. Hary helped us with both rentals and he was great. He was gave us great tips on what to do on Motorcy and helped us plan our day trip in the car to Cheow Lan lake. We would definitely recommend this place and would rent from here again. Thank you, Hary!
reviews.title: Motorcy and car rental

reviews.text:
Booked through the office in Khao Lak Bang Nang office. Off season so I think the price THB 1000 reflected this.

Was given a mobile phone number to call if we had a problem, as well as the office number.

Booked for 14;00 for transfer to airport.

Driver was waiting for us at 13:50. Think he had been there for some time.

Good clean van for the four of us with baggage.

Safe drive to airport.

Highly recommend.
reviews.title: Great service
reviews.review_of: Phuket Airport Khao Lak Private Minivan Transfer

reviews.text: Definitely worth booking a trip on the spot. I must have gotten 1,000 Baths off. Ask not 1 but at least 3 agencies what they charge for a trip. Be bold or you'll pay the top price.
reviews.title: Temples

reviews.text:
Yesterday my wife & I spent a day in & around Khlong Phanom National Park.
We were picked up from The Sands Resort @ 8.30am welcomed by Rollo our guide for the day & headed north.
Riding on the open top of a Misubishi Triton with bench seats & seat belts.
Felt a little exposed to the elements but sometimes living a little on the edge like the locals do just has to be done.. Thankfully our driver was very capable. Wind in the hour with sunglasses on felt great.
Make sure you have a good dose of sunscreen too.
1st stop was ~ an hour later at a canoe pick up point where we were canoed through the mangroves while we relaxed & took in the sights.
On our journey we saw different kinds of birds & had pointed out to us the odd small snake sleeping in the trees. 
This took aprox. 30 minutes till we were ashore. Then it was a 5 min. walk to where we had a break resting under a shelter having tea & coffee provided. You could have cold bottled water or cola as well. Toilet facilities there also.
Perhaps a small snack provided would have been a nice touch.
Once rested we were driven from there to our place for an early lunch. Was in a tucked away place but was lovely (see pics) none the less giving us a chance to chat with a couple who were from Dresden Germany. 
We were then driven to Khlong Phanom National Park.
It was there Rollo invited both my wife & I atop of the custom built Mitsubishi Triton double seat over the cab for best view. Once seat belted in away we went! 
Thankfully it was a nice slow trip along the solid track around up & down slopes. We stopped every now & then as Rollo showed us lizards from the trees, coaxing big spiders from their holes, viewing elephant tracks etc. 
Eventually we reached the spot where the Ton Tonmak waterfall was so we dismounted & walked down the tight track to it.
It was a beautiful sight so many pics were taken.
Back to the Triton up the hill. 
If you are coming on this trek make sure you have at least covered shoes with grip. Thongs won't cut it.
It was the German couples turn to be in the top seat. 
A thunder shower appeared so it was a quick scramble to get the back ute cover up so all good. It didn't last long so then the cover was put away.
Once we were out of the park it was time for home visiting a temple along the way.
All in all a great day.
If going you need to be prepared to rough it a little. 
Thanks Rollo, Eddy & co.!
reviews.title: Great day out bush

reviews.text:
Our 3-day trip (no printing error, I expressly rate the 3-day tour here!) to the rainforest led again (my Third party KhaoSok trip) Rolo - it was just nice again! The program was slightly changed (elephant riding is no longer - now it goes in water with the thick skins, very nice!). The weather was really good despite the rainy season, we were totally lucky with it. Rolo explained a lot again, gave insider tips - you can feel that the rainforest is his "second home". Sometimes you might think that Rolo knows every tree there by now :D
Bathing with the elephants was very nice, you finally get really "on" the animals. You also get the impression that this is fun for the elephants - and: there are beautiful pictorial motifs!
At the lake it was also absolutely beautiful again, there are new cabins to sleep in, there are now really thick mattresses in there. But the overall flair up there on the lake is breathtaking without changing. Should everyone have done it once, you always realize how little you sometimes need in life. We have seen animals many, many more than in the previous years. Wild elephants, rhino birds, monkeys, white-tailed eagles, varans, crooks - there were also animals to discover for the night trip.
At Rock & Tree House it was also great again, the entire facility is a gem in its own right, who sees this for the first time and stands in front of it, first of all has this certain "wow effect" in which the rest of the world is faded out.
Food was very good, there is always something to drink anyway (the guides of KhaoLakLandDiscovery (KLLD) always have boxes of drinks on ice!).
Since we were there in the rainy season, the tours were not overbooked, so we then had a private tour with Rolo as a threesome. That was the icing on the cake, which made the tour especially valuable for us. Bad of course for KLLD that not so many people booked the trip.
Dear readers: I can only recommend it to everyone - if KhaoSok, then definitely the 3 days with KLLD! I have now to the 2. Once done, before that there was the 2 day tour. You have a lot more time here with the 3 days and can enjoy all this and get involved. Since you spend a lot of time in the car and on the boat, you should really treat yourself to the 3 days - no one will regret it, I promise!
Overall conclusion: for me (us) the really nicest excursion of KLLD (also very nice and recommended: Hong Island!), and the crazy thing is: we will book the 3 days next holiday in KhaoLak safely again, is just zuuuuuu beautiful!
The booking with KLLD worked excellently, we have already booked this from Germany, as it is sometimes better to definitely get the desired nights (with good booking situation you can just be out of luck). The advice and scheduling at KLLD is top - there is absolutely no problem here if you want to postpone an excursion for any reason. At KLLD you also look at the weather forecast and then try to find the best package for you with the guest. Also with all other problems (for me it was travel sickness) you always fall on open and above all helpful ears in the office of KLLD (just past the traffic light left side facing north).
At this point: lots of lovely greetings to Rolo (he did really great with a lot of love for things as always), to Lars and Paula from the KLLD office (totally nice and dear people who are absolutely attentive to the guests), it was 3 really nice days in the rainforest - a big THANK YOU to all of you from Annika & Co.:-)
reviews.title: 3 days Kaho Sok - so coooooooool :-)

reviews.text:
I had one of the best days out on this Safari.
Once at the Jungle track, I sat on the roof, belted in for safety.
The trek up and down this hill was amazing, our guides explained things on the way, we stopped to view amazing views, were shown Turantula nests, even managing to see a couple, and film them at the front of their nests.
A trek to the waterfall was just brilliant, fast rushing water just beautiful to see, and hear.
We saw where elephants had recently roamed through, even wild pig.
A huge monitor lizard ran up the embankment as we approached.

We had lunch at a beautiful spot where lotus grow.
Canoeing through the mangroves we were privelidged to actually see a troop of monkeys at play, and able to film them.
We saw snakes resting amongst the foliage, ciccado symphony the whole trip.
Was a wonderful memorable day, thank you to all.
reviews.title: Wonderful Day Trip

reviews.text: Just got back from Khao Sok Day Trip and it was so much fun that I had to leave a review. Tanya, our guide, was amazing and really took the time to tell us about the area. The canoeing was magnificent - saw snakes, huge frogs, iguanas and monkey's to name a few of all the fauna in the gorgeous national forest. The waterfalls, elephant bathing and views are all amazing and so was lunch. Definitely worth booming through this company and I can't wait for the next adventure in a few days time!!!
reviews.title: Would highly recommend this company!

reviews.text: We booked the Amazing 3 Temple tour and our guide Miss Gift and driver Mr Air showed us a wonderful day. The three temples we visited were not like each other and the contrast made the day all the more interesting. Miss Gift was a fantastic guide. Her English was excellent and her knowledge of the temples and area in general was fascinating. She was extremely informative and explained everything very well. The lunch venue was beautiful. It offered lovely views and the food was delicious. There were plenty of opportunities to take photos, ask questions and look around each site. We’d highly recommend this tour. Not only great value, but a really interesting experience.
reviews.title: Amazing 3 Temples

reviews.text:
With the many offers Khao Lak provides, we decided to go on a wildlife tour with overnight stay in an artificially created treehouse area and a tour in the middle of the jungle - fortunately! 
After a two-hour drive, we took the Long Tail boat for another 2 hours into the deep jungle, far from any civilization. 
Once there, accommodation with simple bamboo cabins, water bungalows, a headquarters and super nice Thais awaited us. 

Led by the professional and super cool guide named Rolo, two long tail boat safaris and a jungle hike are conducted. Watching the wild jungle dwellers like elephants, monkeys, dragonflies, snakes and much more is the bright madness. 

Those who seek their adventure of a lifetime will find it there. We will never forget this two-day trip.
reviews.title: Wildlife Tour with Rolo- the best guide

reviews.text:
This tour in Khao Sok National Park meets all expectations to experience wild animals and the jungle experience. The property is 2 hours by longtail boat from the reservoir wall at the entrance. If you want to see the national park with its originality, you really can’t avoid this tour. The accommodation is accordingly simple and designed for an expedition. You sleep in a bamboo cabin overlooking the reservoir - just amazing. The food on site really impressed us as there were very delicious Thai dishes for breakfast, lunch and dinner. 

Thanks to the super nice and experienced guide Rolo, we saw an elephant, rare bird species, deer, bird spiders and much more during the wildlife observations. 

Thank you for this incredible experience!
reviews.title: Experience the jungle up close

reviews.text:
We did the tour in August, we were lucky enough that the three of us were alone with our guide Rolo and boatmaster Lu. The scenery you can admire on the way out is impressively beautiful. As expected it rained again and again but we were well equipped by Rolo with rain jackets and caoe. On our boat trips we still saw many animals in the wild. Elephant , gibbons, macaques..., white-tailed eagle, kingfisher, sea owl, gaur... The cottages we stayed in were dry and pleasant. There were , normal’ toilets and a shower in a washhouse. The food that was waiting for us after the excursions was good and plentiful .At the station you are allowed to rumpaddle with the canoes and can also go swimming very well.
It was a great experience even for our 11 year old son and we can recommend it to anyone!
reviews.title: Nature experience

reviews.text: We did a 3-Temple tour and our guide was Miss Gift, our driver Mr Tee. The temples were well contrasted, interesting and Miss Gift was very knowledgeable and informative about buddhism and Thai culture. She is also a very friendly, happy soul. Lots of cold drinks provided, rain jackets and a lovely lunch. I'd recommend it to learn something about the country you're visiting. Oh, and the Phuket Night Market is a MUST. We found it overwhelming for about an hour, with so many people, so much food, the mopeds, but after that we got into it and it was a fabulous experience. We'd like to go again!
reviews.title: Temples and Phuket Market

reviews.text: Excellent excursion not in French at all so explanations exclusively Englishes if you do not understand it go your way ...the gift guide knows its subject by heart the driver very well ...in the rain but seeing how hot it is on some certzins site ct youpi so much better...as in English explanations to extend can be long very long so I picked up at times...but that it was waouh...watch the steps of the stairs slopes...don't take little ones with you...and it's barefoot everywhere...meal very good no spicy..you speak 🤨 I recommend if you like the steps the English language...otherwise excellent full of pictures 🤗
reviews.title: Amazon not in English

reviews.text:
My wife and I had a great day visiting the 3 temples. First of all I would like to say that our tour-guide Boy and the driver really tried to avoid the big crowds in all 3 of the temples, so we had time to visit the temples 'in peace'. It was a very nice experience to see the different kind of temples in the neighborhood.

The whole day got even more interesting due to the input of tour-guide Boy. He is a very kind, friendly and knowledgeable person who taught us a lot about Buddhism, the Temples and his own life when he was a Monk. Thanks again for that Boy!
reviews.title: Amazing Three Temples Tour

reviews.text:
Picked up promptly. Great communication from firm beforehand.
Grouped with another family also from England.
Our guide, Gift, knew everything there is to know about temple life and traditions- or it certainly seemed that way. No question was unanswered, and then some. Very goo English and clearly loves her subject matter. This is a long day in the high humidity, and several of the party fell asleep on the way back to hotels. Visited 3 Temples...all very different, and chosen and for that reason.  Each had the wow factor, but for different reasons - natural; awe inspiring; scale; intricacies. I urge you to see for yourself...
Lunch was a splendid affair, overlooking podcast formations over a lake.
Thoroughly recommend this trip with KhaoLak eco land discovery.
reviews.title: Thoroughly recommend

reviews.text: The Khao Lak Safari involves all must-sees of Khao Lak and its surroundings. Our guide was just perfect (Ray) and we had lots of fun. The tour was well planned through with enough time on every stop and our tour group was really small, which made it extremely comfortable. Ray gave us many interesting information and even asked the locals when he was unsure of a certain fact. Khan Lak Land Discovery and the Safari Tour is highly recommendable! Thank you for everything.
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari - a perfect trip

reviews.text: We took the Khao lak Safari today. Well-organised, super-friendly guide and driver. Nice, generous lunch. Highlights for us were bathing and scrubbing the elephants and the bamboo rafting.
reviews.title: Busy, brilliant day

reviews.text: We booked this trip with Khao Lak Land Discovery and visited the Big Budda, Wat Chalong, Old Phuket Town and Simon Show our driver Supap apologies if the name is incorrect was great he drove safely and was funny too!
reviews.title: DIY Phuket day tour

reviews.text:
Full day trip.
Started with Quad Moto in a beautiful location, riding through flat and rough terrain and across a shallow river as well. Perfect for 1st timers.
Then was time for White Water Rafting along a mild course and reasonable water flow and speed. Paddlers were very good. 
Next was Zipline over the river course and again was quite mild, so good for beginners.
Finished with visit to a Buddhist temple.
Reasonable lunch was included and water/soft drinks supplied free by KLLD.
Ms Waan, local representative, was a pleasure to deal with
reviews.title: Khao Lak Adventure.

reviews.text: Super kind and very professional. We will use your service again next time when we come to Khao Lak.
reviews.title: Perfect transfer from airport to Khao Lak
reviews.review_of: Khao Lak Hotel Phuket Airport Private Minivan Transfer

reviews.text: Not as advertised actually a very expensive taxi tour of the country.......started off well with a drive to a waterfall which saw some dirt track driving but no swimming.  Then we had to drive for hour on main road to get lunch, no views just someone’s fish farm, nice enough.  Then another hour of main roads back the way we came I on main roads to get in a canoe and get paddled through mangroves, water too dirty to think about swimming.  This was followed by more main road driving, in all over half the tour driving in open cab getting covered in road grime in hot sun.  Save yourself thousands and get a taxi to a waterfall and tell him to drive around randomly  for hours.  I feel this tour could have been better and will be asking for some refund.
reviews.title: 4wd tour of doom

reviews.text: what a great day out went to see a temple sea turtles a waterfall went on abit of a bush bash in four wheel drive went bamboo rafting had a great lunch tour guide tanya was top notch and so was the driver well  worth the money
reviews.title: bamboo rafting

reviews.text:
We went on the tour from May 10 - 11 and were incredibly lucky that we and our guide Rolo were alone.
It was a super great experience and despite some pouring rain we saw many animals ( elephants, warblers, gibbons, rhino birds and much more ). It was all super organized and also the food in the very simple "restaurant" was very good.
The overnight stay in the very simple bamboo hut we liked very much.
Overall, it was the highlight of our Thailand holiday and we can recommend it to anyone. 
Thanks again to our guide Rolo.

Kind regards from Romy and Frank
reviews.title: A super great experience with unforgettable impressions

reviews.text:
amazing tour ever, loved it so much. The tour was really good, elephants were amazing.
We will definitley do this tour again.
reviews.title: WONDERFULLY AMAZING, BEAUTIFUL

reviews.text: I used Khao Lak Land Discovery for several trips when I stayed in Khao Lak. I had originally booked one before I went after reading Trip Advisor reviews. I was not disappointed!!! Service was fantastic, before during and after the trip. I can recommended them highly enough to be honest
reviews.title: Solo travels

reviews.text:
I can not even explain how disappointed we were with this tour. It cost us over £300 (me, hubby, 3 kids age 12,11 and 8). We had to be collected at 4.30am to get to Phuket o we could sit in an over crowded room for over an hour while we wait for the speed boat. The snorkelling stops were lovely but rushed. There was a severe lack of refreshments provided (only water, coke and Fanta. No diet drinks). PhiPhi island beach where the boat dropped us off was  disgusting. The other side was beautiful. Why we went to the ‘party’ side I have no idea. Bamboo beach was lovely but the food provided was luke warm. Only stayed 1 hour even though we were told we would be having more. My children were all asleep on the boat on the way back (4am starts are tiring!) and the tour guide woke them up to take the plastic bangles off them. I was cross! 
It was supposed to be a full day arriving back at our hotel at 6pm. We were back by 4.30. All very rushed. Very disappointed!
reviews.title: PhiPhi islands ‘Full Day’ waste of money

reviews.text: We had done a real stroke of luck with our tour guide Volker. He has vividly brought these wonderful temples and their teachings to us with passion and dedication. This gave us a comprehensive insight that sticks in our minds. Thank you for this great day with the impressive temples and stories.
reviews.title: The Teaching of Buddha

reviews.text:
Yesterday we had  amazing tour through 3 awesome
temples in the Phang Nga region. What made the tour even more enjoyable was the hospitality of our guide  Volker. His knowledge and humor kept the tour interesting all the way .We can thoroughly recommend this tour.
Thanks Volker.
reviews.title: Awesome temple tour

reviews.text: Great day trip 9-4. Prompt pick up from our hotel, with a guide who spoke good english. There were only 6 people in our group which was goodeveryone was friendly and we made a great team white water rafting. Our cooked lunch was included and was very good and plentiful.
reviews.title: Quad bike, Zip wire and white water rafting, temple visit.

reviews.text:
This organisation is reliable and the trip was perfectly organised. Boat trip can be ‘a bit bumpy’ so in case you are seasick....

Downside is that the amount of visitors and boats coming to the island is simply too high. It was over crowded and really a shame to see that tourists do not respect nature and the environment.
reviews.title: Similan islands

reviews.text:
Cute off-road tour of the bamboo forest, with canoes through the mangroves and short temple tour.
The bamboo forest was not as spectacular as expected, but the dimensions of the plants are impressive. There was also a bird spider and a giant beetle to see, also leeches, which were bothering us (don't do this tour with open sandals!).
The canoe trip through the mangroves was also not spectacular, despite everything the landscape is very beautiful.
The temple could have been saved, since the 3-temple tour is rather recommended.
Guide Michael was very helpful and attentive, over the sometimes rough way of expression you can overlook.
reviews.title: Nice off-road tour

reviews.text: Had the tour with tour guide Volker, great! Very punctual pickup, plenty of drinks, a tour guide with a lot of knowledge, delicious food , I can only recommend!
reviews.title: Make it absolutely necessary

reviews.text:
Very interesting and informative tour.
You learn an awful lot about land to people. This with special thanks to our tour guide Volker , who is well informed and spreads good mood.
Thank you very much
Wolfgang
reviews.title: With Volker

reviews.text:
We booked after walking in off the high street although I had found Khao Lak discovery on the internet before hand. We wanted to do a couple of trips and the rep helped us decide the best way to do this. Really helpful and honest. We went on the Khao Lak safari which takes in the Tsunami memorial, turtle sanctuary, temple, waterfall, bamboo rafting and elephant bathing. That is quite a lot to do in a day! 

We had a minibus with 1 other family and a couple and our guide Arnon (I think that is right?!) was brilliant, great with the kids and gave a brilliant commentary through out the day. 

It didnt feel rushed, the bamboo rafting was probably the highlight but the elephant bathing was definitely an experience the kids will remember.

We were given ice cold water, soft drinks through out the day and lunch was lovely with a mix of food to meet everyone’s needs.

We are off to James Bond island on Monday.

Good company, great communication - highly recommended 👍
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari - super day!

reviews.text: Our guide Anne was awesome. She spoke English very well which made our tour even better. She could answer all the questions we asked. The temples were beautiful, the tsunami memorial was an eye opener, rafting was great fun, lunch was scrumptious and fresh, turtle sanctuary interesting and lastly the elephant sanctuary was amazing. My family of 4 got one on one time with 2 elephants in the bathing pool to wash them and it was an experience worth every cent. Overall we would recommend this Khao Lak Safari to everyone. Well worth the price we paid and we were looked after very well!!! Thank you Anne
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari

reviews.text:
Wanted a relaxing trip, and that is what we got. You could get off and sit on beach and have a swim, for 2hrs, which we felt too long, and you couldn,t get back onto the boat before. Taken to beach by dingy holds 10 at a time, so few crossings were mad.
My hubby and I stayed on board, and I read a book, and soaked up the sun.
My hubby has PD and the our guides Tanya and Oliver were very good, considerate, and did not try rushing him when getting on/off  the boat.
We were given lunch which was really nice.
After Lunch we moved off and cruised a long, until reached next stop, where we could snorkel or swim off the boat, it was lovely and saw some lovey fish,
reviews.title: Boat Trip

reviews.text: We booked this trip at the office in Khao Lak with family that were staying at a different hotel.  We asked for the trip on the saturday and was told pick up would be 8.00/8.10am. At 8.20 they hadn't arrived , but when i checked receipt they had put sat the 17th, not the 16th. The receptionist at hotel phoned them and I spoke to a lady who informed me trip was next day. I said we had plans for next day so would cancel and get a refund. She then said to wait by phone and she would call back. She did and said we could be picked up at 9.50 that day which we accepted. The trip was good, the guide very informative and she stopped on route to get banana fritters for us! The elephants seemed well cared for and it was a sanctuary where you feed and bathed them, no riding allowed. We stopped at a waterfall on return journey. Overall a good trip after the mix up at start.
reviews.title: Elephant bathing

reviews.text:
Well organized and assembled full day tour .Small group (8P.)
There was a lot to see.
Temple,waterfall,by inflatable boat through the mangroves,ride through the jungle as well as difficult walk through the jungle.
Great food by the lake.
Since it goes through the jungle sturdy footwear makes sense, if necessary swimming shoes for the waterfall as it is very slippery when entering.
Highlight were the bird spider and the waterfall for us.
The tour should not be underestimated,there are some difficulties to overcome in the jungle,eg the descent and ascent to the waterfall.
Many thanks to Antoine
reviews.title: Great experience through the jungle and country ,no mass tourism

reviews.text:
Though Similan Islands is now being visited by a lot of tourist annually, booking with the right tour agency can help you get the trick if you want to have an amazing adventure. I booked my trip with Khao Lak Land Discovery again, without any hesitation (first trip was Surin Islands). From the pros I've mentioned before, they still got it well maintained. From land transfer, private port, informative guides, private speed boats, and they won't even let you starve before and during your trip. Guides will even tell you when is the best time to take a "selfie" so that you will not be missing any beautiful shot.

If you're planning to go Similan/Surin Island trip, I highly recommend their team. I've seen and felt their compassion towards keeping the Islands clean and maintaining it. As well as how they support the locals in Surin which is in a bad shape because of the accident that happened. So let's not just go for a trip that will benefit us but let's choose also an agency that helps and protect what they're promoting too. Let's practice responsible tourism.
reviews.title: Best in Khao Lak!

reviews.text: Since the guided tour is not a zoo visit and you just have to be lucky to see an animal, we didn't expect much. But the guide's flair and our quiet patience was worth it. We could see a variety of wild animals up close and not too far away. Even if we hadn’t been granted this, the place with its small mountains and the somehow ghostly reservoir is a wonderful experience. By the way, the lake is quite clear and invites you to swim. We had two great days with very good food and an exciting night, so in the stock darkness in a bamboo hut. Highly recommended!
reviews.title: An unforgettable experience!

reviews.text: You wont disappointed at all from khaolak land Discovery great servcie on time pick up and delivery thank you guys.
reviews.title: Great servcie

reviews.text: Our guide Volker was very interesting and informative and made sure our small group of 8 people (Two families of 4 - English & German) could understand and took time to get to know us all during the full day. Our favourite parts were sea kayaking, visiting James Bond Island, although this is full of tourists. The Gypsy floating village was also very interesting to see how these people live their lives. We ate lunch at the restaurant there and it was really lovely food. On the way back we visited a Thai temple and a wild monkey park. We also had a great and safe driver that day who saved us from what could have been a terrible accident. Thank you to him and to Volker for such a memorable day
reviews.title: Full day out to James Bond Island

reviews.text:
We were a group of 3 (my husband, brother-in-law and myself) and we opted for Dolpin Cruise for a day as this was the only boat trip they would take me given I am pregnant. 
The boat was ok but there were way too many people (like 40). The issues were:

1. Complete lack of safety sense from the crew including the local guide - we had to take a speed boat to reach the first beach, in groups of 10 - 12 pax; we were the 2nd or 3rd round and thought it should be all good for just a less than 5-min ride from main boat to the beach. the boatman accidentally dropped one of the lifejackets in the choppier side of the water, and was speeding up to pickup the lifejacket - water started going into the boat and was reaching everyone's knee's level and the boat was beginning to sink. That's when the boatman realised they had to turn back to head to the beach (calmer side of waters). Everyone was fine but there was a scary moment -- what annoyed me was that none of the guides / crew seemed to care and they were just brushing it off as if nothing has happened. No checking in nor explanation - except the German guide Sabrina who did care to ask if I was ok. 

2. No briefing on surrounding / potential hazards / watchouts - snorkeling was cancelled due to weather (water was too choppy), which was understandable - yet they never managed customers' expectations nor was clear on what to expect. They headed to an area for the group to swim in the waters, the local guide Tania kept asking the group to jump into the waters (and taking videos) but after 15 - 20 mins, everyone rushed back up to the boat as a lot got stung by small jellyfish!

3. Horrible contingency plan - given no one wanted to swim in the waters with the sting and there was still time left before end of tour, they decided to offer fishing to the group, which in the end only 2 out of the whole group of 40 was interested so as you could imagine it was the whole boat waiting / watching just the 2 people fishing, nothing else was done to manage the awkward situation. 

The most annoying part was, there was zero acknowledgement on what happened nor any apologies on the bad management etc even there was serious safety issue. 
It was only when I called out the issue on their feedback form that the German guide offered to talk, which I appreciated but the local guide Tania was simply brushing the whole thing off as if nothing has happened - and interesting enough she even blamed me for not telling her I was pregnant (as if they would offer better care?), when their customer service was the one who mentioned this was the only boat trip I could get on with my pregnancy!

Yet even after raising all these concerns, there was no offer on compensation - so we literally paid approx. USD 70/80+ per pax to get on this ridiculous boat and got stuck for the whole day. Total rip off with no safety sense - DO NOT JOIN THEIR TOURS!!!
reviews.title: Total rip off and lack of safety awareness

reviews.text:
2 days, 1 night...
Very pleasant excursion especially thanks to our guide: Rolo, a German guide who speaks perfect English.
Really rudimentary accommodation but allowing to see really wild animals.
Be sure to negotiate the price, because 7500 bats is a bit expensive for the whole (they really win on housing and meals!).
reviews.title: Shrine of Khao sok

reviews.text: Apart from perhaps the somewhat rustic comfort of the boat and banbou huts, we spent two wonderful days in this sanctuary reserved for a privileged few (the southern part of the lake is open to mass tourism). Many animals including wild elephants, beautiful landscapes and above all an exceptional guide because really passionate (Rollo). He is German and speaks English (and Thai) but we never regretted that he does not speak French!
reviews.title: Beautiful

reviews.text:
The 3Temple Tour with Volker/Vodka was very well organized, booked spontaneously the night before...yummy lunch with nice views always cold drinks, the driver was also great!
During the trip he introduced us to the culture and his personal impressions.... breathtaking scenery, the temples of madness and then there is the good knowledge of the guide....we were a small group of 4 women of different ages, so the tour was even more personal!we could ask him questions and he always had an answer ready!
Super successful day
reviews.title: 3 Temple Tour with Vodka

reviews.text:
We went on a temple tour again after four years, and it was absolutely worth it! Since the Monkey Temple is no longer in the program (very understandable due to the health hazard of partly aggressive animals) we were presented with a partly new route.
The absolute main prize was our goal guide Volker, so competent and understandable we have probably never gotten a wealth of interesting information and also personal experiences with this great country - thank you very much!  
The Service was perfect and even the small group size (9 people) is a big plus for this organizer.
reviews.title: Also perfect the second time!

reviews.text:
We booked three trips with this company and all were fantastic. 
Khao lak safari, similan islands and Hong island great time and all three tour guides were very professional and informative. 
Definitely recommend this company for your trips
reviews.title: Professional service

reviews.text: We booked 3 trips with this company and all were fantastic. Well organised and great guides. One trip a must Hong Island.
reviews.title: Fantastic Trips

reviews.text:
Fantastic day! Really well organised from start to finish and the snorkelling at the Surin Islands was amazing. The two guides, Hugh and Tanya were very knowledgable, professional and fun too. Everything was included in the price so we didn’t have to pay any extra throughout the day which was easy. 

I couldn’t recommend this trip enough!
reviews.title: Surin Islands Day Trip

reviews.text:
Thanks to Rolo, our tour guide, and Nong, our boatman, we had a wonderful tour.
Just four participants went to Khao Sok and on to the Klong Sang wildlife sanctuary. In addition to plenty of time to watch animals, there was also room for bathing or even exploring the lake by canoe.
The accommodations were basic but still had electricity and Wi-Fi, which I couldn't believe given the remoteness... Also the sanitary facilities are perfectly fine and the food was always very delicious and plentiful.
All in all, a great trip that I can highly recommend to nature lovers!

P. S.: Don't forget the telephoto lens :)
reviews.title: Impressive experiences top organized

reviews.text: Half a day elephant trekking experience with our guide Dido who was very knowledgeable, entertaining and he ensured we had a five star experience. We also enjoyed a tasty lunch after visiting a waterfall in Khaolak.
reviews.title: Elephant Trekking

reviews.text:
I booked a couple of day trips and transfers. The drivers and cars are fantastic - private car even had WIFI.
The day cruise along the coast of Khao Lak was relaxed and nice, good food and nice guides. The Dolphin Cruise has been recently done up and offered plenty of space.
The land day trip was mixed. Bamboo rafting was a hoot and the turtle Conservation Center, temple and Tsunami memorial informative. BUT the elephant bath was a tourist attraction and not a welfare centre as I had thought. I wish I had informed myself better - I would definitely not have gone to that one and perhaps donated that money to a real elephant sanctuary or charity instead.
reviews.title: Great cruise, avoid the elephant bath

reviews.text:
Fantastic day with guide Dido and driver. Travelled by jeep to trek up to a beautiful waterfall. Have to be pretty mobile to climb up, well worth it to see the sights and wildlife. 
        Took a bamboo raft down the river which was amazing! Followed by a visit to a 500 year old tree, and temples. Spent time at the boat memorial site for the tsunami, very sobering, and interesting to see. 
         A lovely visit to see turtles at the sanctuary. A delicious lunch feast, followed by a visit to a lovely beach. We loved our day from start to finish. Made all the more enjoyable by the guides good grasp of English, local knowledge and fun humour. Recommend :)
reviews.title: Jeep safari

reviews.text: Very comfortable boat, wonderful crew, delicious food, beautiful way to see the country, spent time at the singing beach with white sand, opportunity to snorkel and fish.
reviews.title: Lovely tour

reviews.text: A really great trip with an informative insight to Thailand’s main religion. Jenny our guide was very down to earth and, as a practicing Buddhist, extremely knowledgeable. The 3 temples were extremely different, yet each one amazing in its own way. Lunch was in a lovely restaurant overlooking the river and feeding the monkeys was delightful. I would definitely recommend this trip, especially if Jenny is your guide.
reviews.title: 3 Temple Tour

reviews.text:
3 days of lake and jungle tour with Phuketastic have left unforgettable impressions. "Rolo", our German-speaking guide, took care of us 2 "older girls" charmingly and humorously. 
Recommendations have already been made.
GlG Jutta
reviews.title: Perfect organization, fascinating impressions

reviews.text:
Great tour guide
Small group 
Well positioned with drinks 
Even with regard to ashtrays 
More tours undertaken with them 
specified period is not correct, but had no other choice
reviews.title: Super

reviews.text:
We booked the Elephant Bath and Trek, Surin Island Snorkel and The Hong Island tours. 
Our 2 boys 9 and 11 loved the Surin Island Snorkel the most. It was a short drive from our hotel (The Mai at Khaolak) to the pier then a 1.5 hr boat ride to the 1st stop.  I would pack some reef shoes, as you leave your thongs at the pier, my son cut his foot at the lunch stop on the beach. The water was amazing, so warm and clear, we saw Nemo and Dory and so many more 
The Hong Island was something I've always wanted to do  canoeing under/ through caves to come out in an amazing lagoon, just incredible. 
The elephant trek was surprisingly our least fave, it was great, just trumped by the others. 
All tour guides were awesome and all the lunches on the tours were delicious!
Couple of tips! If you suffer from motion sickness I would take medication as a precaution for any of these tours, theres some windy roads (especially if you're stuck on the back of the bus) and it would be a shame to have an otherwise awesome day  ruined by feeling yuk. 
Take smaller notes to give tips (totally optional) to your guides, we only had a 1000 BHT on us for the Hong, we would've liked to have tipped our guide, but perhaps not that much!
reviews.title: 3 Tours booked

reviews.text: Our guide, Jenny, was such a trooper. She came along with us via Khao Lak Land Discovery as part of a Southeast Asia Contiki tour I was on this month. She was so knowledgeable and friendly! With Jenny along for the ride, we were able to have quite an adventure through Khao Sok National Park and at Cheow Larn Lake. We had door to door service from the train station to all the sites visited and onto our next destinations. We made some great memories and learned a lot!
reviews.title: Amazing Local Guided Experiences

reviews.text:
From the moment we were picked up, we felt very at ease. The tour guide Kim was absolutely amazing. We had a small group of 4 and Kim was speaking between English, French and Thai all day. Her English was exceptional, very fluent. 
We were collected at our hotel right on time at 8.15. Upon pick up Kim explained the activities of the day and the itinerary so we knew exactly what to expect. She was extremely knowledgeable about the Thai history and landmarks as we made our way into Khao Sok. First stop was the morning markets. Was definitely an experience. So much fresh seafood and fruit/veg. Lots of items we couldn’t even identify! Was a short visit, about 20 minutes, but it was all we needed to look through the food stalls. Upon returning to the bus we were offered cold water from the esky which was much appreciated. Kim has purchased some unusual food items at the market for us to try. We were then given the opportunity to try local fruit, deserts and the unusual century egg in the bus that Kim has selected. Before long we arrived at the viewpoint. It was a lovely sight, great for photos. Once again Kim explained about the history of the formation of the landscape and the surrounding flora and fauna pointing out some unusual plants to us. Then we were off again to Khao Sok. We arrived a short time later at the river for our canoeing experience. We were provided with the essentials to keep us dry with raincoats and a waterproof bag. Once again Kim explained everything and gave us tips for along the way. Our canoeing guide was amazing. He pointed out a lot of wildlife and took the time to stop and show us. We saw snakes, monkeys, fish, frogs, and birds along the way. It really was a great experience. The highlight of the trip. We stopped half way down the river for coffee which was supplied in a bamboo cup and boiled through bamboo. At the end of the canoe trip we were given an opportunity to purchase photographs however this was not pushed on us. Photos were 250 baht. Once again we were offered cold drinks and refresher towels. We then went to the treehouse resort where we were spoilt with an array of Thai cuisine for lunch. The food was fresh and very tasty. A bottle of soft drink was also provided. After lunch we were off to Win Park elephant conservation park for our elephant ride. We stopped off at the monkey park on the way and saw lots of monkeys playing. They were quite tame and came up to us looking for food. We didn’t feed them because we didn’t want to be jumped on! Lots of good photo opportunities. On the way Kim gave us a lot of valuable information regarding the elephant laws in Thailand. It was really interesting. Hot tip - even if it is sunny, take a raincoat on the trek! We should have listened to Kim because about halfway through our trek it started pouring rain! It didn’t seem to worry anyone though because we were all having such a great time. The trek took about 45 mins and we went through small streams, jungle and rocky areas. The elephants seemed really well cared for and were very well trained. After the trek, we were given the opportunity to feed our elephant some fruit for 50 baht. Once again our driver offered us cold drinks and tea and coffee after the trek. Then it was time to head back to Khao Lak. Kim gave us more information about things like the tsunami, Thai police, driving laws, Thai trades etc. She was so knowledgeable! The driver stopped off for a few moments so we could see the tsunami mass cemetery. We were back to our hotel by 4pm. All in all we had an excellent experience which we really think was largely due to Kim. She was absolutely amazing. Our driver was very friendly too. Was a long day but it didn’t feel as though it dragged at all. We had so much fun and enjoyed some experiences we would never get anywhere else. One thing I would suggest is to make sure you bring a waterproof bag, bug spray and sunscreen. And a camera for lots of photos!! We had a good time going through all our happy snaps when we got home. Definitely recommended!! Thanks Kim!
reviews.title: Khao Sok day trip

reviews.text: It is another site to enjoy pure nature while you are at phuket. The best in it was the bamboo raft in the jungle. And swimming in the  cold waterfall. It deserves to do it.
reviews.title: Beautiful nature

reviews.text: We went on two tours with Khao lak land Discovery and could not fault them, visits to James Bond island, temples, elephant park, tsunami memorial, floating gypsy village and more.  our tour guides Volker and Kim were the best guides I've had, they both had a huge amount of knowledge about all the places visited and were able to answer all the questions put to them about Thailand. When I tour next I will definitely use them again.
reviews.title: Khao Lak land discovery brilliant tours

reviews.text: This trip was a lot of fun great food and staff were so friendly and happy to assist where ever they could hotel pick up was prompt
reviews.title: Great day out

reviews.text: We booked four adventures with Land Discovery and we had a very exciting trips. Our guide, Pang (we had her for all our trips) was so wonderful, spoke four languages and was very knowledgeable about the trips, Thailand's history, geography and traditions.We were very happy with the service by Land Discovery. Thank you!
reviews.title: Excellent Service and Guides!

reviews.text: Great organized tour very friendly people everything do on time picking and doping i had day trip to phi phi i really liked it.
reviews.title: great service

reviews.text: We had a great day ( even in the rain ) with our guide Tania and driver Dick, very well organised tour,with many happy memories made.
reviews.title: Day tour to Khao Sok

reviews.text: It was a good trip for us and a lot to do with this attraction, all to all that my family enjoyed the trip and every one was very happy and satisfied with this excurson.
reviews.title: Good Trip

reviews.text:
We booked 3 excursions in total with Khao Lak land discovery, the first was the Khao Lak day trip, where you bathed elephants, visited the turtle sanctuary, participated in bamboo rafting and saw the tsunami memorial.  Our guide was dido, he was good organising everything, we loved seeing and feeding the elephants, our elephant was a bit feisty, so we didn’t linger bathing him as he seemed to be fed up, my children loved stroking him and feeding him.  The photos they sell you are very reasonable in price.  The turtle sanctuary was interesting and lets you see all ages of turtles close up. The bamboo rafting was a great experience we loved it, the guys that paddle you down the river do a fantastic job and really deserve a tip, we also bought the photos, as again they are really cheap and support the locals doing the rafting. 
The second excursion we did, was the temple tour, our guide was Gift and she was just lovely, a practising Buddhist herself, she was so knowledgeable about all the temples and gave us a great insight into Buddhism.  We visiting 3 amazing temples, the first inside a cave, my boys loved this as you had to climb lots of stairs to access it, then you could explore inside and there were some fantastic viewpoints over the surrounding jungle.  The 2nd was very grand and we actually met a monk, which was amazing. The 3rd was equally as interesting with a massive monk statue, which was an amazing sight.  All of this was made better by gift, who spoke good English and explained so much to us about all aspects of the culture and religion, she is the perfect guide for this excursion.  She is a real asset to the company. The 3rd trip we did was to James Bond Island, again another fantastic highly recommended trip. Including a long tail boat to James Bond island, sea kayaking around caves and visiting the floating gypsy village, which ins a great experience.  We had Gift again as our guide. She is so friendly and has a great rapport with all the locals that she deals with.  All excursions were in minibuses with good air con and helpful drivers. The lunches they provide are all to authentic Thai restaurants in great locations and the food is lovely.  Thanks for 3 wonderful trips, great company.
reviews.title: 3 great excursions with Khao Lak discovery

reviews.text: We had a fantastic experience on our Khao Lak Land Discovery tour, we ended up having a private tour due to others pulling out because of the weather. This was great as we could do things out our own speed. Great overall experience
reviews.title: Fantastic Full Day Experience

reviews.text: Great day out, great tour guides, lots to see an do, highly recommend to all as a must do in Thailand! From Phuket the drive is quite long to Khao Lak -2 hours either way, but you do get to see a lot more of the country an some nice spots that are less none about.
reviews.title: Good relaxing day out

reviews.text:
A long day ,but with the inclement weather, the THB 1000 for each of us it was a great fill in.
Worst part of it is that Khoa Lak is a 2 hour drive each way from the resort to Phuket area. The Big Budha is still not complete,had been here about 5 years ago ,  it's getting there but a long ,long way to go.
The trip started at around 12.00 , Budha  first then to the markets for their 16.00 opening, some stalls  were a little late opening but we had 4 hours to kill, shopping eating and drinking
Shopped for what we wanted for about 2 hours then some cocktail at The Waiting Bar, back to more bargaining then dinner at one of the seafood restaurants. Pineapple rice and some prawns, drinks,(did not get name of eatery) but most of them are on the right hand side of the markets.
Some more shopping, back to the bus by 19.30 and on the road by 20.00. Made it back to the resort buy 22.15 an exhausting  but an o/k day
reviews.title: Half day trip ,Big Budha,Naka weekend night markets

reviews.text:
Five of us booked a tour from Khao Lak for the day and it was the highlight of our visit.
We bathed elephants, saw the turtle rehabilitation, went bamboo rafting, swimming in river and beautiful waterfall and a visit to the tsunami memorial. 
Our guide Tanya was great even taking photos for us while we were with the elephants and showing us a baby one....
Highly recommend to everyone
reviews.title: Fantastic tour

reviews.text: Super Service, always in time, good price, super cars, super drivers. It is very easy to book over the homepage
reviews.title: Best Service you can imagine

reviews.text: good services of tours and they also have guides who can speak many languages whcih was good and important for us. prices are little higher than around but worth it. thanks karan for sending us here and to the team discovery for the adventure we had a great time
reviews.title: good trips and language

reviews.text: If you are looking for a day excursion from Khao Lak, and want to experience the colour and fun of snorkeling on coral reefs, then I highly recommend this trip offered by Khao Lak Land Discovery. Their bus picked us up from the JW Marriott in Khao Lak at 7.30am and we were at the dock at Baan Nam Kem by 8.15. After a briefing and making payment (please note cash and credit cards are accepted - debit cards not accepted), at 8.50 we boarded the high speed boat for the voyage to our first dive spot at the Surin Islands. This took just over one hour, but can take longer if weather conditions are not as favourable. This is to be honest, a boring trip, with not much to see and the noise of the three outboard motors making conversation or even music listening very difficult. However as you plunge into the crystal clear waters and catch your first glimpse of the colourful reef fish, all is forgiven (if not forgotten). The water clarity and temperature were great, but the actual coral was mainly brown and dead, with only the occasional more colourful species to be seen. But the fish were stunningly beautiful and plentiful and 45 minutes here passed quickly. The Village that is home to the Mokan people was our next destination. It was interesting and very picturesque, but I felt a little sorry for the people, who seemed to have little to do but offer hand made trinkets for sale to the tourists. A short trip to another island and we were at our lunch destination in a national park. The buffet lunch was good and we were given an hour to swim and look around this beautiful place. Other tour groups arrived too. We were called back onto our boat at 13.50, and by 14.10 we were back in the water for another snorkelling session. This reef had less fish but better and more colourful, healthy coral. Back onto the boat by 15.00 before we set course for our home port which we reached around 16.00. The day was well organized and very enjoyable. The tour guides spoke English, French and German and addressed us as three separate groups. Thanks to Tania, our Thai, English -speaking guide.
reviews.title: A beautiful day at the Surin Islands

reviews.text: We participated in the Khao lak full day safari on the 3rd of May. Our tour started with a very sweet girl called 'Gift' and her driver picking us up at the front of the hotel. She told us we would go visit the tsunami memorial first because it was an important part for the history of Khao lak. After that we went to a temple where we participated in a Buddhist ceremony. Next we went tot the bamboo rafting, we first enjoyed a self made coffee before heading onto the river. On the raft we were shown multiple things like a snake, a frog and a place where we could go for a swim. After the bamboo rafting we headed to the elephants, here we received a delicious meal while we could see the elephants passing by. After the meal it was our turn to bathe with the elephants. The elephant keeper took a lot of great pictures of us while we were washing the elephant. The elephant bathing was followed up by a visit to the turtle conservation centre where they raise turtles so they can release them in the sea again and preserve their existence. Last but not least we finished this tour with a relaxing visit to a waterfall. In short, this tour was a full day of great activities. We would like to thank the tour guide 'gift' one more time for this amazing day and we will certainly book more tours with Khao Lak land discovery in the future.
reviews.title: Khao lak full day safari

reviews.text: Went on the Dolphin Cruise - was an amazing adventure. Thank you to our guide Gift who was amazing and all the staff on the cruise, they were all so friendly and made our day great. We were picked up from our hotel and taken to the harbor where we boarded the cruise. We were then taken to a beautiful beach (had to get in the tender boat and then a 10 min walk) but was worth it, then were were back on the boat, lovely lunch was served, and then we went snorkeling, and fishing. Was a amazing day out, well worth it.
reviews.title: Dolphin Cruise - Must do.

reviews.text: Khoa lak was a hoot and just a slow down we needed .sandy beaches, the small  little restaurants touring around on scooters just great ,booking now for new year  love the enviroment
reviews.title: would go back tomorrow

reviews.text: Sam was absolutely amazing an abundance of knowledge on all places that we visited, the tsunami, turtles, elephants, temples! He couldn’t have made our day any more interesting and exciting! Thank you Sam. A big shout out the our driver also Mr. Kodet, driving 2 hours to Phuket to pick us up and 2 hours on the way home. It is definitely a big jam packed action day but an amazing day at that.
reviews.title: Wealth of knowledge.

reviews.text:
The staff were amazing in particular our guide , Mint, very knowledgeable . True picture postcard scenes white sandy beaches and turquoise sea. Came across a sea turtle while snorkeling 

But the lunch lodgistics were horrendous hundreds of people were crammed onto one small space no where to sit , toilets were disgusting , tour operators would be far better giving their clients packed lunches to enjoy on the comfort of the boats
reviews.title: Similan islands day tour

reviews.text: Initial communication was good and quick when inquiring about the 3 day trip, however I should have asked more questions in advance as the website did not provide information that in retrospect, I should have known about. When independently researching the Rock and Tree house, I found detailed information on the internet as well as their website, however there was nothing about the raft house - no images or name provided. I should have asked the company directly as the raft house used by Khao Lak Land Discovery was a 2.5hr longtail boat ride to a very basic raft house with no electricity (unlike others I had seen online at the same location and to the lovely raft houses we went to for lunch) - something we only found out the night before this part of the trip because we asked our guide to go through the itinerary for the next day. With two young children, we wished we had known this before, no information was available on the the company website to inform us of this so we assumed it would be a similar standard to the tree house - which was superb. Having decided that we could not stay somewhere with no electricity and sleeping on the floor in accommodation separated from each other which unsettled the children, our guide, Tanya, was fantastic. Nothing was too much trouble to reschedule our itinerary and book an extra night at the tree house. (At additional costs to us). Tanya had lots of ideas of things we could do in Kho Sok National park and was only too happy to take us the turtle sanctuary and Poseidon Beach on the way back to Khao Lak. She was incredibly flexible, enthusiastic, energetic and great with the children. A very expensive trip and not as we had anticipated but lots of memorable experiences enjoyed by all and the most was made of the situation.
reviews.title: 3 day trip with stay in a tree house and raft house

reviews.text: This is a mixed review.  For what it was, this was not cheap.  2500 baht for each adult (£62) and 1800 baht (£45) for a 5 to 12 year old, for essentially just over half a day of boat ride. There was no safety briefing, what to do in an emergency etc, you normally get these on any tour in any country. There was not an explanation of what was going on when we stopped to disembark for the beach stop. The guide did not wait for the final 2 boat loads of tourists and unfortunately about 10-15  of us started walking in the wrong direction. Fortunately one of the group (including several young children) decided to turn around after about 10 minutes otherwise the problem could have been worse. It seemed the guides did not have a good understanding of where all the members of the group were at a given time. The beach itself was very nice. It was deserted, lovely clean sand, the sea clean and warm. The shaded area was very run down and untidy. If this is a regular stop then some effort should be made to smarten it up. The boat itself was very nice, the crew very helpful and seemed competent. Everybody enjoyed the lunch, it was nice, drinks to purchase were reasonable, the fishing and snorkelling was enjoyed. In summary, duty of care needs to be taken seriously (we put ourselves in your hands), health and safety needs to be stepped up, needs a bit more visible coordination and control, positives as mentioned above, boat, crew, food, beach and snorkelling.
reviews.title: Family day trip

reviews.text: Staff are great from the center staff to the dive guides booked multipal dives all were good qaulity and very enjoyable :)
reviews.title: Well taken care of by the company
