reviews.text: We found this Dolphin Sunset Cruise online through Khao Lak Land Discovery after our hotel in Khao Lak told us we would have to go to Phuket for a sunset cruise. This cruise was one of the best things we did during our Thailand stay. The crew were great, they took as many photos of us as we wanted in the beautiful sunset. We were able to buy cocktails on board which was a plus for us! Dinner was delicious and they ended the night with a lovely surprise which just made the whole experience just that much better. Couldn't recommend this tour more highly! A must do if you're staying in Khao Lak!!
reviews.title: Dolphin Sunset Cruise- AMAZING

reviews.text: We took the dolphin Cruise for full day and the sunset dolphin Cruise. The team was really great and so kind! With a 3 years old boy it was great. It is calm and totally different than the others with speed boat! Fishing on the boat was really great! The team was wonderful. Good souvenir.
reviews.title: Dolphin Cruise and sunset.

reviews.text:
While staying at the Mai Khaolak  we booked two tours from Khao Lak Land Discovery: "James Bond Island" tour and the  Introduction to Khao Lak". 

We were collected for both tours in a timely manner in very nice tour vans. Our guides were Sam and Dennis, and both were knowledgeable, personable and nothing was too much trouble for them. 

Our "intro" tour included bathing and feeding elephants at an ethical centre, bamboo rafting through the jungle and a visit to a temple.  A long, satisfying and well paced day. 

The "James Bond Island" tour was mostly water-borne with a glorious ride by longboat to the island and beyond, early enough that we could see the place without many tourists in the way.  The visit to the Muslim sea gypsy village was respectful and interesting. Dennis our guide was great company and cared a lot for Thailand and our pleasure. 

Very highly recommended for visitors to Khao Lak.
reviews.title: Well chosen tours,  great facilities and brilliant guides make these trips magical

reviews.text:
We were staying at Beyond resort 10 minutes north of Khao Lak. Given the great sunsets from this location we thought it would be good to have dinner on a boat and watch the sunset. Our resort could only offer us a dinner cruise out of a marina by phuket some 90 minutes drive away. We didnt want to go that far for a cruise. A bit of googling turned up Khao Lak Land Discovery Dinner Cruise. They have a sunset cruise on a fully renovated boat for $100 per person and just 20 minute drive to the marina from Khao Lak. We booked this through the internet for 4 people. Pickup from the hotel was at 4pm and return by 8:30pm. We had no idea if this cruise was good or bad as there were no reviews for it online and no photos of the boat. Let me say this cruise was the one of the best nights out we had in Khao Lak. 
Once we got to the Marina our tour guide on the boat was Jero. Jero has perfect english, very knowledgeable of the local area and current affairs, probably the best tour guide I have ever had on any tour! Jero told us we were the only people on the cruise, this just gets better! When we saw the boat it was huge just for the 4 of us. The boat has 2 levels, open deck upstairs and covered downstairs. You can take some wine on the cruise if you like or there is a cash bar on board. We cruised out of the Marina at a leisurely pace enjoying the scenery, some nibbles on board, relaxing music and a cocktail in hand. After about 45 minutes we dropped anchor for a spot of fishing, this is optional and you don't have to fish. It was entertaining watching the crew fish from the deck upstairs. We then moved further off the coast to watch the sunset around 6:15 pm. As we had the boat to ourselves were were spoilt for choice of where to sit. While we were enjoying the sunset the crew had a prepared a lovely dinner downstairs of some wonderful Thai food. Once dinner was finished Jero asked us to move upstairs for a surprise. We took our seats and then the fireworks began, right of the top of the boat... a perfect way to end the cruise. On the journey back to port we enjoyed gazing at the starry night away from the light pollution and we even saw the space station fly over head!
The whole cruise end to end was perfect. Jero did a fantastic job as guide, the girl behind the bar with the great cocktails and all the other crew that ensured we had a wonderful and safe cruise.
I would highly recommend this cruise. I believe it has only just started so book it quick before it gets too popular!!
reviews.title: Amazing Sunset Dinner Cruise.... Unmissable

reviews.text:
We booked our tours while in Thailand through Khao Lak Land Discovery and found them to be very professional and easy to deal with. Both of the tours had them picking us up from our hotel, they arrived promptly and on time and had great tour guides who were able to speak a variety of languages.
We went on the Hong Island tour - an amazing day with an early start (0430) the boat ride was interesting and the kayaking through the caves simply breathtaking, and the 3 Temples Tour - our tour guide Jenny was organised, friendly and very knowledgeable about the various temples we visited. Lunch was provided both days, plenty of drinks and modern vehicles were used. I can recommend this tour service. Our only problem was an unscheduled stop on the Hong Island tour to one of the Temples which we visited the next day!!This I believe was the guide trying to fill the day out which then impacted on the tour we were taking the following day. The day had been long enough and didn’t need extending by this detour.
reviews.title: Tours of choice

reviews.text:
We (4 adults) booked a day trip to the Surin islands.  We were picked up just after 7AM at our Khao Lak hotel.  We were first on our minibus and after 2 more pickups we arrived at the port at 7:45.   A light breakfast was available then a safety briefing before we boarded our speed boat.   Our boat was registered for 50 people but there were only about 30 of us on the trip.  The journey out took 1.5 hours and we covered almost 50 miles.  Although the sea was flat calm it was a bit bumpy and a few people were quite pleased when we arrived at our first stop.
 The first stop was for snorkelling (all equipment provided) over a reef on the south island. It was great with plenty of  healthy reef, fish and a few octopus.  The water was crystal clear and warm.
When we returned to the boat after 45 minutes or so the guide suggested that he would like to change the itinerary.  He explained that if we went to the main island then, we would be the first boat to arrive.  We arrived there at 11AM. We were again given a briefing and had 2.5 hours to explore and have lunch.  The beach was one of the most beautiful I have ever seen.
At the end of our time there we again boarded our boat and set off visit the  Morken Sea Gypsy village spending about 30 minutes there. The guide gave us lots of interesting information about the people and their history.
Our last stop was another 45 minute snorkelling stop over another reef.
When we started our return journey it was obvious that the wind was much stronger that on our journey out and some of the waves had started to have white crests.   The journey was very bumpy and a few people were ill.  We arrived back safely just over 90 minutes later.
The busses were waiting for us on our return and we were soon back at our hotel.
The 2 guides on the trip were excellent always keeping an eye out for us and supplying information when needed or asked for.  An excellent day out.
reviews.title: A great trip to the Surin Islands

reviews.text:
We just got back from an amazing day with Volker, our tour guide! There were 10 on the tour in total which didn’t feel too many at any stage and the tour was given in English and German to accommodate everyone’s needs. 

We firstly visited the turtle sanctuary- one of the highlights of the day - getting to see turtles at different stages of development (a few weeks to a few months) before they’re released to the sea. Also some very large older turtles who were there for treatment! Next we went to a small waterfall which was very pleasant. 

Then we went to the highlight (for me) which was the river raft. This was so relaxing and completely different to anything we’d done before. Definitely opt in to swim as it’s great fun and there’s a swing you can use to throw yourself into the water with. The main guy took photos from the bank and sold them later for 300bht, which we thought was very reasonable as a ‘thank you’ for a great journey. One of the pairs even asked to captain the raft which was fun to watch!

Next we went to the temple where Volker’s incredible knowledge of Thailand and Buddhism shone! It was truly enlightening!! 

Then we went to the elephant bathing which was my partner’s favourite part of the day. This was great fun and it was incredible being so close to such majestic animals. We were keen not to ride them as we weren’t sure on how humanely they’re kept. In the back of my mind I still question how ethical it all is but Volker said they use elephants in the wood trade for moving heavy timber... so I suppose without the tourists the elephants would be doing that - it’s the lesser of two evils for sure! Again, they take photos for you to purchase for 250bht and you can buy bananas for 100bht to feed the elephants after. 

Lunch at the elephant centre was great (if not a little late in the day!) although the taxi driver and Volker were treated to more authentic Thai food whilst the rest of us enjoyed very tasty non-spicy delights. I would’ve loved the opportunity to have requested the authentic food as my partner and I both feel we’ve not really been exposed to true Thai food whilst on holiday here (our friend is Thai so we’re used to some pretty spicy food from her!!). 

Finally we went to the Tsunami memorial. This was a poignant visit as it highlighted the devastation from 2014. 

The tour was excellently organised (we even received a note from our hotel advising us the pickup time had changed, even though it was only by 5minutes!!!!). We left our hotel around 8am, returning at 5pm. 

The office staff are super helpful - we booked it online and altered the date after everything was confirmed without any difficulties. Our questions were replied to very quickly by email which was very reassuring. 

All in all this trip had the perfect itinerary. We got to do lots of different activities whilst only using up one of our days in Thailand!! Recommended to all ages!
reviews.title: Khao Lak Safari review

reviews.text:
We had Rollo as a German guide and you can only recommend!
It was a great, exciting and experiential tour.
reviews.title: Jeep-Tour

reviews.text:
I booked transfers from Australia. Eike looked after us. Very happy with the drivers. All of their drivers were prompt and safe. Eike also helped me with bookings to the Similans.
Would certainly use them again if we are ever in Khaolak.
reviews.title: Transfers

reviews.text:
We went on the Khao Lak Safari from the Haven Hotel on the 15th January 2018.
We had a brilliant day and have to say it was one of the best trips we have ever been on. Our guide was Kim.  What an amazing young lady she is.  She was very knowledgeable and obviously passionate about nature and her country.  She spoke very well and explained everything perfectly. There was enough time at each activity to enjoy yourselves and not get bored. We also had an amazing lunch.
Would definitely use this company again.
reviews.title: Brilliant Tour and Guide

reviews.text:
So we decided to book one trip to go wash, bathe with the elephants, lunch included and visit to a waterfall.
We had the lovely Dido as our guide, a warm friendly guy, who made it all fun! The Elephant Farm was over an hour away, we were taken with a small group of nice people on a minibus there. Of course I didn’t realise we would be properly trekking on elephants for a good length of time, and when our elephant went up and down, I had to breathe as I felt I would fall off, but it was something that was amazing and petrifying at the same time! Once that was over we had the chance to wash them and bathe with them, it was awesome ! They are wonderful animals, and then we were given a nice lunch at the farm too, at which Dido announced that as was his birthday today he was taking us to see a baby elephant of one week old! He was so sweet! Then we stopped off at the waterfall which was not as exciting for us as the elephants, but very nice! We had the most amazing day and very grateful to Dido for making it such a special memory for us. A great end to a wonderful holiday , thanks and thoroughly recommend this trip!
reviews.title: Had an Amazing Day with the Elephants! Thoroughly recommended!

reviews.text:
We had a fantastic stay of one night on the water bungalows and one night at the rock and treehouse resort. Our guide, Sam and our driver Toy were excellent for the entire thing! Our pickup was the only hiccup as it was supposed to be at 745am and we luckily woke up at 7 and I checked my email and they wrote to me at 6am that morning our pickup was going to be at 7! My husband ran down and told the driver we got the email last second and luckily they were kind while we quickly got ready and left. Our group was small with 4 people which was really cool they were all very nice. The lake is beautiful and pictures do not do it justice. The 2 hour long tail boat ride was cool just hurt sitting on our butts that long! We saw so many things on our safari elephant, lizards, king fishers, wild chicken & deer (hilarious to us the guides were SO excited to see deer when where we live we almost hit them constantly with our car) boars, turtle, pigs, monkeys and toucans. Not much to see on the night safari but being in the water in the deep jungle at night with a full moon was just gorgeous and such a cool experience. The water bungalows are BASIC. For one night we didn’t mind and it’s the trade off for being so deep in the jungle and being able to see all the wildlife you can. It’s a little hut size of a large closet with two small mattresses (more like cushions in width) a mosquito net and to my surprise an outlet and small outdoor light. Bring earplugs for the insects they’re quite loud but again the lodging is just fine for one night. The bathrooms are the best they can do up the hill on a somewhat rickidy makeshift dock that you need to dump the water in to flush yourself but again, you’re two hours into the deep jungle you can’t expect or demand more than that. The food was plenty and delicious and always more than what we could finish. Lots of bottle water always readily available. Definitely bring your swim suit as the lake is warm and clear and so clean and we could’ve stayed in it for hours! Kayaking is nice as well. Second day jungle river hike was cool they give you ‘socks’ to wear as you go through some water with your shoes (bring ones you don’t mind getting wet and light in color to see the leeches to pull them off) and there are leeches in the water and they stop the leeches from getting on you. One little guy still made it on me but now I have a cool story no biggie! We stayed in the rock and treehouse resort our second night and that was so beautiful! All the amenities you could ask for and the food was delicious best green curry I’ve had! The kayak ride the next day was so stunning the water cool and clear (and leech free!) and we got so close to some adorable monkeys. The elephant ride was cool but after we felt kinda bad for her and thought about her having to do that all day everyday and wouldn’t do that again but respect the respect they show their elephants. Then we fed monkeys banana and left early to travel to Koh Samui which Sam so nicely arranged everything for us to get there in an affordable way. Highly recommend Sam he was a hoot and such a nice guy to guide us as well as chat and learn about Thailand and their way of life. He’s just so softly spoken. We missed some things he said he’s gotta speak a little louder😄! Would recommend spending time in this beautiful park if you’re in Thailand it would be an absolute shame to not! Sorry about the lengthy review but I wish there had been one for us before going to better know what was ahead!

Bring: bug repellent (not as buggy as we thought but they’re out there), waterproof shoes, sunscreen, bathing suit, flip flops for boat adventures, waterproof bag for phone while in boat to take pictures (amazon has great cheap ones) and a great attitude!
reviews.title: 3 day/2 night lake and jungle stay

reviews.text: We booked an airport to hotel transfer with this company when we flew into Phuket and stayed at Khao Lak. We were impressed with the price and service so asked them to send someone to the hotel the next day to book some tours. A German chap called Lars came and spent ages with us going through all the options. He was really knowledgeable and helpful but not at all pushy. We ended up booking 3 trips and thoroughly enjoyed them all. Couldn't fault them on anything. Vehicles and drivers all top notch. Trips were all very well organised. Included lunches were at really nice places and food delicious. The best thing was the guides.. we had Dido  for the elephant safari and bath  (ABSOLUTELY LOVED THIS TRIP!!) Don for the Similan islands trip (he was so funny, but also so knowledgeable and really careful with safety and comfort of all the guests) and Michael for the off road safari (a lovely German man who was obviously a regular on this trip and his enthusiasm and love for the area was infectious) All three were first class and made the trips so enjoyable. Thankyou Khao Lak Land Discovery. Would definitely use you again.
reviews.title: Highly recommend this tour company!

reviews.text:
This is a very commoditized offering with hundreds of companies offering the same trip with a near identical itinerary - you see them all at the lunch stop, which was accurately described as ‘chinatown’ by our guide. It could still have been a fun day spent in beautiful, picturesque islands, with soft white sands and perfect blue water - as the surroundings are perfect, but ruined by hoardes of tourists being shepherded around in packs. The company did nothing to curate or enhance the experience. We were sold on this company, which is more expensive than competitors, by our diving school owner saying it was a smaller, more intimate experience with good guides. But the reality was the opposite.

There were 30+ people on the trip, we followed the same itinerary as everyone else and ended up on crowded spots with boat after tourist boat arriving, hordes posing for pictures etc. - it had a package tour feel which wasn’t justified by the price. 

The main disappointments were 1. very little time spent snorkeling (2 x 45 mins stops) with the rest being free time at a crowded beach - not what was advertised 2. The guide spoke barely any English, so you couldn’t really communicate behind the limited script they knew. Given this, the instructions were very poor and made the whole experience annoying (eg.; being told we will go for a walk after lunch, but not that the walk will end in a beach with an hour plus of swim time - we did not carry towels, sunscreen etc.) 3. The snorkeling locations didn’t have a whole lot to see - maybe it was our bad luck, but the guide did nothing to orient us to the terrain, point out interesting things etc. All he said was go and be back in 45. 4. Just before we finished, we got lucky and spotted a turtle, but instead of watching it from a distance as any experienced diver or snorkeller would, the guide encouraged the group to surround it, touch it and feed it bananas. This was the most annoying part of the trip and probably explains why there was so little to see at the snorkelling spots 5. Lunch was awful (we are not picky eaters and love local food), but that we were not terribly upset about, as is to be expected on a remote island. But there were other groups whose companies had put up much better spreads.
reviews.title: Similian Snorkelling - Barely any snorkeling

reviews.text: Excellent customer service helping my family and I to set up transportation to Ao Nang. They were busy every time I stepped in the air conditioned office. They do tours, transfers and bike rentals. Would definitely recommend and use them again!
reviews.title: Helped with transportation to Ao Nang /Krabi /Railay

reviews.text: Used for transfers to and from Phuket to Khao Lak. Website appeared more professional than other transfer providers and the price was reasonably competitive. Payment was efficient and simple to follow. Generally the service was very good with a comfortable vehicle and cold water provided. There was a little delay at the airport as our driver was waiting elsewhere and had to be called by another meet and greet rep but the driver on the way back was prompt. I would use them again.
reviews.title: Airport transfers

reviews.text:
First off - the negative experience we had was not with someone representing this company so for that reason we have given a 5* 

James Bond Trip
Tim was our guide on a small group of 6 we were the only English but he happily translated  into all languages, we had a impromptu stop at the local food market which we loved a real insight into Thai life! Tim had a great sense of humour and had us all in stiches all day! Unfortunately during the canoe trip we did not have the best guide, he repeatedly asked us if we liked 'Bob Marley' which in our innocence we said yes, at this point he produced some cannabis he obviously wanted to sell it or smoke with us - before we boarded we were advised by Tim that canoeist expect a small tip he advised 100baht is a fair amount, he badgered us the entire time telling us about how he lost his parents in the Tsunami and that he had a baby to feed (showing us pictures etc) to the point we were unable to enjoy the canoe ride - all of that he said may well have been true however when we gave him 100baht he looked at us in disgust and made us feel really awkward about giving him more money I got the feeling everything he said was a ploy to get us to give him a big tip - we both got off feeling really unsettled by the whole thing. The rest of the day was without hitch the lunch at the floating village was superb! 

Surin Islands 
A must do trip - Don was our guide, again great sense of humour, we were well fed and watered the whole day, the boat ride was long (over and hour) but it was totally worth it, have never experienced snorkelling like it, the Mokens stop was unreal - lunch at the national park very good buffet - really can not say anything negative about the day!!!!

We were offered these trips with another operator much cheaper but we did find that It was worth spending slightly more - they were a really friendly well organised company - would recommend!!!
reviews.title: Great Service let down by 1 individual

reviews.text:
Great way to spend one day if you are in Khao Lak or near by. When Brenda from Pirate Divers suggested this tour i was a bit skeptical, but I was very much mistaken. 3 Temple tour is whole day trip that includes visits to Wat Manee Sri Mahathat Temple, Wat Bang Rieng (Thap Put) or Kings Temple and Dragon cave Temple. Also, very good lunch is included in Tasai restaurant which is on the place where Sea is going up to river. Beautiful place.
Every Temple has its own story to tell, and our guide Voelker give us great value for money spent. His deep knowledge on Buddhism and Thai culture and history helps a lot to understand and appreciate those Temples, and their significance to Thai people. Our driver Supap drive us very safely, and we could not enjoy more.
I will post some pictures, but you really need to be there to fully understand, and above all to feel the atmosphere of those sacred sites.
reviews.title: 3 Temple tour - What a way to spend one day

reviews.text:
We did the Hong Island tour; fantastic trip from start to finish. Loved all the stops and the staff were lovely; although we were the only English speakers so felt we may have missed some information. 
I had some difficulties with credit card payment (overcharged by €530) which were satisfactorily resolved when I got home, but was stressful.
reviews.title: Great experience.

reviews.text:
Unser Tourguide Volker war äußerst sympathisch. Volker's Wissen über die Tempel, die Religionen und Thailand ist beeindruckend. An allen Tempeln und im Restaurant waren fast keine anderen Touristen. Also konnte Volker uns Alles in Ruhe erklären. Das Restaurant hat eine traumhafte Lage an einem langeln Fjord, und das Essen inkl. dem Service waren sehr gut. Resumee: absolut empfehlenswert ! (wie üblich im Landesinneren: kein Wind. Bei dieser hohen Luftfeuchtigkeit und Neigung viel zu Schwitzen, kleines Handtuch mitnehmen. Obwohl es kühle Getränke und nach jeder Tempelbesichtigung große Erfrischungstücher gibt.)

Engl.: Our tour guide Vodka* was very sympathic. He explained everything in German and in English, because in our Minivan where also three danish tourists.  (*how Thais call him, because their problem to pronounce Volker) His knowledge about all Thai Temple, the religions and Thailand is impressive. At all three temple were nearly no other tourists. So Vodka could explain everything unhurriedly. The restaurant is at a beautiful place (kind of Fjord), and the meal incl the service where very good. Conclusion: 100% recommendable! (as usual in the interior Country: no wind. Because of the high humidity and if you sweat fast/a lot , it's better to take a small towel for this trip. Although you will get Cold drinks and a refreshing tissue after each temple visit.)
reviews.title: 3-Tempel-Tour

reviews.text: A great days adventure guaranteed here, and good value for the price. 2 great Hosts for the 1 & half hr ride, plus, 1st stop is a local fishing village, with good initial information, and ongoing, on island, and info about the 80 inhabitants x (Burmese) from our Thai guides, fascinating! 2 fantastic snorkel sites, punctuated by an inclusive Thai lunch, and swim on island. Highly Recommended days outing here. Thanks to you Ajax.
reviews.title: Day Snorkel trip to Surin Islands

reviews.text: Myself and my husband went on the off road safari, which entailed canoeing down a river for 1.5hrs , lunch and an off road jungle safari.  All I can say is we are so glad we booked with this company, they only have small groups which makes the trip feel special.  We were with a group of four Australians so there were only 6 of us on the tour.  This made our day, the group we were with were funny and friendly.  Our driver and guide were awesome (unfortunately we cant remember their names) we went on the 25.10.2017.  The guide let us stop at the Tsunami memorial, which was very moving, not a part of the trip, but we really appreciated him stopping and telling us about this event.  The canoeing was just awesome, we had so much fun - our guide made our trip really special and funny. We swam in the river and after canoeing we went for lunch.  This was amazing and they made sure they catered for me (Vegetarian). We felt very well looked after and not rushed what so ever.  We then ventured off on the off road jungle safari, this was fantastic, even in the rain!! We could not make it to the waterfall as it was too slippy, but our group made the most of the rest of the trip. On the way back the rain was torrential, which made it all the more fun, even stopping to see the rubber trees.  I would recommend this trip to anyone, the guide and driver made this day one of the best on our holiday as well as being with the lovely people we were on the trip with. Well worth it.
reviews.title: Off Road Safari - Amazing

reviews.text: Excellent tour with the multi lingual guide Volker, who explained everything we wanted to know about, whether about the temples or the countryside we travelled through. Lunch and cool drinks included. Highly recommended.
reviews.title: Tour of three temples - full day

reviews.text: We did a full day trip to Khao Sok NP.  First of all they were quite late picking us up.  We had to phone them to find out what was happening.  By the time they arrived, we could have used the extra time to have some breakfast before leaving.  The driver was very erratic and just a bit scary.  Overtaking on blind corners for example and going at extremely high speeds the entire way.  Was going so fast (which I assume was because they were running behind time), that going over several bumpy parts at high speed, we would bounce quite high up in our seats and get thrown around even with the seat belts on!  The boat ride on the way over on the lake was nice but again felt rushed.  The jungle trek was okay - we just sort of walked along without much explanation of anything.  Lunch was nice and the swim in the lake was also nice (although would have been better to have longer than 15 minutes doing this).  The long tail boat ride back had no shelter and it was raining so not very comfortable.  I just expected more based on the reviews and just felt disappointed in the day overall at the price charged.
reviews.title: Below average day

reviews.text: We had a great time on the half day tour. Di Dho was a very friendly and knowledgeable guide. The elephant jungle ride was very good, much better jungle than we have experienced further south in Phuket. The elephant handlers were friendly and the elephants were well cared for. Ton Pling waterfall was excellent - a beautiful spot and we enjoyed an excellent swim. Lunch afterwards was one of the best meals we have had in Thailand to date. Overall we had a wonderful time.
reviews.title: Half day elephant ride and waterfall swim

reviews.text: Just took a full day discovery tour to phi phi island with them departing from khao lak. It made for a tremendously long day but so worth it!! Departed hotel at 6am and returned around 5. 2 hour car ride to marina. 1 hour boat ride to "the beach" and it was worth every second of that bumpy boat ride. Then snorkeling twice, monkey island (scary), swimming once in a gorgeous bay, lunch on a beautiful beach (free lunch was crap though went out after). Such a gorgeous day with a great crew!!! If you don't want to be on a boat with 20 you can pay for a private boat.
reviews.title: Phi phi island tour - departed from khao lak

reviews.text:
We took the off road tour, booked thru our hotel's travel counter.
The transfer was on time, the guide (Di Dho) and the driver (Au) were very kind and welcoming.
Driver was very experienced in rough roads, the guide was very professional and made many adjustments just for our pleasure (adding an additional stop, changing the lunch hour, etc)
We were 3 people and were the only group for that day yet surprisingly the company still deployed the tour just for us.
We were informed in advance that the whole tour or parts of it might be canceled if weather will won't allow, It did rain pretty badly that day but we managed to do almost the whole tour besides going down to the waterfall when reaching the mountain peak. Besides missing a small portion of the itinerary, the rainy day actually enhanced our experience: Riding thru the muddy road. The guides provided us with rain light jackets and the car had an open rain cover. Definitely would recommend not canceling by yourself if you hit a rainy day.
Would like to point out how professional and very caring 

I would recommend bringing a wind coat also in sunny days due to the fact the car is open and a big portion of the ride is pretty fast which made it feel quite chilly back there.

Overall a very great trip with lovely guides, very recommended!
reviews.title: Off road tour with Di Dho

reviews.text: We chose to do the Temple Tours as part of our 'things to do'. We learnt a lot about buddishm and the 3 temples we saw. Our tour guide was bilingual so we were able to understand and learnt everything at no disadvantage.
reviews.title: Temple Tours

reviews.text:
We booked the James Bond Island tour with Khao Lak Land Discovery. We were picked up early enough in the morning so that there were very few people on the island by the time we arrived.

After the island, we went to a floating village where we learned about the history of the village and had lunch. The lunch was amazing, nice fresh food. 

We then went to the Monkey Temple. Our guide, Sonny, was really friendly and told us all about the history and culture. His English is very good and he’s very helpful. We were constantly asking him questions, which he seemed to like as he said that not many people care about the ins and outs of Buddhism, they just want to take a picture of the big Buddha and then leave.

We really enjoyed our trip and it was definitely one of the highlights of our trip.
reviews.title: James Bond Island Tour

reviews.text:
We made a last minute decision to visit the elephants & were keen to find somewhere that was ethically run.
We researched as much as possible in the short time we had & ended up going with Khao Lak Discovery Tours.
We had an exceptionally wonderful tour.
We went on the half day tour which went either side of a delicious Thai lunch. 
We travelled in a comfortable minibus via a stop at the waterfalls. 
We first met our elephant guide who obviously had a lovely relationship with his elephant.  After a delightful ride through the jungle, where our guide happily took photos all the way along, we arrived back to what was the highlight. ... awonderful swim with our elephant.
Our guide from the tour company was very helpful, knowledgeable & friendly. 
I would definitely recommend this company & experience to anyone who has just a short time available to experience these beautiful animals.
reviews.title: Elephant bath & trek

reviews.text:
We booked the half day Elephant Trek, bathing and waterfall visit with this company who have a branch just down from our hotel (The Merlin Resort). The tour was an unforgettable experience and my worries over the welfare of these wonderful creatures was put at ease when I saw them with their keepers. We took our two children aged 7 and 9 with us and the tour was appropriate for their ages.
As we'd had such a great experience, we booked the 'James Bond Island' tour with some trepidation as to how our children would cope with the heat and long day. Yet again, we were delighted with all aspects of the trip. Air conditioned minibus, plenty of cold drinks supplied, lunch laid on and the most stunning scenery. Our tour guide, Gift, was an enthusiastic and friendly host and her English was perfect. I would highly recommend this company.
reviews.title: Brilliant Tour Company!

reviews.text: Brilliant day out, tour guide very helpful, flexible and knowledgeable. Great fun for everyone in our party. We also used this company for our return transfer from the airport. Very efficient and reasonably priced.
reviews.title: Khao Lak Nature Safari

reviews.text: Did Khoa lak safari tour and really enjoyed it. Our Dutch guide was very good and knowledgeable of the local culture
reviews.title: day out

reviews.text:
I went there because i wanted to see temples and i got just that and more i did not want to go back to the hotel room i think that everyone should go there ☺
Lucymc11 age 10
reviews.title: EPIC

reviews.text: We took the one day temple tour. It was just the 4 of us and we had a perfect day. Our guide, Gift, was knowledgeable and brought her own experiences in as a practicing Buddhist. It was not just a tick list sightseeing trip as s result- it was much deeper than that and showed us the way of life in Thailand as much as the sights. The lunch was unreal - done if the best food we'd had in Thailand overlooking the mangroves. The weather was pretty shocking but it didn't make any difference (they even gave us waterproofs to use!) thank you for a day we will never forget. would highly recommend.
reviews.title: Temple tour

reviews.text: Did this tour recently with tour guide Michael he was fantastic he spoke German English andThai we had a great day my wife and I are in our mid fifties and reasonably fit so the jungle trek part wasn't too bad for us or you can sit this part out Many thanks
reviews.title: Kha Sok national Park

reviews.text:
We made a last minute decision to go to some tours. We went on the Culture Safari where we had a chance to bath and feed elephants, go down a river on a bamboo raft, lunch and play by a waterfall, visit a turtle conservation and visit to the Tsunami museum. 

Our guide was so knowledgeable and attentive to our needs.

The tour was well organised and value for money. It was appropriate for all ages. We had my father who wad 81 and my children 8 and 5.
reviews.title: Amazing trips

reviews.text: A fabulous experience and the highlight of our 8 days in Thailand. Our tour guide, Volker, was experienced, spoke about 4 languages- made the day fun and extremely interesting with his anecdotes. Would highly recommend it .
reviews.title: Khao Sok Land Discovery day tour

reviews.text: We were picked up promptly and from this moment Dido and our driver treated us well. We were fortunate that we had great weather. The canoe trip was very relaxing. Cool to see monkeys and a catfish. Lunch was yummy and plentiful.  Elephant handler was respectful of the animal and was happy to take photos. The little extras made this a tour to remember.
reviews.title: Full day khao sok canoe and elephant ride

reviews.text: This was a well organised tour, and very private as well - after our group left the dock in our boat, we saw no other tourists on our tour. We had a great time at Singing Beach, as well as snorkelling around in the ocean. The buffet lunch had a few choices but was cooked very well, and the tour guide very friendly. The trip we chose was the Dolphin Cruise Daytrip. Thanks for a great tour!
reviews.title: Great tour!

reviews.text:
We booked this trip through our hotel, got picked up at 8am in a nice air conditioned mini van, our tour guide Gift was so lovely all day and made our day really enjoyable, she had lots of knowledge and her English was really good. 
We were offered complimentary cold water and soft drinks throughout the day which was a nice touch. Great value for money considering everything you get, we saw the island, a floating village with lunch included, and visited a temple.
The whole day was amazing I really recommend this tour and company.
reviews.title: Great day trip to James Bond Island

reviews.text:
We used this organization for a 3 temples day tour and snorkeling tour around Surin Islands and we weren't dissapointed! Our trip to the 3 temples tour was with tour guide named Kim and she made the day so enjoyable and her knowledge of Thailand and its many wonders was impressive.  We loved the whole day and the  day went as planned lunch was amazing and the sites well worth it. 
Surin Islands was simply stunning and as a first time snorkeling experience was made so easy by the experienced staff. Our guide was Joe and he was informative and very humorous thank you Joe!
reviews.title: Awesome tours!

reviews.text:
We went on a tour with Khao Lak Land Discovery last week. Our tour guide Gift was amazing! First off I want to thank Gift for a truly wonderful experience. Our tour started off by visiting the Tsunami Centre. The passion Gift showed & felt when explaining what had happened in the area and how it impacted her & her loved ones was so genuine it really made us take a look at our own lives and the privileges we have. Next we went to the Turtle Conservation Center. It was touching seeing all of the hard work the locals put into trying to revive the turtle population in the local area. Then we went bamboo rafting. Wow! Just wow! Our guide on this tour was a charmer. Stopping off down the river to pick us flowers, showing us the bamboo, bananas, snakes. It was one of the best back to nature experiences, feeling like we were the only ones floating on a bamboo raft down the river. After the rafting we drove to a waterfall where we had lunch. As you can imagine the food was absolutely delicious. And taking in the views of the waterfall as we ate was the icing on the cake. Then we visited a temple, we seen the monks and we were treated to some more amazing knowledge that Gift shared with us about local traditions and buddhism. It was insightful. 

The best was saved for last. The Elephant Bath..... and it was amazing. We were up and close with the youngest female elephant and her trainer. Bathing in a large pool and giving her a nice scrub down. It was something I will never forget. It was scary I must admit but the experience was amazing.

Our driver Suk then drove us back to our hotel in Karon Beach which was a bit over 2 hours away. We did spend a lot of time travelling but I would do it again and again as it was well worth the travel and the money. 

I can't wait to do another tour next time I visit.

Thank you so much Gift. We had a truly amazing day.

Kristy and Shenayah xoxo
reviews.title: Absolutely Amazing!!

reviews.text: Had a great day snorkeling around Surin Islands. Staff were incredibly knowledgeable and helpful. Our guide Don was great! Thanks for the great experience.
reviews.title: Great trip & great staff

reviews.text:
I had a great experience 2 day Khao Sok stay in the Tree House. Took my 6 year old boy he loved it a great learning experience. Not to visit Kha Sok would be a crime. Great guides I am from uk and everyone else was from Germany both guides chatted to me in english and for me not being with a load of english was a big bonus. Both guides were great but i loved Eric he was funny and helpful and knowledgable.
Also hired a car and driver and guide for another day. Don fantastic guide learnt so much. thanks both these trips were the best part of my visit.
reviews.title: proffessional and personal

reviews.text: Had a great driver Eakasak Siripthanan who took us around all day to the sites we wanted. He was friendly, always on time and really looked after us. Made it a great day out
reviews.title: DIY Tour

reviews.text:
We had a brilliant trip to Elephant Fantastic with Land Discovery. The guide was great, telling us all about Thailand and its people as well as the elephants at the site. I was a little concerned about going to a place that exploited animals, and while I'm still slightly concerned about elephants carrying tourists all day, the trek was short and the elephants seemed well seed for. The handlers were friendly and kind to the elephants and the site was professional and clean. I enjoyed bathing the elephants at the end of the trip.

Worth the half day visit for sure. We also had a great lunch provided by the locals. Better than I have had on other trips.
reviews.title: Great day with the elephants

reviews.text:
My husband and I booked this day trip through our hotel (Sentido Graceland) and thoroughly enjoyed it. The tour guide Nedo and his colleague picked us up promptly from our hotel in a clean, air conditioned minibus with unlimited water and Pepsi available. We drove to a pier where we boarded a long-tail boat that took us on a 45 minute trip past mangroves and rock formations to the famous James Bond Island. We stayed there for around 45 minutes to take pictures and explore the small bay. We then headed to a Kayak experience, where we had our own guide who steered us around a cave and into some mangroves - this was the highlight of the trip for me, the scenery is beautiful. Make sure you take your phone and wallet in something waterproof (the kayak-driver expects a reasonable tip!) and you can even buy a coconut from a passing boat. After this, we stopped off at a local fishing village built on stilts for lunch. The food was fabulous and there was plenty of it! You can then explore the village, but expect to be hassled by market sellers as you go. 
Finally we took the long-tailed boat back to the pier and a short minibus ride to a Temple in a cave, surrounded by Monkeys. This again, was a highlight. The temple itself is fascinating, but be sure to wear a long skirt or trousers/shorts and cover your shoulders as with all Temples in Thailand. You can also buy food for the monkeys and hand feed them (they're still wild so be careful not to feed too many of them at once, as they can get aggressive with one another). 
Our tour guide spoke relatively good English and was keen to practice, but was a lovely, genuine guy who really wants you to enjoy your day. He offered us cold towels and drinks at regular intervals and thoroughly looked after us. Thank you for helping us to create such special memories.
reviews.title: An excellent trip  to Pang Nga Bay (James Bond Island) with Nedo

reviews.text: We booked the transfer from the airport to our resort. This was smoothly alright. The guy from the company came the day after to sell some daytrips to us. I really liked to go to surin island with this company so we booked it with them. No one arrived on this date to pick us up - we waited more than one hour. So we decided to do something different on the day. The good thing was we didn't payed yet. A couple of days later (we told the guy we will stay Til Friday ) on Sunday the driver to pick us up arrived. But It takes a few minutes to explain what's went wrong. So the stuff of our hotel we're annoyed and also the driver and at least we because we get all the trouble on a Sunday morning. It's not a big thing. Normally for me but this was the holiday with my mum and it should be a good vacation. Maybe it's a good agency for tours but in our case everything went wrong. Sadly.
reviews.title: Could be a nice trip.

reviews.text: First half was a little boring due to the driving but monkey temple was funny and good, turtles were a bit dirty and I feel like they shouldn't be kept there but hey each to their own. The rest of the journey was amazing and would recommend for the price. Elephants and bamboo rafting
reviews.title: Absolutely amazing

reviews.text: My husband, two children (aged 7 and 9) and I joined a full day tour on the Khao Lak Nature Safari and loved every minute! The day was very well organised, our guide and driver were friendly and professional and the sites we toured were all worthwhile. We loved exploring "Hat Lek" beach (which was deserted apart from our small group) and enjoyed visiting the turtles at the Turtle Conservation Centre. The elephant ride was enjoyable and lunch was divine! Overall, this tour was a great way to explore a beautiful pocket of Thailand and I would highly recommend them to others.
reviews.title: A great way to explore a beautiful corner of Thailand!

reviews.text:
We've had an amazing tour through 3 fascinating temples in the Khao Lak region. Our guide Volker and his driver Somkit did a really good job that made this tour unique and it will definitely stay in our mind. A lot of background informations about Buddhism and Thailand are shared so that this tour never got boring. We can recommend it all the way!
Thanks
reviews.title: 3 Temple Tour

reviews.text: We booked on 2 trips with Khan Lak Land Discovery. The first was the 3 Temples tour. Our guide, Volker, was amazing, so knowledgeable and informative. He made this cultural trip really interesting, as well as educational and was more than happy to answer any questions. I would really recommend this excursion, although it's not for anyone with mobility issues due to the amount of steps, especially at the third temple.  The 2nd trip was to James Bond Island and was great fun, especially the sea canoe through the mangroves. Lunch at the sea gypsy village was very good and again, our Thai guide was lovely and we even had an unscheduled stop at a traditional Thai market on the way back. There are many booking agents in Khao Lak but I am happy that we chose Land Discovery for the quality of the guides and transport.
reviews.title: 2 fabulous trips

reviews.text: We have done 2 tours and both went well. They do care of people and make all activities in a safe environment. I take this opportunity to thank Joelle for her commitment, her professionalism and her kindness. Thanks to her we keep a marvelous souvenir!
reviews.title: Well organized tours !

reviews.text:
We booked the 2 days / 1 night trip to khao sok (the jungle expedition one) from phuket.

They usually pick guests from khao lak but they can come and pick you up from phuket provided that you pay 3000 baht per vehicle.

Our tour guide was Sam, a local thai who was awesome ! He was really accommodating with us, took great care of us. He even brought us to some places that were not included in the tour, made us try many thai food when we were on the markets. He speaks perfectly german and english.

About the tour : the elephant trekking was really nice. Im not keen on elephant trekking but i have to admit, the scenery is pretty nice.
We then went for a ride in canoe.. really peaceful. We saw monkeys and snakes.
The trek in the jungle is really peaceful too, careful for your shoes it is really muddy but they can lend you some plastic shoes if you want (50 baht).
We went to the lake, which was amazing !

The food was awesome and really good.
We stayed in the rock and treehouse hotel which was one of the best room we had in our trip.

The only downside of this tour is that I wish we could have spent more time on the lake.

Anyway, thanks Sam for this awesome trip !
reviews.title: Best tour guide I had so far !

reviews.text: We used this company in November for our day tour to Khao Lak, from being picked up in a ac van, very comfortable.  The drive was great and the guide makes you feel very relaxed. We went to watch the baby elephant, then riding around. Definitely recommend this to anyone who visits Phuket 😀
reviews.title: Elephant Trekking!!!

reviews.text: Spent a day with Gift and Tu around Khao Lak.  Little Beach, river rafting and elephant tour. Our guide was very informative at each stop. A big thank you from Mom to Tu for his attention while she was feeling ill. Lots of cold water available in van.  Very happy with our tour. Would also like to add that all elephants appeared in very good health and were treated with respect by handlers.
reviews.title: Khao Lak discovery tour.

reviews.text:
We had an awesome day out with our guide Gift and driver Phong. 
The drive for 1 hour, with a total of 12 pax in a new comfortable mini van, was safe and informative. Once at the pier we got on our longtail boat for ride out to James Bond Island. We had been before but from Phuket in a big boat, so we were very happy to arrive before the crowds👍👍👍.
Then on to canoe trip through caves and carsts was good despite it getting windy with weather to the south being bad.👍👍.
On to lunch at the stilt/floating village which was awesome to see life on the water. But you only need a little time there. Food was good and plentiful. 👍👍.
Then was back to pier before storm and off to temple of the monkey which was ok. We have seen loads of temples over SE Asia. Then as a surprise for a guest who was celebrating a 21st birthday we stopped at a swimming spot on the way home which was nice as it was a hot day.
Many thanks to Gift and Phong
reviews.title: James bond island

reviews.text: We went on two excursions with them. Both were well organized and the guides were all very kind. I would recommend them to you, if you are staying in Khao Lak, as they are locally embedded.
reviews.title: Elephant Bath & James Bond Island

reviews.text: We booked this trip according to what was written in the tour book and really liked the sound of visiting a number of snorkelling spots much different to other companies we saw. We were disappointed to hear at our morning briefing that the itinerary would be very different and nothing like what was written. We went to the sea gypsy village which we didn't want to do (as we purely wanted a snorkelling trip which was what was suggested in the book). We only went to 2 snorkelling spots and spent 1.5 hours at the National park lounging around on a beach. Beautiful snorkelling spots but again we would of loved to of snorkelled more!
reviews.title: Surin island

reviews.text:
Very professional and welcoming company, with German and English speaking guides. They were were thorough and helpful when we arrived at the hotel, and we ended up booking two activities with them, a cooking course, which was very fun, they made it very personal, by making a limit of 6 people, so we all had a change to prepare different Thai dishes. The second activity, a trip to Similan island was also very good, the guides took really good care of every single person, and even though there was a few bumps on the trip, I never felt that it was not under control.
A very pleasant experience with Khao Lak Land discovery.
reviews.title: Dejlig oplevelse

reviews.text: I booked the Surin island trip with Khao Lak Land Discovery. The boat was from Adventureworld. The trip was horrible and in my opinion not safe. There was a lot of puking and also was the snorkeling bad because no good spots were available due to the weather. But biggest complaint is that they went out with those weather conditions. I hurt my hip and shoulder while clinging on in the boat for 1,5 hours to and 1,5 hours back. It had a big impact on the rest of my vacation and I had to spent time in my hotelbed with ice cubes to get the swelling down. I went to Ecoadventures to find an easy and short day trip. Even if I could have physically done a snorkeling or boat trip, I found out that they were not available due to a weather warning from the Thai government. Ecoadventure had cancelled all their boat trips for four days, including the day I went on the boat trip with Discovery. I went to Discovery head office with my complaint. They did not take it seriously and laughed me out of their office after offering me a refund of 12 euro (taxi to their office cost me 9) for a trip that cost around 100 euro. Discovery did not dissociate themsemselves from Adventureworld to go out with that weather. Safety should be first and foremost. Be careful booking a snorkeling trip with either of them. In my opinion: don't do it!
reviews.title: Irresponsible boat trip

reviews.text:
Booked the elephant half day and the day trip to Surin
Very pro in both occasions 
very dedicated staff speaking English, German and French 
Program fully respected from pick up to the end
All the family really enjoyed
reviews.title: Serious and pro

reviews.text: The early wake up was worth it. We were a big group traveling together and it was well coordinated. The scenery was amazing and we avoided the crowds. Excellent guide.
reviews.title: Hong tour

reviews.text: Very well organized trip to Phang Nga Bay with longtail boat: James Bond Island, canoeing, Koh Panyee village... our guide Gift was very friendly and helpful, thank you once again!
reviews.title: Phang Nga daytrip was excellent!

reviews.text:
We used Land Discovery for a day trip to Phang Nga Bay, James Bond Island and Hong Island. 

We left Khao Lak at 5am to reach Phang Nga Bay at dawn for an incredible sunrise while riding on a longboat. This also meant we missed the rush at James Bond Island and the Hong Island sea canoe through the rooms! Incredible experience!

Lunch was provided at the Sea Gypsy settlement which was HOT but probably one the tastiest we had. And finally getting back on the sea canoe through the mangroves and tight fitting caves!

Huge thank you to our guide Fabio who made sure he translated everything clearly to both us (English) and the four others (German). The guys we went with on the canoes and longboat were also really helpful and good fun! They have great knowledge of everything they do! 

Our driver Keattisak was one of the best we had in Thailand. After leaving a spare pair of shoes in the van we managed to get Edwin to very nicely ask Keattisak to drop them back to our hotel, I can not thank this team enough!
reviews.title: The Tours of choice!

reviews.text: We used Khao Lak Land Discovery for our day trip to the Surin Islands. The service was exceptional. Pick up was directly from our hotel (Casa De La Flora) and on time. We arrived at the pier to friendly guides (1 English speaking, 1 German speaking, 1 French speaking) and a sufficient breakfast with teas/coffees/juices/water and toast/cereal/fruits. Payment was by cash on the day, again very organised and efficient. All three guides came with us on the boat; always making our safety their top priority. Although the journey to Surin took 1hr 30mins by speed boat, the scenery was enough to keep us all occupied. The whole day went without a glitch, and constantly offered cold drinks and snacks to keep our energy up for snorkelling. The areas where we snorkelled was brilliant and very quiet. We had free roam of the area and the corals and fish we saw was outstanding. The staff were exceptional, very approachable, very knowledgable, and most of all fun. I could say so much more, but overall I would highly recommend booking any excursion with Khao Lak Land Discovery.
reviews.title: Exceptional Day

reviews.text: We booked this tour through our resort and were really impressed. Picked up on the dot at 7.30 in a new air-conditioned mini bus we were warmly greeted by our English speaking guide. We saw three very different but all beautiful temples. Stopped for a nice thai lunch near the beautiful Tha Sai river with about 8 different dishes and great views. Be warned there are stairs to climb especially at the first temple, if you get travel sick take something and if you are scared of monkeys be wary at the third temple
reviews.title: A Packed Full Day Tour

reviews.text: Loved this group.. we did three tours and all were fantastic.  Buses were perfect and workers were responsible, entertaining and knowledgable.  They made our trip truly memorable.  As we were in a group of 6, two trips were just us, which was great!  The third trip was snorkling at Surin Island which was amazing!  Loved visiting the moken villages.  Hong Island was great with a traditional touch.  Very recommended!
reviews.title: Three tours and all amazing!

reviews.text: Our trip organiser  Jimmy at The sands , Khao lak recommended this trip as the weather was too rough to visit the similian islands , l did not think anything would be as good but we had a wonderful day , our guides You our English guide was amazing , spoke A++ English , informative , so much fun , genuinely enjoyed his job and his guests which showed in his work , thank s so much You for an amazing day , also on boat German guide Ken , he spoke 5 languages , and was also incredible and mingled with all the guests German and English , these guys and the crew made the trip . We started very early 5.30 from Khao lak hotel , driving to Phuket , boarded a speed boat only about 20 people in the group and everyone mingled well despite language differences this was due to our tour guides , 1. Hour out to  the most amazing bright blue waters in a lagoon where we spent time diving from the front of the boat swimming in the most beautiful water with limestone cliffs all around us near Krabi national park , it was spectacular , then we went to Maya bay for snorkelling which was good , the coral coming back from tsunami and fish , this is not Fiji islands or the Maldives but if you love snorkelling was good , the crew set up a beautiful lunch for us at Bamboo island which was wonderful , visited monkey beach then more snorkelling , the crew pick out different areas where snorkelling is good , the second snorkel not as visible but still really enjoyed the day . Then an hours speedboat back to phuket marina and an hour drive back to Khao lak .it was well worth going so early as very little in the way of crowds , so not many people later boats coming from everywhere  so much food and whatever in soft drink , water you wanted to drink in huge esky on boat complimentary , they also supply sea sickness pills before leaving if needed . A big thank you to all the crew for an amazing day , Leah and Brett buckwell ,australia
reviews.title: Wonderful snorkelling trip with exceptional guides You and Ken

reviews.text: First time we have done a tour in Khao Lak. We were picked up in an air conditioned and looking forward to our day trip. Overall the long tail boat ride to James Bond island and the canoe ride along the mangroves. Lunch at the Muslim village was fantastic however the time there was one hour too long just wondering the markets. 45 minutes to shop would have been sufficient.  Our guide could have done more and provided more facts about James Bond island, fishing village or even Khao Lak. We were told we would get an English speaking guide however as majority of our group was German our guide seemed to provide more find facts in German than English which meant we did a lot of standing around. Overall your was enjoyable but have had better.
reviews.title: Phang Nga Bay day trip

reviews.text:
We did the nature safari tour which included a jungle walk to a beach, bamboo rafting, turtle sanctuary and elephant ride and a swim at a waterfall. We just loved the whole trip!!! Our tour guide Porn did everything to make us safe and comfortable offering drinks, wet cloths to cook us down, jackets if it rained and great info throughout the day. 
A day we won't forget.....thank you
reviews.title: Khao lak nature safari

reviews.text:
We booked this trip the day after we arrived in Thailand. It was fantastic value for an all day trip. They picked us up in an air cooled taxi van. Our guide "Gift" spoke great English. She was both interesting and insightful into Buddhist culture. She was so enthusiastic about each temple she's inspired me to learn more about Buddhist culture. 

This is a fantastic trip.We gained so much from it and both felt we learned a great deal about Thai culture.
reviews.title: Three Temples Tour

reviews.text:
expensive but worth the price! we made a family tour to the islands, went snorchling and enjoyed good food on a dream beach.
take your headphones or ear plugs! the 1 hour powerboat trip is very loud ...
reviews.title: pipi islands tour

reviews.text:
Really enjoyed this tour.
Our guide was great - friendly and a good communicator.
Great to have such a variety of things to do and see - James Bond Island, Kayaking, Kohpanyee, Cave Temple.
I had my 9 year old son on the tour but there were 2 families with very little kids -(1year old - 4 year old) - this tour is not ideal for kids this young and was quite disruptive the other travellers.
reviews.title: Great all inclusive tour

reviews.text:
Thank you to Faulkner who was outstanding and made us so welcome. We appreciate the wonderful job you and the canoe guides did. The other guests were lovely too and we were the only non German guests but felt like we were amongst friends. 10 out of 10. 
The trip was amazing - especially the nature part. The caves were stunning and the photo opportunities endless. I don't want to ruin it for you by going into descriptions but it was heavenly in parts. 
The only thing ruining it was the fact that the canoes were dreadful, with punctures. Seriously, need to replace these urgently. I would not recommend this for people that can't swim or with young children. Our canoe nearly collapsed and was hard to get out of and into the boat. Amusing at best, dangerous at worst. Please do sort this out. I noticed your company had the worst canoes. Lucky for you you had the best tour guide - otherwise you wouldn't get 5 stars. 
The fishing village was interesting. The lunch was great and very tasty. There were some delicious dishes and highly recommended. The toilets were awful - bring your own loo paper and sanitise your hands. There isn't much to do or buy in the village. Don't photograph the mosque - it has a sign saying "Muslims only" . You can walk pretty much anywhere though - including the school. The school children were very sweet and well dressed - it was interesting to see the school. All in all a wonderful day and an eye opener.  Hopefully you will have replaced your canoes by the time other people read this, so they won't need to worry. I would like to see your response. 
Thank you.
reviews.title: Great Trip - crappy canoes, brilliant tour guide

reviews.text:
What an awesome experience to see an bathe with the Elephants. We went on the trek in Wednesday 20 July. Janya, our tour guide, was extremely friendly and helpful. Tho, our driver, was excellent  and drove us around safely (the drivers in Thailand scare me most times). Unfortunately the weather wasn't on our side and we cut the trek short because the elephants weren't happy trekking in torrential rain. I didn't mind because if an elephant refuses to go up a steep muddy track I'm ok with that! 
Although one thing is I wouldn't recommend taking a 3 yo on the ride as mine was scared stiff and was getting bitten by some bugs which actually hurt. It was hard to hold on to a 3yo when it's raining and especially because there is only a rope to secure you. But lesson learnt not all 3 yo can do it. We returned to the bathing pool and had a great time seeing them enjoy the bath. The 7 month old baby was so adorable.  The love between the mother and baby is just adorable. From the elephant park we had lunch at the waterfall and the food provided was delicious. All in all a good day.
reviews.title: Elephant Trekking with Khao Lak Discovery Land Tours

reviews.text:
We did 2 trips - the Hong island and the phi phi trip.

Both were well organised and food and drink and snacks were great.

The Hong island one was great because it was much smaller.  6 people

Well worth the extra early start
reviews.title: Two great trips

reviews.text: We were picked up early from our resort and were greeted by the delightful Kim. She was a wonderful guide to all of us as we experienced visiting the Phang Nga Bay. The sights were great and being a small group of 8 easily enjoyed. I would definitely recommend this experience and the Tour Company. The only negatives were the disappointing water pollution and the continual reminder from our canoe man that we had to tip him. Otherwise a fantastic outing.
reviews.title: A great day!

reviews.text:
Did this tour the other day with our very energetic guide Ake.

Ake is a fantastic guy, very keen and did absolutely everything he could to ensure we had a good time and were safe. He was a very funny man and gave us plenty of information and tips on things. His english was ok for us (Aussie) but at times he was a little hard to understand and I could tell that some of the other tourists (European) were having a lot of difficulty understanding him. 

Transfers were very well handled and everything ran smoothly as far as timings go. The van and longtail rides were fine and our small group of 12 was perfect size for the trip.

Canoeing:

Yeah, it was ok I guess. Stinking hot in the life vests and you get a guy paddling for you. My guy spent most of the 30min paddle dropping hints about the gratuity/tip which wore thin. Fortunately for him I was one of the only ones in our group that actually took money with me on the canoe and he got a tip (which he didnt seem too happy with the amount which was more than generous in my opinion). Others in our group didnt have money with them which led to some shirty canoeists openly showing their dismay. This embarrassment could have been avoided if it was made clear on the website to take money for tips/gratuities.

James Bond Island:

Nice to look at but 20 mins would be more than enough. Absolute tourist trap, tons of tourists crowded and hustling about (and mayhem on the little thin track you need to walk across the island on). I heard some people had to pay to use toilets. 

Muslim Village Lunch:

Nice meal, good range of food. Village isnt very exciting but the locals are polite and not pushy. Best fed and healthiest cats I have ever seen!!!!

Monkey Temple:

Lame. Walk in and walk out, avoid the monkeys they are very unpredictable and some were actually grabbing and chasing people without any provocation. It has nothing on the Big Buddha not really sure why its part of the tour to be honest.

Overall, it wasnt bad but I cant say it was good. But it killed a day and I guess I am glad I experienced it. If it wasnt for the enthusiasm of Ake it would have got 2 stars though sorry guys!
reviews.title: Phang Na (James Bond / Canoe / Monkey Temple)

reviews.text:
Going stir crazy at the resort I was Talking to some other guests that had just returned from this safari and they raved about it.  We did the tour yesterday and were not disappointed. Our guide Tanya was great. First stop was Small Sandy Beach after a short walk through rain forest it's a fabulous beach swimming in pristine  ocean very secluded but with a small shop. Options are A 20 min jungle walk followed by a swim or just enjoy the beach. Next was bamboo rafting that was our highlight. Easy run down the river with some low key Rapids great scenery and a beaut swim after. Next was lunch one of the best feeds we have had here At cafe by waterfall. Followed by swim at waterfall. 
Next Elephant village that really look after the animals and an hr ride on the elephants that was very good. 
Lastly we visited the turtle regeneration farm run by the military. Vary interesting with turtles at different stages being cared for before being released. 
A great day out.
reviews.title: Khao Lak Nature Safari full day tour

reviews.text: Really enjoyable tour, with friendly tour guide. Went in a boat to James Bond island, got lunch at the floating village, then visited a monkey cave.
reviews.title: Excellent

reviews.text:
Took a full day nature tour with a couple & the tour guide so just the 4 of us.
Spectacular four well driver experience getting to the park. Had a least a full hour riding an elephant with the tour guide. Plus a thai lunch plus Jungle trek crossing logs over raging streams. Exciting! Monsoon rains hit us so be got saturated . Our guide, Benjaporn henge mana, was born & raised in the area and had heaps of local knowledge. We couldn't swim in the waterfall however sure the the water volume. Picked up & delivered from the Haven Resort.
reviews.title: Well worth the effort!

reviews.text: A fabulous day with Don as our guide. Great fun - highlight of our trip. Local knowledge of tides made for a better experience. Don worked hard to cater for different levels of experience. Highly recommend.
reviews.title: Phang Nga Discovery Cruise

reviews.text: After being picked up at our hotel, we were taken to a small, local market not usually frequented by tourists, which was wonderful. The prices were good too. We then drove through some very interesting scenery to the Khao Sok National Park. Our canoe trip, on the Sok river with a local guide, was a highlight. of the day. He carefully steered us to see tree snakes & monkeys as well as houses on stilts almost hidden in the jungle. We were shown how bamboo was cut to make cups, spoons and a "kettle" and we enjoyed coffee in a lovely setting. There was time for a swim in the cool, clear water before heading to the Monkey Temple. The monkeys were a little aggressive and very quick to grab our bananas and peanuts. This was followed by a quick trip to the Rock and Tree House for a delicious shared lunch. The afternoon was taken up by a one hour elephant ride through the jungle more a palm oil plantation!) to a tiny waterfall before being driven back to our hotel. We were very glad we'd taken this trip.
reviews.title: "A wonderful way to see more of the Khao Lak area."

reviews.text:
We did a half day tour elephant trek and bath. Washing the elephants in the nice cool pond was so amazing! Great experience for the kids. Lovely guide and driver looked after us.
Didn't realise we were expected to tip everyone on top of the tour fees. 
We also got airport transfers with this company which was very smooth.
reviews.title: Khao Lak tour and transfer

reviews.text: We went on the elephant trekking and bathing tour which was amazing! The elephants were very well looked after, where we were able to ride them on a short walk through a jungle area, then able to get into the water with them and bath them before feeding them some bananas at a small additional cost. Such an amazing experience! We then went to a waterfall and had a delicious lunch where we were able to socialise with others and able to experience more Thai food - overall such a memorable experience!
reviews.title: Amazing!!

reviews.text: Wow!! What an amazing tour. Very early start (4am) but so worth it. The islands are beautiful and the scenery is just divine. The canoeing for me was without a doubt the best part. James Bond Island was a little underwhelming when there are so many more beautiful spots to see. Our guide Tim made the day for us. His knowledge is incredible and he speaks 5 languages. Thai, English, German, French and Malaysian. He always has a smile on his face and made the day lots of fun. The floating village was much either. The monkey temple was fun, feeding the cute little monkeys bananas. Would highly recommend this trip and this tour company. Very professional, amazing tour guide and a really fun time.
reviews.title: Phang Nga Tour

reviews.text:
Look it was fine. The guide worked really hard to ensure our enjoyment and safety, which we appreciated. 
It was excellent to see the elephants and to see how well they are looked after. Keeping in mind that people have to make a living and if all you know is driving an elephant then it's great that tourism can pay people for what they do. Our Mahout was excellent and his English wasn't bad, enough to add another layer to the experience. 
Out of 10, 7 for the elephant experience. 
It's Thailand so food is important and also people need to make a living. So we stopped by a waterfall and ate at the "restaurants?" The food was excellent and the guide Mr Ake went to great care that any dietary issues were taken care of. 
Over all too expensive for the result.
reviews.title: Half day tour as advertised.

reviews.text:
We have now gone on two tours, one with Don for half day elephant trekking and one day with Paula for the 3 temples. Don was very funny and happy man- he was a very good guide. He took good care for all of us to make sure we were safe and ok. The elephant trekking was a nice experience and bathing with the elephants nice although i was very cautious.
Paula was also good with the 3 temples tour..i.must say i do not particularly like waiting for guides to have smokes and allowing other smokers in the group to light up whenever we were to head off- i did not find this professional for someone getting paid and it did not make us feel good HOWEVER other than this Paula was an excellent guide and was extremely informative. She answered many questions and has a wonderful heart for animals. I would probably choose an english only speaking tour myself had i known however still enjoyed and learnt a lot. We also enjoyed getting to know one other couple we were with the others didnt want to speak to anyone.
I would recommend this company. The tours are pricey when you think about entry fee to most places is tiny and staff do not get paid much so i am not sure where it all goes
reviews.title: Great tours

reviews.text:
We enjoyed a visit to an elephant camp with Khao Lak Land Discovery. The camp is entirely tourism based, as far as I understand elephants only do tourist trekking. They are fascinating animals and don't look mistreated. I'm glad we saw the elephants and had our track and elephant wash ( in a small concrete pool), however the experience included some hysterical screaming from a lady in a separate tour group - she found it scary and let everyone know. I know if I was an elephant I would have started running away at the sounds she was making. The camp elephants remained calm, good on them. 
Our guide was Italian, on an internship in Khao Lak and spoke English and German. The van was clean and well maintained, there was plenty of water and the lunch was nice. 
Tip:if you wear silk pants hold on to the chair when elephants are walking downhill - it's v slippery!
reviews.title: Good experience

reviews.text: Fantastic day trip to James Bond Island. Vaulker our guide was brilliant and took a lot of time making sure we were looked after and well informed. The only two English tourists, with the other tourists being German, didn't seem to be at all an issue, which was lovely. The whole day was excellent and we highly recommend. Elephant Bathing booked with them for tomorrow and am very excited and confident that we are going with a very high quality tour company. Thank you. Charlotte & Charles
reviews.title: Father and daughter trip to Phang Nga Bay

reviews.text:
We have visited Thailand in April for 15 days and stayed In Haven Khao Lak.

We booked a couple of trips via Khao LAK DISCOVERY and all of them were brilliant, very professional and we always had very friendly person to talk to us all the way and teach us a lot about Thailand culture as well as nature and traditions.

We have done snorkeling trip which was beautiful and we have enjoyed corals very much as well as meeting new people and jumping to the water from the front of the speed boat :)

We have done kanoe trip, James Bond island and floating village and temple in one day (my fav trip) with this lovely guy I name forgot l. He was from Belgium and worked for Khao Lak Discovery and he told us so much about thailand .... not only we had great time we learned a lot of new stuff. He also spoke Thai which was impressive!! 

We have done elephant trekking and bathing with lovely elephant called Zisu ....

Definetly worth your money plus very competitive prices ! 100% happy and recomended xxx
reviews.title: Brilliant Agency

reviews.text:
This was a perfect Trip. Beginning with the pick-up, the transportation, the elephant camp and lunch. 
Ake, our guide was very kindly, told us a lot about the elephants and history of Thailand and cared for us in a perfect way. Organization was very good, food and beverage also. This trip couldn't be better.
reviews.title: Elephant Bath

reviews.text:
Great day, very enjoyable!
Our tour guide 'Don' was brilliant!!!!
Kept us entertained though out the day, was a pleasure to be around!

The toilet facilities at the elephant camp were to our surprise amazing! They were as clean as our hotel!!! Perfect when you are travelling with a 4 year old!

Elephants appear to be treated in a very respectful manner!!!

Must do!!
reviews.title: Half day elephant trek

reviews.text:
We took the OFFROAD SAFARI day tour and it was a day to remember.
The tour includes drive in an open backed jeep, kayaking, lunch, and off road trip through the jungle and national park.
Our tour guide and driver were Rolo and Ice.  THANK YOU for taking the time to show us nature at our pace.  
HIGHLY RECOMMEND this tour with Rolo and Ice - you won't be disappointed
reviews.title: Highlight of our holiday

reviews.text:
Booked the Surin island and Hong discovery tours for a family of four.
The island tour was well run from the bus pick up throughout and had some real added value touches that enhanced the experience. 
Arriving at the jetty for departure there was breakfast laid on with drinks and snacks all provided in the price - a nice touch especially with young children who always need "topping up" with something.
