On the boat drinks and snacks were provided with lunch on the island being the only disappointment (though we were told this was limited due to the facilities provided). That aside there was plenty of time to relax, the boat didn't feel crowded and we were left to explore, swim or just sunbathe at our leisure. 
Unfortunately had to cancel the second tour at very short notice due to an unfortunate accident. Thought this might mean the loss of £250 we had already paid for this but the office happily refunded the full price without question and offered their help if we needed it in sorting anything out after the accident.
reviews.title: Well organised tour with a great customer service

reviews.text: We did the half day elephant tour with Khao Lak Land discovery. It was fantastic. We were picked up right on time and bathing with the elephants was a huge thrill. Our guide Chon was informative and funny. The lunch was delicious - our tour group of 11 were the only ones there. After lunch we went for a walk to a waterfall but unfortunately there wasn't much water. The only thing I was disappointed with was the lady outside the restaurant charging for photos with Slow lorises - not sure if this was organised by the tour company though or if she just frequents the restaurant.
reviews.title: Lovely elephant trip

reviews.text:
Staying at Khao Lak, we took the Phang Nga Bay Discovery tour. Forget your speedboats!!! A small group of 11, our tour guide Volker, a German man now a resident, gave us a great insight  into Thailand, the people, the culture & the religion. His passion was astounding. All round fabulous trip on long tail boat to sea canoes, James Bond Island & fantastic lunch & tour at the sea gypsies village. However, could have missed the monkey temple on the return trip. All in all, we enjoyed the whole day. Thank you Volker!
.
reviews.title: 5 star trip at budget rate

reviews.text: The tour started early for us as we had a 1.5 hour trip to get to the tour starting point. It was well worth it. Hopped on a speedboat for an hour to get to the first island which was phi phi island here we stayed for around 45 minutes, it was breath takingly beautiful. You can go up to a small viewing point. There are toilets and a snack bar here too. Back on the boat for a short time to the next stop where we did some snorkeling which was amazing, we saw so many types of fish and even a turtle! We continued to the other islands for a short time and by 12pm we came to our last island to stop for lunch and a 2 hour free time to spend on the beach and swimming in the sea. The guides spoke very good English and were happy and laughing the whole day through. They were very helpful giving us information and with putting our snorkeling gear on. Would definitely recommend it. A great day out! Take lots of suncream though!!
reviews.title: Island tour and snorkeling.

reviews.text:
After extensive research, we decided that one of our holiday's 2 full-day tours would be the Hong Island Explorer with Khao Lak Land Discovery. I really liked how the trip was planned, the low number of people per trip (12) and the early rise which allowed us not only to visit these beautiful islands, but also to beat the crowds. The higher price was totally worth it!

After a somewhat difficult online communication previous our trip (got an answer to my 2 emails only after sending a reminder each after 4-5 days of the initial one), everything went smoothly and I recommend this trip to anyone interested in seeing the amazing caves and islands that you can barely squeeze into. Our tour-guide Oly (sorry if spelling is incorrect) was a blast! Funny and informative, easy to engage with, always careful with us, explaining what we were seeing.

We had an early hotel pick-up, got on the beautiful traditional wood tail-boat, saw the sunrise while sailing along the mangrove forest which later opened to the amazing Phang Nga Bay. There we visited 3 islands and 1 cave, a sea-gypsy village on stilts where we had lunch and bought some souvenirs. Oly told us the places to avoid because of overpriced items and where we could buy the same at reasonable prices. On our way back to the hotel, we stopped at a waterfall to cool our feet. Throughout the trip, cold refreshments were made available, which was a must once the sun got up on the sky.

Next time we go back, we'll definitely try another trip with Discovery!
reviews.title: Hong Island Explorer

reviews.text: Had a half day trip with the elephant rides and bathing! Elephants were very well looked after and enjoyed stopping for abit to eat of the leaves whilst being rode. Trainers worked well with the animals and was shocked how happy they where of stories you would here !
reviews.title: Elephants where amazing

reviews.text:
Today my partner and I went on the 4x4 Safari. The driver and guides who arrived to pick us up were all polite and friendly. 

We headed to some viewing platforms to see the lush landscapes, before heading off to do a morning of Kayaking. This made even better as you have a guy who paddles you around the river. 

We then stopped for a buffet lunch that was delicious. 

We then headed off to the jungle to go off road and find a waterfall to cool off. 

🙂Pro's - Great day out, great food and drink, you meet some fantastic people! Make some great memories. 

🙁Con's - None really! Just bear in mind the time of year you visit Thailand. We went in April which is the hot, dry season. The rivers were very low so you may have to get out the kayak and push in parts as we did. The walk to the waterfall is steep! 

Recommendation - 

🔵Take some kind of footwear that you feel comfortable walking up and down steep paths to the waterfall, and that you don't mind getting wet. 
🔵Take plenty of sun tan lotion!
🔵Take a camera to capture the day!

Get it booked!!
reviews.title: 4x4 Experience, Kayaking, Great Food

reviews.text: So much fun the elephant trainers were great and funny and took lots of pics eith our own camera. Will never forget this trip for all the right reasons.
reviews.title: Amazing

reviews.text: Elephant bath trip, good price, very professional with an English speaking guide, would highly recommend this company for khao lak tours
reviews.title: Excellent tour company

reviews.text:
Good day trip with a variety of venues .
We visited a couple of temples and a boat trip to James Bond Island . Then onto a canoe trip through the caves. Before visiting the floating village where a very good meal was served .
The transport and guide could not be faulted .
Well worth the 2600 Baht per person
reviews.title: Well organised

reviews.text: We went to ride the elephants and had a great time as my husband was allowed to actually sit behind her ears.  The we went for a leisurely trip along a river in a blow up canoe, very relaxing and peaceful, had a lovely lunch, visited the monkeys at the temple and saw the lovely Khao Lak scenery.  Finally we went to the Tsunami Memorial which was very thought provoking. The guide was very helpful, spoke excellent English and made the day special.
reviews.title: Ride an elephant on its shoulders

reviews.text:
My wife and I bought two tours and a transfer to Phuket airport from this company. We went to Phang Nga Island, Phang Nga Elephant Park (where we played with a baby elephant that was 3 months old).

The Phang Nga Island tour included a canoe ride. The canoe peddler (a thai local) was annoying. He kept pestering me to give him 100 Thai Baht as his tip, during the ride and again at the end of the ride. I ignored him and should have lodged a formal complaint against him, but could not spare the time to do so.
reviews.title: Reliable operator

reviews.text: We used Khao Lak land discovery for 3 trips. Similan island , James Bond island and Phuket sightseeing. They were all very well organised with messages left at the hotel the evening before with pick up times. The guides were very informative and interesting. Very good value for money .
reviews.title: Trips

reviews.text: We chose this tour as we thought it looked like a good mixture of activities and would get us out of our luxury resort. It was a great pick. We were picked up at 7am in an air-conditioned bus and got back to the resort by 4pm. We saw lots of country side, enjoyed the boat ride out to the island with beautiful scenery and then had a canoe ride. Afterwards we had a beautiful lunch at a restaurant at Koh Panyee and then explored the village. Our last stop was a temple with monkeys which the kids were desperate to see. I was a little hesitant going on this tour as a larger lady and just because I'm not good in the heat but it was  manageable and our tour guide handed out lots of water, soda and wet towelettes. We decided that the tour was a great all-rounder and it was enough adventuring for us. Our tour guide and driver were great and I would completely recommend this company and day tour.
reviews.title: James Bond island - day tour.

reviews.text: We can only confirm the earlier comments. We did the Similan island snorkelling tour. A driver picked us up at the hotel on time. We were the first to arrive on Similan island, so we could take nice pictures without the crowd. We had a very bad experience a few days before in a tour to Phi Phi Island, where we paid 2000 Baths, but the boat and all beaches were crowded. So this time we decided to pay more and we don't regret it at all. Also I want to mention that the lunch was very good, compared to other tours we did. And the staff informed us a lot about safety, which we appreciated. We highly recommend this company.
reviews.title: Perfectly organized, professional

reviews.text: Khao Lak Land discovery is a great alternative if you don't want to organize trips on your own. Everything is provided for you, the pick up from the hotel, a lunch and drinks during the trip. Of course you have other traveller with you what might be a problem for some people, but for doing a guided "all inclusive" trip once, I recommend this agency highly. Very friendly and professional guides! A little too expensive though, that's why no 5 stars..
reviews.title: Great for guided day tours!

reviews.text: We did the Half Day Tour Khao Lak Nature Discover which included an elephant trek. Really fabulous day.  The Elephants seem really well looked after and the Mahuts who ran the tour were lovely.  Julie our guide took a lot of time to educate and inform us of the area and how the elephants were looked after.  Would highly recommend this tour.
reviews.title: Fabulous Experience

reviews.text:
We booked a phi phi island tour through this company.  The driver that picked us up fell asleep at the wheel.  My husband had to physically shake him to warn him we were about to hit a car.  I felt very unsafe the entire ride.  The tour itself was fabulous once we arrived the tour was with aqua adventures.  Oh and he was 40 minutes late for pick up.  

I am sure this is just one bad driver but it was my only experience with this company during our stay and I feel obligated to warn others.  Khaolak Vista was much better to work with and I always felt safe.
reviews.title: Careful of Drivers

reviews.text:
Our first time in Thailand and the tours only added to our wonderful stay and experiences of this lovely country.  We booked 2 tours: Full Day Khao Sak Jungle Exploration, this was a lovely day and our Guide Dieter  was great and cared that we enjoyed our day.  Plenty of drinks were supplied free throughout the day.  The market was so enlightening and to see so much fresh food and locals doing their daily shop. Stopped for coffee at the Rock and Tree House then took a trip down the river in a canoe-bit tricky as the levels were low but the local guide paddled, pushed and pulled us over the low bits, stopped again to have a cuppa in bamboo cups and water boiled in bamboo tubes over a fire.  Lots to see on the way, monkeys, snakes, frogs, fish and the scenery was stunning. Back to the tree house for lunch and swim in the pool.  Then to the elephant camp, no babies but well looked after elephants, they were happy, contented and ropes used to tie on the seats were covered in plastic pipe to stop the rope rubbing and cutting into the skin, no evidence of poor treatment.  Fun riding on their neck, not too comfortable but we had a teenager boy who was full of character and made us laugh on several occasions. On the way back we paid tribute at a memorial for the 2004 Tsunami as this area was badly hit.
The next day we went to Phang Nga Bay- "James Bond Island".  Again a lovely day, spectacular scenery.  Longtail boat round the bay and to the island (can get a little wet from the spray), again plenty of free refreshments supplied by the firm. Time to be on the actual island, don't buy anything on this trip as not very good quality and soooo expensive.  Sea Canoe to see the mangroves, caves and cliffs then a superb lunch on the floating village.  Finally a visit to a Temple on the way back with a huge Buddha and macaque monkeys.
From start to finish the firm was very professional, discount made for doing 2 bookings.  Pick up times appropriate to miss the crowds and to get the most out of the days.  Attentive guides and briefing and information from guides excellent.  Only minor irritation was the constant tipping to the locals for their services, it was discretionary but we felt we had to give a tip as they put so much into making your outings enjoyable and they need the money, unsure what the pay is like out there for them.  It is only a little at a time but it does add up.
We would thoroughly recommend  Khao Lak Land Discovery for booking your trips, you wont be disappointed and we will use them again when we return to Thailand as the trips are well organised and has enough variation so you can see and learn about the area and culture.
reviews.title: Brilliant

reviews.text: In January I made a trip to Kao Sok national park with Khao Lak Land Discovery. It was a excellent tour with the right amount of variation. Our guide even  made some changes during the trip to make  it even more suitable to hour taste. It was a tour with a lot of variation and me and my family enjoyed the unexpected journey to Kao Sok Lake a lot. I only would advise them to not ask so much tips for the local people, but give them a fair price as company. I was not used to give tips in Thailand and I know that not all Europeans like to be asked for money.
reviews.title: Kao Sok national park 2 day tour

reviews.text:
give the elephant bath a miss-the elephants were not well treated,were periodically thumped with a blunt instrument & had mark s on their foreheads which were badly covered in an iodine type lotion.
We were there at Christmas and my daughter was upset by the whole experience.
reviews.title: Re -elephant bath

reviews.text:
Second time we have used Land Discovery (first two years ago) and impressed this time as well. We were in Khao Lak for ten days and arranged two trips. The staff in the office on the 'high street' are all very well versed in the product, full of advice and engaging. because it was our second time with them and my wife's 50th birthday, we managed to negotiate a good price on both trips.

Bathing with Elephants - 11:30 pick up from hotel was on-time and the Thai lady guide was brilliant (if not vocal!). The day consists of a quick brief on the camp, a 40 minute or so ride on the elephants up and down a hill and through some 'jungle' and then about 30 minutes in the pool with your elephant which was great. We also saw a month old calf with Mum which was nice. 
From there, it is a 40 minute ride to a waterfall where you can have a cooling dip and then a very good Thai lunch (better than you would expect). Then it is back to the hotel for around 4:00PM.
Note: There is a lot of controversy about the elephants and the way they are trained and treated. All I can say is they appeared to be well treated whilst we were there but there were two that were trained including the nursing mother (maybe to stop her being aggressive with her calf). The animals were all 'friendly' and great to be in the water with. I have to admit though, we would maybe think again about doing this for the third time due to some recent reports. It's a fine balance I guess between the experience and enjoyment and the welfare of the animals themselves.

Surin Islands - Excellent day and the best trip yet. Pick up at 7:30 am from the hotel and a trip to the Northern Pier. Good briefing there, meet the staff and have breakfast (if you have not stuffed yourself at the hotel first!). The boat trip is around 90 minutes in a good sized boat - sit in the front if you can but mind the sun.
The day starts with a 45 minute snorkel, then a trip to the Moken 'Sea Gypsies' village. Then its on to lunch and a great beach via a Long Tail boat transfer. Lunch again was great and well worth the inclusive deal. After that it is back to a different location for another 45 minute snorkel and then return to the mainland, getting back to the hotel at around half four. Water, fizzy drinks, fruit and snacks are supplied freely on the boat each way.
If you are thinking of the Similian Islands or Surin - please do this. We have done both and this is 100% better. Less crowded by far, better snorkelling and better service.

In sum, another two very well organised trips from a company that is experienced and seems to know what it is doing. Well recommended, particularly for these two trips (with the caveat on the elephants)
reviews.title: Elephants and Surin Islands

reviews.text: You've got to do it! Floating down the mild river, you feel truly in the jungle. Birds, lizards, fish and snakes hanging on the trees above your head. If we didn't have a problem with our 2&4 years old, you can do it. Just a little water on your bottom, but that's alright in that heat. The rest of the tour (elephants&turtles) is just mainstream. Enjoy the rafting!
reviews.title: Bamboo Rafting - The Best!

reviews.text:
I have booked two trips with Khao Lak Land Discovery, Similan Islands and Phangnga Park.
They are very professional and try to give you the best experience of Khao Lak surroundings. I really felt the will to give you the best experience trying to avoid the tourist crowd starting earlier that the other groups. You are then first on each spot before the crowd get there. I was not expecting to go on tour with a kind of Wikipedia-Guide, so the explanations I had were good enough to enjoy the places and dig more by myself if wished (google if my friend). As whole,  I would say:
-	Departure on time
-	Try to avoid the crowd
-	Enough explanations on the visited places
-	Food ok for lunch
-	Drinks and snacks all day long
-	They care about you and try to give the best experience
-	German oriented but good English spoken (I’m not German)

Price wise I cannot compare with the other local agencies, but since I was happy with my trips, and based on my past experiences in Thailand I found it good value for money.
I do not see any negative point I could put on their service.
reviews.title: Similar Island and Phangnga

reviews.text:
All I can say is steer clear of this trip if you love elephants.

I thought the elephants were being cared for in a conservation type environment but I couldn't be more wrong. 

The elephants are chained up by one leg and I saw one swaying side to side in a manner which was worrying and suggested ill treatment. 

The elephants are poorly treated as they use a metal hook to hit them with on the jungle trek.

This is a purely money making exercise and I am ashamed for supporting this cruelty.

Avoid at all cost.
reviews.title: Half day elephant safari

reviews.text: Very good half day tour, although you don’t really go to the ‘jungle’ but just up the hill and back down again behind the elephant camp. It is all done very well, although it will cost extra if you buy the basket of bananas to feed the elephants, and the photo they take of you on the elephant. Recommended. A Thai lunch was part of the deal, with transport from your hotel and back again.
reviews.title: Elephant trek

reviews.text:
We recently went on the full day safari- elephant trek, waterfall, lunch, small sandy beach, bamboo rafting and turtle conservation park.
Our tour guide was Don, he was funny and informative and did well with his English. We felt very looked after by him and also the bus driver. They give you wet face towels, water and cola throughout the day.
Elephant trekking was enjoyable, about an hour but you don't go heaps far as the elephant stops a lot to eat and poo along the way!! 
They take a photo of you that you can purchase for 250 baht at the end. Toilets at elephant ride were clean and nice.

Next was the waterfall and lunch. Lunch was rice, massaman curry, vegetables, chicken nuggets and chicken soup. Was very nice. Waterfall is quite small but still nice- you are able to swim in it. 

After we went to the national park and visited a beach called 'small sandy beach'. The tour guide pays 200 baht for you to enter and walks down to the beach with you. It's about a 10 minute walk through the jungle/trees, before you reach a cute little secluded beach. They have drinks for sale down there. When we were there, there was only about 10 other people on the beach so it was nice and private and the water clear and beautiful to swim in. The walk back is quite steep but the guide encourages you to take your time about again it only takes 10 min.

After this we drove to bamboo rafting! Highlight of day!!! The driver of the bamboo raft guides the raft down the river through some small Rapids and peaceful pools of water. Lasts for half an hour then Don the guide meets you again at the other end.

Lastly was a quick stop to the turtle park. This is an army area where they are protecting species of turtle and looking after sick turtles before releasing them into the wild again. You can see turtles of all sizes, all in pool like tanks. It's no sea-world but Don was informative and it was still interesting. Only there for about 10 min.

Overall a wonderful day and felt it was worth the money as we saw/experienced a lot. Would definitely recommend this tour company :)
reviews.title: Safari full day tour

reviews.text:
Went to Surin island snorkelling with them. Great experience. Our guides were happy and energetic. Could see they enjoyed their jobs and rightfully so. Surin islands are magnificent.
 Speedboat was good, plenty of snacks and drinks onboard and decent lunch. 
Only thing to complain about is that i would have preferred more snorkelling time as there were plenty to see but not enough time to do so. (2x45mins) But i guess for older people it was fine.

Sadly had problems with our trip dvd delivery, that kind of left bad taste in the mouth. They promised to send it to our hotel whereever it may be. But next morning they called the hotel saying it was too far away and they cant deliver there.. In the end we had to go pick it up ourselves covering all the transport costs to the pier.. Very unprofessional by the video company.
reviews.title: Excellent, but beware of the video company.

reviews.text: We had a guide named Toin, short for Antoin. He had been a Buddhist monk for 12 years and he was very knowledgable about the Buddha.  We were a group of 8 and were taken to two cave temples, one was very high in the mountains. The other was very beautiful and expansive. The place we had lunch served us an incredible meal that we will never forget! The place is on a river with gorgeous views. At the last cave temple, the monk gave us a special blessing.
reviews.title: AMAZING THREE TEMPLE TOUR WAS REALLY AMAZING!

reviews.text:
Absolutely Brilliant. Our favourite part of our holiday. 

We had an early pick up at 4.45, so were a bit apprehensive as to what the day would bring. We went by minivan to Bang Nga stopping to pick up the rest of our group (8 in total) on the way. We then stopped for coffee before driving to the pier. 

Our transport was a large longboat which was half open at the front with a cabin in the other half so lots of room for us all to sit in the sun or shade as we preferred. We sailed first to "Ice Cream Cave" where our excellent tour guide Raj gave us a tour by foot. He explained that the reason we left so early, was to miss all the crowds who would come in the afternoon. How right he was!

Next on to Hong Island where we got off our long tail boat into 4 inflatable canoes each paddled by Raj, the captain and two crew members. We got to go inside the island and around under some narrow tunnels/caves. Then back on the boat to James Bond Island which by 10am was still quiet before the hoards were due to descend. Next stop was another island for more canoeing around the island and through mangrove trees (really pretty)

Finally we went to the Floating Village for our lunch, which was an excellent sharing meal for the 8 of us. We then had an hour to wander around the village and buy souvenirs, visit the mosque, football stadium, etc. In your own time as you wished. From the restaurant we could see boat after boat speeding towards the islands, so knew we would have been crammed in the different sites if we'd done the trip in the afternoon instead of the morning.

Then back to the mainland and the minibus to drop us back to our hotel for 2.30. Couldn't praise Raj and his crew highly enough. For 2700tbh this was by far the best value and most enjoyable trip we did and was all the more special because we were really looked after and didn't feel rammed in for maximum profit like on some other tours.

Thank you Khao Lak Land Discovery!
reviews.title: Hong Island - Amazing

reviews.text: We absolutely loved this trip.  Picked up at designated time from our hotel in an air conditioned van.  Our tour guides Noy and Tommy were very friendly and informative and we knew exactly how the day was going to be run.  The trip to the islands takes about 1 hour via speed boat.  Cold drinks and food are always available on the boat.  The beaches are spectacular and the snorkelling is beautiful.  Lunch is provided and there is plenty of time at each stop that you don't feel rushed.  The whole day is amazing.
reviews.title: Similan Islands snorkelling trip

reviews.text: We did the elephant bath in Khaolak and also the phi phi island snorkelling tour. Both were wonderful experiences and I would recommend both tours to families staying in Khaolak. My 2 children aged 10 and 11 raved about the tours and we have very fond memories of our time in Khaolak thanks to Khao lak discovery tours.
reviews.title: Great tours with wonderful tour guides

reviews.text:
My partner and I booked the 'James Bond Island' Tour and it was one of the highlights of our holiday.
We were picked up right on time and made our way to Phang Nga Bay where our long tailed boat was waiting for us. The trip over to Ko Tapu, James Bond Island took around 45 minutes, the scenery was stunning and it was great to be able to spend an hour on the island. After that we took the boat over to a mangrove forest where we got into canoes and explored the area, this was magical, just beautiful. We then made our way to Ko Panyi which is a floating village overlooked by a fabulous Mosque, here we walked around the village and had lunch, which was probably one of the best meals we've ever had (and we eat out a lot!). Once back at Phang Nga Bay our minibus took us to a Buddist temple where we could climb through the caves and have a Buddist blessing by a resident Monk. 
All food and drinks were included in the price and the trip was great value for money. Our guide Cha Lin (or Charlie as he's also known in case I've mis-spelt his name) was knowledgeable and friendly. We were so pleased with our day we went back to Khao Lak the next day to thank the office staff for such a memorable experience.
We'd highly recommend booking trips through Khao Lak Land Discovery, we'll be using their services again.
reviews.title: Fabulous trip

reviews.text:
My 10 year old daughter and I did this tour yesterday, booked through our hotel. We had a 5.50am collection from the hotel to ensure we got the right tide to access the caves. The bus ride to the pier is approximately 1 hour and our guide Noi(?) barely spoke a word the whole journey. Just before we stopped, there was a quick pit stop for ice at a nearby 7-11. If you haven't brought snacks, I recommend you buy some here. It's a long morning before lunch. Water and cola are provided and a very small Thai snack around 11.30am but nothing substantial. 

At the pier there are clean toilets. I recommend you use them as again, it's a while before you get another opportunity. 

We boarded a boat (not the long tail).  It was very slow, noisy and extremely uncomfortable seating. Take a towel to sit on or even better, a cushion. It's a two hour journey up the river to the bay and into the caves. However it is fascinating to see the tidal change, the bird life, the mangroves and later in the bay the limestone formed islands. 

On arrival at the island we boarded our own canoes and the guide, boat driver and another took us inside the caves as there were 6 of us on the tour, 2 to a canoe plus canoeist. Depending on the tide, some are not accessible. We went into two caves, diamond cave and mangrove cave. The latter we walked into as the tide too low for the canoe.  They were really interesting and fascinating to visit.  The highlight of the trip.  Our guide gave some interesting tidbits of information but nothing substantial. After about 45 minutes we boarded our boat again and went to Hong Island where there were many more tourists, many arriving on speed boats. Amongst many other groups, we had another sea canoe into another cave as well as an opportunity to have a go yourself. Some people on other boats swam but the water wasn't terribly clear and didn't look too inviting. We had around 45 minutes here. Then back on the boat, (11.30am) and mini Thai snacks handed out before heading back. We passed James Bond island but did not get off although there were obviously hundreds of other tour groups who had. 

Around 12pm we arrived at the Floating Village where we stopped for a very delicious lunch. Toilets available here but not very clean. We were then given over an hour to explore the markets and the island, way too long as it is not very big and nearly every stall sells the same thing. Try to visit the mosque and the school. This is a Muslim island so no alcohol and a slightly different feel to mainland Thailand. I read a very interesting history of the island at the mosque but again, nothing from the guide as to its origins and way of life, which would have added so much more to the experience. 

A much quicker boat journey back as the tide was higher, however it was obvious we were on the slowest boat in the water as even the long tails overtook us. This probably adds at least an hour to the day, in which there is an awful lot of sitting. At least the boat was big enough to wander around unlike all other boats. 

We got back to the pier around 2.00pm and then an hour back to the hotel in the bus. 

This trip had the potential for so much more. An interesting guide that provides ongoing commentary and background information can add so much more to the experience. Unfortunately our guide was little more than a facilitator. 

A note on tips. Another couple on our tour was asked by their canoeist for a 200 baht tip. They didn't give it. They had been told by another guide on a previous tour that tipping is not necessary. Personally, if the service and in particular the guide is excellent and really adds to the trip, then I tend to tip about 10% of the trip cost but in this instance I didn't as the trip was already very expensive compared to others and I wouldn't consider good value for money.
reviews.title: Program 3 Phang Nga Bay Hong Island

reviews.text:
My wife and I used this tour company in nov 2013. we booked a full day tour which included bamboo rafting, tsunami memorial and elephant trekking.The price included lunch at a restaurant too. We happened to be the only people on the tour so we were given the choice as to which way round to do the tour and any extra stops we wanted. We started by going to do bamboo rafting. prepare to get wet though, once at the location you were passed to a guide who steers the raft down the river and through rapids, steering under the trees to point out the snakes hidden away. following this we took a detour to pay our respects to the victims of the tsunami. whilst a sombre location its powerfully moving to see the police boat which was washed 1km inland and remains in the same place as where it came to rest. memorials and photos surround the area, take your time to soak in the moving stories and pictures. Our guide for the day spoke excellent English and explained where he was during the disaster, totally captivating and powerfully moving. from here we went to a small beach, it involved a steady walk down a hill through woodland where we came across a few ani,als and insects. once at the beach we had spare time to swim and sun bathe.....we then went for lunch in a lovely local restaurant near a beautiful waterfall, we sampled traditional thai cusine which included a curry and some rice amongst other things such as soup. we left very full......lastly we went to the elephant park where we took a 1.5hrs ride aboard a big beautiful elephant. the animals are incredibly well looked after and treated like part of the family which is great to see. the staff don't do the usual thai tourist thing by allowing you to sit on the animals neck as this is not comfortable for the elephant. again this shows hoe they care for the animals there.at the end we fed the elephants by hand. 
This tour was highlight of our whole holiday and as Khao Lak is a very quiet area it was a great way to break up the sun bathing on the beautiful thai beaches.
Booking was easy from start to finish, we also used the company for the airport transfer....highly recommended.
reviews.title: Great day tours

reviews.text: great Van, very nice driver, good organization by email with reminders before each transfers. Payment after the transfer. We would re-use this service as it was excellent.
reviews.title: Transfer by Hyundai H1 van from Phuket airport to Khao Lak and back

reviews.text: We did the half day tour with the elephant ride followed by bathing/ swimming with the elephants in the stream. We had previously done elephant rides before but this was a totally unique experience that we would recommend to anyone. The ride through the bush & then on the beach was great with plenty of time for photos, then back to the compound for refreshments whilst the elephants were readied for the bathing time. Heading to the stream for a further 30 minutes in the water with these magnificent animals including bathing, riding, cuddling, scrubbing left a never ending smile & great memories. Plenty of time for photo's. on the way back to the resort we stopped at a waterfall although due to the recent heavy rain was a bit strong. Our tour guide was exceptional in his knowledge regarding the elephants. Highly recommend this tour & company.
reviews.title: Unreal experience

reviews.text:
We did three day tours with this company (Amazing 3 Temples, Phang Nge Bay Hong canoe Discovery and the Elephant Bath tour, all unforgettable experiences.  All guides were great particularly Twain who took us on the temple tour.  What an amazing human being and guide. Prices were good but take tipping money.  The canoe tour was worth getting up early for, an absolutely wonderful experience.  Food on these tours was 5 class. Very well organised and professional company.
Paula Goncalves was the representative for this company at the Mai Khoa Lak resort and I cant recommend her high enough.  Paula went above and beyond to make our holiday experience wonderful.  Thankyou so much Paula.  Your an amazing girl. x
reviews.title: "Three amazing tours"

reviews.text:
I had the time of my life. We did the elephant bathing half day trip, we came from Phuket especially for this and I have to admit it was beyond our imagination ! We started by the trekking in the jungle, I had the chance to be directly behing the ears of the elephant (and not just sitting on the chair) while the Marhout was taking a hundred of pictures of us with our camera ! Then, as soon as we are back to the camp, you just have the time to change your clothes, the elephants directly go to their pool ! And they enjoy it ! We spent 30min with them in the water, playing with them, sitting on them in the water.... That's a great experience, something you do only once in your life !
Finish by the waterfall which are not that interesting but beautiful, and then you have a HUGE lunch to finish your day ! It was so good !

Thank you so much to this company that made our day magical !
reviews.title: A unique experience

reviews.text:
We went elephant trekking and canoeing with Khao Lak land discovery, excellent day would recommend to all, Gift was a great guide, she was funny and made us all feel very welcome we even found time to stop and feed the monkeys which was great 10/10
Thanks
reviews.title: Elephant trekking

reviews.text:
We did three one day tours with this company (off road safari, Khao Sok and Phang Nga) and all were fantastic! All of the guides were great but a special mention to Eric on the Khao Sok tour, whose knowledge and enthusiasm was infectious.  
The groups were small and because the guides know the area well, we were able to experience the best elements of southern Thailand.   Prices were good and the food for lunch was excellent.  They even faxed and called the hotel to confirm the details for our third trip which had an early start. 
We did another tour with a different company which was awful.  Definitely wished we had stayed with KLLD.
reviews.title: Three fantastic tours.

reviews.text: Staff at our hotel managed to book us onto this trip on the wrong day so we got an unexpected early morning wake up call one morning but the tour company were very accommodating and rebooked us for the day we initially requested. 5am start to ensure we could visit Hong Island around the tide (only accessible at low tide) then some kayaking with a guide then on our own for approx an hour. Chance to take photos of James Bond Island but didn't actually stop there. Amazing experience non the less. Our tour guide Tim spoke fluent German and English and gave a mixed group of us the full history of the island and caves. Tim took great joy in steering the kayak under a spiders web (complete with poisonous spider the size of my hand) before laughing hysterically as my husband and I nearly capsized the kayak. Then onto the floating village where we stopped for a lovely Thai lunch and the chance to look around the shops. On the way back we stopped at a temple then fed the monkeys which gathered around outside. Arrived back at our hotel at approx 3pm. Overall, great day out and highly recommended.
reviews.title: Phang Nga & Hong Island

reviews.text:
Hello, 

Thanks for the wonderful trip e had with you on the Khao Lak Safari! A special thank to Erich for his very interesting and qualified guidance! Absolutely recommendable!
reviews.title: Great trip

reviews.text: We opted for the half day elephant trek tour. Glad we did! All in all a great experience. Picked up on time and take  to the elephant camp which was about an jour from khao lak. The camp was small but the elephants seemed well looked after. the elephant trek was simple a walk up a hill and back down and was a little disappointing and the mahout did stop and smoke an awful lot he did take tons of photos for us which was great. I thought the photograph that they took was lovely and 250baht  was very reasonable. We saw it as more a donation to the elephant camp. The tour then took us for lunch at a little hidden waterfall which was lovely and the traditional Thai food was delicious.  Great value and a great day out.
reviews.title: A bit "toursit-y" but a good experience

reviews.text:
We went on the Nature Discovery tour this week which included return hotel pick up, Bamboo Rafting through a jungle river, a visit to the Sea Turtle Conservation Park at the Naval Base, Small Sandy Beach, visit to a small waterfall where we also had lunch and then an elephant trek.

As it is wet season, it did rain on and off throughout the day but our awesome Tour guide Eric provided some waterproof jackets for the day. He translated in both German and English - his English was easy to understand. We were picked up at 8:20am on the dot in an air conditioned mini van. It was a full bus, two other couples and a grandad with his 2 grandchildren.

Bamboo raft was just amazing. Make sure you wear bathers & thongs or clothes and shoes you're willing to get dirty. You will get pretty wet from waist down but it is amazing. I am petrified of snakes but we were told we would be able to see monkeys, snakes and other animals. 100THB ($4 AUS) tip for the person steering your raft is respectful and appreciated. They also take photos of you and you are able to buy them for 250THB. 

Sea Turtle Conservation Park was OK. The turtles were cute but personally felt it was time wasted as once you see a few of the big turtle 'pools' there's not much else to do.

The hike to Small Sandy beach is through a steep-ish jungle/forest. Once again, nothing too flash about the beach in wet season. Sneakers would've been good for this walk. Its approx 300-400m but with bad knees and ankles + wearing thongs it was a bit of a struggle.

I personally didn't see much as the waterfall as my ankle couldn't handle the stairs to get down and back up again but from what I heard it was okay. Depending on the weather sometimes the fish are in there to eat the dead skin on your feet but they don't like the rain.

Lunch was included in the tour price and was had at a homestyle restaurant next to the waterfall. We had a variety of dishes including masaman curry, a vegetable dish, crumbed chicken and a soup. All tasted lovely.

The elephant trek was great. As it was wet the ground was muddy which elephants don't like. It was around 45-60 mins. Lots of stopping for the elephants toilet stops along the way. The workers are more than happy to take photos of you on your phone or camera. We found them very giggly and happy. Elephants seem healthy and happy. You can pay 100THB for a basket of bananas to feed them after which is also amazing. Once again a 100THB tip for your elephant rider is respectful and appreciated. We purchased the photo in a frame made out of the elephant dung for a souvenir/keepsake. 250THB and I love it. Though I was curious about getting it through customs and bringing back into Australia. I'm still curious about it as we don't leave until the 22nd. Fingers crossed, otherwise will just keep photo.

Overall it was definitely worth the cost of the trip. Very good value for money and a very active & tiring day. Approx 8am-5pm.
100% recommend.
reviews.title: Khao Lak Nature Discovery (Bamboo Raft & Elephant Trek)

reviews.text:
We went on a full day tour which included bamboo rafting/visit to turtle conservation centre/visit to waterfall/lunch & elephant trek.

Bamboo rafting: highlight of the tour. Very safe as water in most parts is only ankle deep. Got a wet butt sitting on the raft, but all part of the experience

Turtle conservation centre: I was really looking forward to this but was a little disappointed. Turtles in tanks swimming around. No one from the centre to explain what happens to the turtles etc which would have been informative.

Waterfall: just a small waterfall....nothing to get excited over

Lunch: Nice homemade Thai lunch served in local home style restaurant, followed by fresh fruit & coffee 

Elephant trek: a little disappointed. Our one hour trek was more like half an hour with LOTS of stops. The guides were more than happy to take photos on your cameras as you went along however there were no professional photographers there to capture any photos for you (as there was on the rafting). Also no run down at the end of how long the elephants have been there or what they feed on daily etc....might have been nice to have had some commentary about the park/elephants.

Overall it was a good experience..... Our driver was good & the English speaking guide was fairly easy to understand.
reviews.title: Good tour

reviews.text:
Our tour for the Elephant Bath of 2 adults and 2 kids didn't start off great with the driver picking us up 30 mins late. He then picked up someone about 5 mins drive up the road who after about 10 mins drive we found out was our tour guide. He then told us it was about 30-40 mins to get there. it took another hour. there wasn't much communication on the way there but plenty on the way back. It was just nice to see the scenery.
Once there the Elephant camp seemed nice but because we were late the mahouts were waiting so we were rushed straight onto the elephants. So no real initial interaction.
The walk up around the hill was ok at best. You have to pay 100thb for bananas half way up and once finished 250 thb for photos if you want them.
The bathing with the elephants was the best part.
Afterwards we were taken to a not so big waterfall and had lunch next door at a small restaurant which was nice.

Overall the Elephants do not look happy and I wouldn't do this again.
reviews.title: Elephant Bath

reviews.text:
We (5 adults & 3 kids) joined the tour which included elephant trekking, lunch, visit waterfall.  Overall, everything was well organised at a good standard.  

Transportation - safe driver, cosy van, reasonable distance
Tour guide - nice & friendly, good advice
Elephant trekking - 3 elephants for us, clean, walked through a path with ups and downs, quite a wonderful experience, but the elephant trainers smoked and were just so so....
Lunch - very nice in a local restaurant next to the waterfall, quiet, simple & relaxing
Waterfall - a small scale, and the guide only spared 5 minutes for us to take a look
Others - water, umbrella, free fruit were provided
reviews.title: Half day tour - worth to try

reviews.text:
2 trips booked 
First elephant trek and bath a bit of a disappointment weather didnt help .
Guide and driver great ,tour not so good 
Typical tourist trap tour  but for what it cost when will you ever get a chance to get up close and interact with these magnificent creatures.
Phang nga island tour no 3 highly recommend tour guide was great with a good sense of humour  amazing scenery  even a bonus stop cant say anymore incase the guys get in trouble
reviews.title: Good guides and transport

reviews.text: A excellent day out exploring on the back of an Elephant, we had a great Mahoot who took loads of pictures for us along the way. Small group of us (8) picked up from our hotel and away we went, water provided throughout the day. After riding the elephants, they had a bath and we all hopped in and helped wash (and play) with them.  Fantastic. We then went for lunch at a little hidden out of the way restaurant in the hills where we had a lovely feast of massaman curry, chicken, thai soup, rice, and pineapple.  A great day out, well recommended.
reviews.title: Elephant Bath

reviews.text: Enjoyable 4x4 trip to the waterfall, though not a true 4x4 required as dodgy roads were the most hectic terrain covered and these were easily traversed by locals on scooters who zipped past us. Lunch was delicious and plentiful. Canoe trip was relaxing and picturesque. Beware when feeding the monkeys that they don't bite your fingers while trying to grab the food bag from your hands. Stay standing and you should be fine. There was a bit of a mix up with our trip- somehow they assumed we were German and put us with a German guide and all German tourists. Not ideal as we missed out on a lot of chatter and felt a bit excluded. The guide seemed friendly enough but at the end of the trip we were the last to be dropped off and he complained and belittled tips left for him by the other travellers leaving us with a bit of a sour after taste for the day.
reviews.title: Good day out

reviews.text:
I was very pleased with this tour. This was one of the highlights of our visit to Phuket Island. I went with my wife and 4-year-old daughter. My daughter went free of charge. The ride on the elephant was very pleasing, feeding bananas to the elephants was a nice experience. Bring bananas or some spare money to buy bananas during the trekking. Our elephant sure enjoyed all the bananas we feed him/her. The elephant bathing was great, bring a go-pro for photos. Do not forget mosquito repelant as well. 

You will love this trip :)
reviews.title: Do not miss if you are in Khao Lak

reviews.text: 8am-5pm Full day service. Eric our guide was very knowledgeable and translated in English and German all day. The raft ride was fun and my hubby and I both loved the experience. We were very impressed with the turtle rehabilitation centre at the Naval base. The elephant trek was a trip highlight for me. The animals were all very loved and I was encouraged to climb onto my elephants head for the ride. Very scary at first ( so high off the ground) but I was so glad I did as it was a very up-close and personal experience for me. The trek went for about an hour into the jungle and then we purchased a basket of bananas (100 baht $4 Australian) and fed them to our elephants a treat for them. We had the opportunity to purchase a framed photo (250 baht $10 Australian) which I definitely wanted- keepsake. Brisbane airport customs said that we couldn't bring into Australia but after showing the frame to them they actually said our frame was ok to enter Australia as it had been dyed, I was very relieved! Tour was an absolute Thailand highlight.
reviews.title: Must do....favourite day in Thailand.

reviews.text: What a truly wonderful series of trips. Can't thank the team enough. Worth every penny, thanks a lot .,
reviews.title: Great Fun.,

reviews.text:
Thankyou Eric (tour guide) and Lung (bus drive).  James Bond Island not really much to see but Eric managed to make a very informative tour of the mangroves and stilt village with delicious lunch.  We were the only two non German speaking in the group, Eric made sure we were given information in English and we had a great time. Lung took us into the reclining Buddha cave temple.  Monkeys everywhere, be careful.
Go on an elephant ride - a great experience. We loved our holiday in Khao Lak.
reviews.title: Elephant ride, bathing and waterfall lunch.  James Bond Island.

reviews.text:
Recently took the Amazing Temples tour,  it was brilliant.  Our guide Renatta was great and was passionate about the experience.  We had a great lunch at a seafood resterant which was fantastic and had a wonderful outlook over the river.
Would recommend highly to anyone visiting the area
reviews.title: Amazing Temples tour

reviews.text: Advertised as a 3 hour event in reality trekking lasted 15/20 mins and bathing 15 mins.  An interesting and expensive experience. We Treked through brush ( make sure you use insect repellant) bought baskets of banana and pineapple peels and then back to the mini bus and taken to the bathing pool which was relaxed and clearly enjoyable to the elephants. The English speaking guide showed utube clips of elephants cycling really in  poor taste.
reviews.title: Elephant trekking and bathing

reviews.text: My family (hubbie and two boys aged 3 and 5) did the half day Khao Lak Nature Tour and we loved it and highly recommend it.  The guide was so friendly, the tour was professionally organised, we arrived at the elephant safari and went off with two elephants on a safari trek.  The boys loved it and so did my husband and I, it was a lot of fun and a once in a lifetime adventurous excursion we all did together.  We then got to feed them a bucket of bananas, take photos, have a little rest/ice creams/drinks/pineapple at the little café.  We then moved on to view the turtles in the conservation centre which was educational and then went to a really nice outdoor restaurant for lunch (organised through the tour and included in the price).  The food was really delicious, and we were just a small group, not a huge throng of people.  I highly recommend this excursion, we all had a lot of fun, and came home with some photos and great memories.
reviews.title: Elephant Trekking was such great fun

reviews.text:
We did four trips with Khao Lak Land Discovery.

Koh Tachai  01/04/15 – this was our first trip and a bit of a taster as we were worried about sea sickness.  We need not have worried as we both took Sturgeon and it worked perfectly.  An amazing trip overall.  Early start to the companies’ own pier, then a short wait while the last passengers arrived.  We were offered food and drink and they have great toilet facilities here.

The boat took about 1H15 to get to Koh Tachai and we were the first on the beach, so had it to ourselves for quite a while.  A real bonus.  Then a quite tour through the forest on a circular track to see any wildlife such as chicken crabs and monitor lizards and if you are luck a nicobar pigeon.

Then we were given an option to stay on the beach or go to the first snorkel session.  We came to snorkel so were went.  Visibility was good and once again the first boat there so brilliant.

Saw loads of fish, octopus, lobster and a reef shark.

Back to the island for lunch, which was cooked by the island staff – good food and lots of it.

After lunch we walked to a view point on the other side of the island, worth the walk as we were alone for a lot of it.  Then a 1pm pick up went wrong as I think the captain of the boat fell asleep – this moved to 2:30pm when he woke up!  Funny in hindsight and at least we were on a beautiful island!

Second snorkel was just as good, bit more crowded as we were late! Then one more stop at a sandbank on the way back.  Overall a brilliant day out, the guide was good and spoke both German and English in equal measure without any concern.

Khao Sok – Tree & Rock House and Boathouse 03/04/15 – 05/04/15.

The highlight of our holiday.  Day one was a decent time pick up by Julie and Sophon.  Both these people are a credit to the organisation and made our tour.  Elephant rides, lunch and check in at the tree house, canoe rides and trips to supermarkets to stock up.

Throughout the day Julie gave us information about Thailand and it’s history and nature as well as handy hints such as putting electrolytes in the water.  The Treehouse was an amazing experience we had room 8 which was one of the best as it was at the back and had great outlooks across the forest.  

We saw many bats coming out the caves and a squirrel type animal several times.  Food was great with lunch being a particular favourite of my wife.  

In the free afternoon we walked to the temple down the road and fed the monkeys with peanuts and bananas.  As with feeding any wild animal – be very careful and watch your kids, especially with bananas.  We also walked down to the river, but as it was dry season and a very dry season it was very low.  And night was coming with the mosquitoes!

Day two we went on a rather tame jungle walk, but it ended up at a nice waterfall which you can climb if you have a head for heights, more so on the way down.  

Then a jungle BBQ, with rice and chicken cooked on the BBQ in an interesting way, as well as other Thai dishes.   After lunch the other people in our party returned to their hotels as they were scared by the night on the boathouse!

We went back to the Treehouse for a quick shower that Julie arranged for us and then we drove off to the Lake.  Wow, just wow!  If you can do this trip do it!  Or even just the boathouse or the day trip!  We loved in there and would return in an instant.

The boathouse was amazing, basic with two mattress on the floor, but had it’s own internal toilet!  A bonus.  (Smiley Lakehouse).  It was well run and the food was the best food we had on the whole holiday!  A great afternoon spent swimming and canoeing and then a sundown boat ride where we saw hornbills – also just wow!

No aircon so was warm, but a 6am canoe to watch the sun come up and then a swim followed by an amazing breakfast made it all worthwhile.

Off on a early morning jungle walk, early was best as you heard all the noises of gibbons etc in the jungle.  Although the route is well treaded it was fun and good to be out of the sun.  Ended in a cave, with cave spiders and loads of bats!  You can go through, but we did not.

Back to a different boathouse for lunch.  The Smiley Boathouse had 12 houses and was quiet and peaceful.  This one had 40+ houses and catered more for the backpacking scene, so was noisy and busy.  But lunch was okay.  Great to swim off and relax.

Then we travelled back to Khao Lak.

Best time ever!  Thanks Julie and Sophon.

Phang Nga Bay with Sea Canoe.  08/04/15

An easier trip with a decent pick up time.  About an hour to the dock in Phang Nga, followed by an hour to the sea canoe.  Dock at big ferry type boats and then canoe though caves and around some Karsks.  Fun time, spoilt a bit by being asked for a tip, but not really an issue.

Then on to James Bond Island, which is very scenic, but very busy!  Worth it though and got some good photos.

Lunch was at Koh Panyee – the food and service was great and interesting to wonder around the “island”.

Back home via the mangrove.

Similain Islands 09/04/15

Early pick up and transport to the same pier as Koh Tachai.  Similar briefing, but as the only English speaking family we did feel a bit left out as most of the information given was in German and then one sentence in English.  So most of the time we did our own thing and just found out the times we needed to be back at the boat.

1H15 to our first stop at “Donald Duck” Bay, Wow and Amazing all in one!  Scenic and white so white sand.  Walked up to the rock and as we were early we were virtually alone!

Then a short swim in the sea and then off to first snorkel stop.  It turns out that they did not have enough flippers – bad organisation as they knew how many people were coming – I was annoyed as I had my own, but after the Koh Tachai trip I thought I would not bring them as they had loads!

Finally found a pair stuck in the corner.  Once again when handing them out it was only in German so we did not know the sizes so could not get any.  

The snorkelling was great, better than Koh Tachai, visibility was better and we saw much more.

Off to lunch, we were offered a choice between a deserted beach or the naval area which would be busy, up to us, to tell the guide later.  But when it came to it there was no choice – we went to the busy spot.  The food was food bought with on the boat so was not very warm, but it was okay, worst food we ate on the whole holiday though.  Disappointing after seeing what they could do on a boat house in the middle of nowhere. 

Second snorkel session made up for it all though as we dived with Sea Turtles – what an experience.  First boat there again.  Which was brilliant.

1H20 back, got a bit rougher, but the captain tried his best to make it less rough.

18H00 back at hotel – loads of drop offs!

I would recommend all four trips that we did. They were all worth it.  But I would triple recommend the Khao Sok trip with Julie!  Thanks you helped make our holiday!
reviews.title: Recommend Fully - Julie is Tops!

reviews.text:
My girlfriend and I had a great day in the jungle on our Jeep Safari. Pick up from our hotel was on time and although the drive into the National Park was quite long, it was comfortable and we did stop for refreshments. There was also a cool box full of ice cold drinks on board.

Once in the jungle our guides were very good at stopping and pointing out different things from lizards, birds, butterflies and spiders. They even managed to coax a big tarantula spider out of its nest.

The stop at the waterfall was beautiful. We had a short walk down a fairly steep slope to get to it but once safely down we were rewarded with cool water from the falls. We spent about 30 minutes there, which was probably about enough.

Then it was back in the jeep, out of the jungle and on to our lunch stop. This took about an hour but again we stopped for snacks on the way. Tip for you, if you buy packets of crisps at the road side shops, buy 3 or 4 packs as each pack only has about 8 crisps in.

Lunch was in the companies Tree House Resort which looked very nice. The food was very good and plentiful. There was a also a shower which was nice to wash off the dust from the jungle.

After lunch we drove for a further 30 minutes or so to the river where we were to be kayaking. Apart from having to stop on various occasions as the river wasn't deep enough, this was great fun. And to be fair, the guides did a great job of helping everyone who got stuck. They we're also very good at spotting various types of wildlife, including making a fairly large snake fall out of a tree we were all underneath. This they found very amusing while all of us tourists were screaming. 

At the end of the kayaking there was time to dry off, get changed into dry clothes (if like us you had them) and enjoy some refreshments on the river bank, before getting back in the jeep for the long drive back to the hotel.

All in all we had a great day and we have no problem recommending this trip. Be prepared to spend a lot of time in the back of the jeep, so make sure you have good sun screen and comfortable clothes. 

Tips -

Take a spare set of dry clothes in a waterproof bag. This will make the long drive back to your hotel much more comfortable.

Start the day wearing trainers or walking shoes/boots, you'll need these for the walk down to the waterfall.

Where flip flops or shoes you don't mind getting drenched for the kayaking, as there will be times you have to get out of the kayak and the river bed is uncomfortable for bare feet.
reviews.title: Great day in the Jungle on our Jeep Safari.

reviews.text: Brilliant, the scenery is simply fantastic. Take the oportunity to ride the elephants and the canoe ride. An experience never to forget.
reviews.title: Book This Excursion

reviews.text:
I'll start with the positives: the scenery we saw was stunning - the islands and crystal clear water are postcard perfect. The snorkeling itself wasn't great as a lot of the coral has been destroyed, but there were some pretty fish around

Unfortunately the negatives outweigh the positives: when we arrived at the pier where the boat departs, we and about 50 other people had to sit and wait for an hour until a (very) late  transfer arrived carrying the rest of the guests. They were late because the guests themselves weren't ready for their pick up. Sorry but you shouldn't make all your other guests wait an HOUR just for a few. Other tours I have been on make a point of not doing this  
The speedboat we were on felt overcrowded with approx 25 guests + crew onboard. In retrospect I'd pay more for a tour with less people. 
The lunch that was provided was awful... I love Thai food but I could barely eat any of it. 4 dishes were provided - 3 were seafood and one was chicken. I'm not vegetarian but the fact they didn't provided even 1 vego option was crap, as the chicken and seafood options were gross - the chicken was boiled and a horrible grey colour that still had the skin on. 2 seafood dishes contained shellfish which I can't eat (not their problem, I know) but the fish dish was full of tiny bones and not a nice flavor. I ate the steamed rice. 
During the whole day, we only got to do 1 hour of snorkelling (2 half hour swims) which was super disappointing. 
Then as we were making our way back to the mainland, we ran out of petrol!!! So our boat was stranded about 15 minutes from land and we had to wait another half hour for about carrying fuel to come save us. 
I do not recommend this tour at all!
reviews.title: Disappointing snorkelling trip to Surin Islands

reviews.text:
My wife and I went on the half day elephant trek in Khao Lak.

We saw a waterfall before going to the elephant trek. The waterfall was pretty but not too much to see. At the time of our visit, there hadn't  been much rain so the waterfalls were lacking compared to usual apparently.

We stayed at the waterfalls for around 30 mins before heading off for around 15 mins to the elephant trek.

We were in minibus with two other couples, so six people in total.

Once we arrived at the elephant trek we got straight to it, and got onto the elephants. 

Each elephant has a seat strapped on top, which seems reasonable secure. At least as secure as a seat is ever going to be on top of an elephant! The elephants were pretty easy to get onto as there is a custom stand that the elephants stand along so you can side step on.

Once my wife and I were on our elephant, we made way following one of the couples in front.

We went out as a group of three (we were in the middle). We headed off down a single lane dirt track which was reasonably shaded.

The elephants did appear tired to us, and this was in the morning, and think we were only the second or third outing for the elephants. 

After a short time the elephant in front decided to side step to pay some interest into some elephant poo. The "keeper" who was in charge of this elephant shouted at the elephant and grabbed some of the poo from the floor and rubbed it into its face/mouth. The elephant then decide to continue on as normal, the elephant looked a bit upset by this. 
The three keepers were all talking Thai and appeared to find this really funny.

My wife and I are great animal lovers and we were unsure what to think of this situation. Is this normally how a elephant should be corrected when it's done wrong? Maybe it is... Strange way to do it though. 

We continued on down a track where the keepers took our cameras and took photos of us, which was nice.

After around 30 mins we turned around and all headed back.

To us it seemed the elephants were  slapped with a bamboo stick where at times it didn't seem necessary. ie our elephant was walking on, on the track. It hadn't stopped or gone off route. The keepers seem to also not paying too much attention. One elephant was around 20 metres ahead of us while his keeper was talking to ours. They were all very casual, and maybe this is fine, but it's a new daunting experience for us so I guess we were more on edge.

We would like to be clear by saying we do not know how a elephant should be treated but with this going on and the poo incident we felt uncomfortable and bad that we had paid for such excursion.

We would also like to state the elephants looked well. They didn't seem unhappy etc.

Once we got back we paid for some pineapples and fed our elephant. This was really nice and allowed us to touch our elephant and thank it appropriately.

After this we had a drink and some pineapple. The booking stated we would get lunch, but a few pieces of  pineapple we wouldn't classify as really lunch. The tour also allowed us to buy two photos that they took of us for 350 baht, which we bought. Apparently the money goes towards the elephants medical bills.

We then headed back to our hotel.

Our guide was "Nimm". She was really good. 

Overall we enjoyed the tour but at times wondered about the elephants welfare based on the above comments.
reviews.title: Half day elephant trek

reviews.text: We went on two excusiona with Khao lak land discovery. We did the temple tour and the Elephant half a day. We loved both excusions and on both excusions we had dinner included. The restaurants we went to on both occasions were lovely and quiet but the food was incredible. I will deffinetly be using them again on my next visit.
reviews.title: Brilliant excursions

reviews.text:
We did 4 excursions with them. Phi Phi Islands, Surin Islands, Elephant trekking half day and Phang Gna Bay.

All guides were awesome, the food was delicious every day and the trips were well planed.  

No complaints at all!.
 
Really good prices and really good staff.

Thanks Isi and all guides for make our dream holidays possible to my mum and me and make funny and enjoyable trips!!
reviews.title: All perfect!

reviews.text: My son and I took the bike tours to the memories beach, buddist temple and then into the jungle to the waterfall. Just brilliant. We felt safe, cared for and what a great day. Scenery was just beautiful, the guide was caring and thoughtful, especially with my 7 year old son. Nothing was too much of a problem. Drinks were a plenty, and you needed them. The lunch was just excellent for where we were! A must do and see tour! Definitely would recommend! Thank you for looking after us!
reviews.title: Great morning!Must see.

reviews.text: We had a wonderful day with our tour guide 'Gift'. Elephant trekking was amazing, followed by a visit to Small Sandy beach, beautiful waterfall & divine lunch there, played with the stray puppies near the restaurant, so cute.  Could the day get any better? Yes!!! Bamboo rafting!!! Take it from me (I'm terrified of water) I absolutely loved it.   Our punters were so experienced I felt completley safe, we saw snakes in the trees above us.  The whole experience was magical.  We finished the day with a visit to the Turtle conservation park.  All in all a very special day.  I would recommend this toanyone.   And the elephants are look after extremeley well  by their mahouts. Great day trip.
reviews.title: Best day tour in Khoa Lak

reviews.text: We had a fantastic day in Khao Sok National Park.  The scenery is breathtaking.  We firstly went to the tsunami memorial which wasn't brilliant but the guide gave a good run down of what had happened at that place and it was very moving and sad.  Then elephant trekking in the jungle - couldn't fault it, a fan traditional Thai lunch followed by an afternoon kayaking down the Sok river with some amazing photo opportunities.  Finally we visited the monkey temple - careful though, they're not as cute as they look.  They are wild and can be viscious.  Would advise against feeding them.  The tour guides were very good and knowledgeble and we would definitely use them again.
reviews.title: Thailand jungle in a day!

reviews.text: So on my last day in Phuket, I was alone and wanted something to do, and my hostel offered a discount on the jungle trek. I was skeptical about doing this in the beginning as Asia isn't known for its care of animals. But I did it anyway. The drive to the actually nature park is about 2 hours out and takes you off the island of Phuket and into the most southern part of Thailand. We started with a visit to a tsunami memorial site and they even showed us pictures of how the area looked when it happened. Then we went off to the elephant camp. The elephants looked relatively healthy and content. We rode on them for about an hour into the jungle and crossing a river at one point. Then we stopped at a monkey temple and seen some monkeys. Next we had an amazing lunch. Then off it was to a river canoe, which I though, but they then dropped off the family groups with the canoes and took us adventure looking types down further to bamboo canoes. I was quite impressed with this and it was definitely the highlight of the day. We stopped along the river and were given tea in a bamboo cup that we could even keep as souvenirs. Turned out to be a good day in the jungle.
reviews.title: Change of scenery

reviews.text:
We went on the half day elephant trek with Khao Lak Land Discovery tours in November 2014 and I cannot recommend it enough.

We initially had to call the tour to ask to move our trip to another day (my boyfriend was in bed with food poisoning all morning - stay away from fried chicken) and a man (who spoke very good English) said it was no problem to change dates. Good start.

We were actually staying in Patong, Phuket and so had to pay 3500 baht (around £70) extra to include transfers (which is reasonably priced - it was around a 1.5/2hr drive to Khao Lak). On the morning on the trip our driver arrived on time in a pleasantly air contioned mini bus and began the drive to Khao Lak. When we arrived at the tour office everyone seemed to go out of their way to ensure we had the most enjoyable trip possible after hearing we had travelled quite a way for it. Our tour guide was incredible, her name was Nuut/Noot (forgive me for the spelling) and she was brilliant. In the office she told us about the most playful elephant at the camp and promised she would try to arrange for us to ride her as we had travelled up to Khao Lak. We then got back in the taxi to pick up the other parties. As we had a short wait inbetween picking up a few other people, Nuut asked the driver to stop at a local Buddhist temple/monastery, which was a lovely time-filler for us! We then eventually got to the elephant camp which was just amazing. I was a little worried about the treatments of the elephants in these places (which is why we chose to travel to Khao Lak rather than the camps in Phuket) but to me at least, the elephants looked like they were well taken care of, and it seemed the elephants and their mahouts (keepers) genuinely had a lovely relationship. True to Nuut's word, me and my boyfriend got to ride on the youngest most playful elephant - Booy (again, spelling). It was amazing, after a few minutes the mahout jumped down and offered me to slide down onto the elephants head - which I of course did! He then took our camera and took 20 or so photos of us on the elephant. We went on a short trek (20 mins or so?) before arriving at the lake. This really was the highlight of our day, swimming and playing in the water with these beautiful elephants was a one-of-a-kind experience! Nuut had our camera ready snapping away taking wonderful photos of the whole thing! Perfect! After a short swim and some more photo ops, we trekked back to the elephant base camp where we could feed the elephants bananas, had some snacks, and if you would like to there was the option to buy extra photos. We also used this as an opportunity to tip our mahout (quite generously, but I really enjoyed the trek and was delighted to see the treatment of the elephants.) Now, I thought this was where the trip ended but I was wrong! We then jumped back in the minibus and took a short drive to a beautiful waterfall where we ate lunch and went for a swim - we loved it, it was a brilliant surprise and really added to the day. We then made one last stop at the Tsunami memorial (at the request of someone in our group) which really was amazing. The whole day was brilliant and I cannot recommend it enough, Nuut our guide was absolutely amazing and I would do the whole thing again if I could! Thank you Khao Lak Land Discovery!!
reviews.title: Half Day Elepehant Trek

reviews.text: Tour was very well run and organised. They handled the mixed languages on the trip really well. The island was extremely busy at Ma ya Island but the tour company made sure everyone got the tourist photo opp and that we all made it back on the boat. Food at lunch was surprisingly good considering the environment they have to work with. They offered 4 differnent Thai dishes and plenty of water and softdrink throughout the day. Overall the whole day was managed and delivered really well.
reviews.title: Phi Phi Island trip

reviews.text: My mom and I went to Thailand in February 2013. We used Khao Lak Land Discovery to book tours during our time spent in Khao Lak. On day one we did a tour of the Similan Islands. This was a great way to see the beauty of the ocean and offered really good snorkeling spots. The boat ride was super bumpy so luckily I took some anti-nausea medication beforehand. The second day we did a tour of Phang Nga Bay. We were picked up for this tour around 4am in order to arrive at the sea caves before the tide filled them. On arriving to the caves via boat, we were able to first walk through them on foot then do more exploring in our kayaks. This turned out to be a highlight of the trip. Ryan was our tour guide and we loved him.
reviews.title: Thailand February 2013

reviews.text: Went on a full day Khao Lak Safari tour and it was great. The tour included bamboo rafting in nice rainforest setting and calm waters approx 30-40 min. We spotted cobras, mambas sleeping up in the trees. Afterwards a short bus trip to the Lamu national park for a walk to a beauteous beach for a swim. After an hour back to the bus for a trip to a turtle farm inside the naval force station! After watching cute turtles in different sizes it was lunch time at a waterfall. After lunch we drove north for an hour- usually the tour visits an elephant camp nearby but due to high season right now it was fully booked. The camp selected this time was absolutely great. The treeking was serious with the elephant striving up a rocky muddy path and downwards- boy that was a thrilling ride! The elephant keeper took lots of pictures of us with our own camera. Do not forget to buy and feed the elephat bananas after the treeking!! Recommended!!!
reviews.title: Great value

reviews.text: My wife and I booked a trip to the Similan Islands with Khao Lak Land Discovery, and it couldn't have been more fun despite the circumstances. No problems on the way to Similan nor once we got to the islands. Plenty of food and beverages provided during the day by the staff. English- and German-speaking tour guides on our boat. On the way back from Similan, our boat ran out of gas in the middle of a thunderstorm because of engine trouble. But the staff took it in stride, worked on the vessel, called for helped, kept the atmosphere light and fun with the passengers, and always were in control of the situation. We felt safe and calm despite the chaos, and it ended up being one of the best days during our trip to Thailand -- complete with a story for the ages!
reviews.title: Chaotic, Fun Trip To Similan Islands

reviews.text: I was on a 3-day tour in Khao Sok with my 4 year old daughter. It was a beautiful three days I will never forget. I would like to thank Oliver Weber for his professional and human approach. Oli thank you!
reviews.title: Khao Sok 3 days

reviews.text: We booked the full day safari trip with elephant riding at the end. Very good day with a very good guide. If you want to do a trip and not go on a typical packed out tour booked through your holiday company, use these guys, small and friendly groups. Excellent communication throughout the booking process and no money paid until we were picked up.
reviews.title: Good value

reviews.text:
We booked a Phuket night tour and have mixed feelings about how it went. 
There was no communication pnce it was booked. I emailed the day before to re confirm and never got a reply. 

Then the afternoon of the tour we had a note at our room stating we had missed a 'welcome meeting'. I was some what confused and again tried to contact them but could not get any one.

Eventually I spoke to some one in the office who didn't know what a welcome meeting was but confirmed our tour.

The driver arrived on time and in a nice van. He gave us a map and some info stating we must be back at meeting spot at 1130.

The drive to Phuket was incredibly long and the traffic was terrible, obviously not the drivers fault but I felt unwell with the crazy thailand driving.

We set off and enjoyed the sights of Patong and at 11 went to the meeting spot.
