Unfortunately there was a torrential down pour and it blocked some traffic, walk ways etc and we were struggling to get back to the meeting point. We ended up finding a driver to drive us the short distance but he was helps up by rain too it was full on!

When we got to the meeting spot less than 10 minutes late we could not find the bus. We searched for 20 minutes , called the company with no answer and eventually realised they had left!! Left us stranded on Phuket Island with no phone, no transport back to koah lak and no idea what to do.

I understand we were late, but it was hardly our fault and I would expect they'd wait at least 10 minutes if there was adverse weather!

Further more, no one contacted us to ask if we got home. For all they know we could still be stuck on Bangla road! We paid an EXTRA 3000 baht to get home.

Cannot recommend this company. Poor service!
reviews.title: Mixed feelings

reviews.text:
Had a 4am start to get the tides so that we could explore Phang NGA Bay Hong Island with KHAO LAK LAND DISCOVERY. Dirk was our tour operator and he took great care to ensure we understood what he was explaining about the region. He needed to carry on the conversation in English and German which he never seemed to tire of. Wonderful knowledge and obviously loves the region, people and his job. The scenery was fabulous and timing perfect to ensure that we were able to explore as much of the sites in the available time. A stop at the fishermans village and a great lunch really topped of the day. A big thank you to the "paddlers" and their pointing out rock outcrops in like images. A great tour group from booking to the drop off at our hotel.

Beware, bathroom facilities are obviously limited. Be prepared in advance by not drinking too much water.
reviews.title: Very Professional and Attentive

reviews.text: We booked the full day Khao Sok safari with canoeing and elephant trekking via a combination of telephone and email the day before the tour. The day started off on a not so great note with a fax underneath our hotel door changing our pickup time 20 minutes earlier than we had been told the day before. It left us a bit rushed as we had not been expecting this but we were ready on time for pickup. Our guide was good and conducted the tour in both French and English for the 11 of us on the tour.  The driver was safe and we were picked up in a very nice mini van with working seat belts.  The first stop was a food market.  We could have done without this as it's very similar to other outdoor markets in SE Asia. I guess it would be a good stop if you've never been to one before.  We went on the canoe trip first.  We absolutely loved it. You just had to sit back and relax while the paddlers did all the work.  They were a very friendly bunch and spoke a little English,stopping and pointing out snakes and birds we otherwise wouldn't have noticed. One of the paddler guides made a sort of bamboo whistle for the kids in his canoe which was a big hit as you can imagine.  The company provided life jackets if you wanted but the river was low and not very fast in most places so you really didn't need them.  The sky looked perfectly clear as we set off, however, it started to rain about 5 minutes after we started and hadn't bothered to get ponchos from our guide.  Our butts would still have been sitting on the wet bottom of the inflatable canoe so ponchos wouldn't have helped anyway.  Didn't matter we still loved it so quiet and peaceful... They suggest bringing an extra shirt but I would suggest also bringing an extra pair of shorts, or bathing  suit bottoms or wear quick drying shorts. We never did fully dry off for the rest of the day.  Lunch was a buffet at the Khao Sok Rock and Treetops restaurant.  They had non fish options for my allergic daughter.  The food was good but not enough for my very hungry husband.  The elephant trekking came next and  was the absolute highlight of our vacation for my 10 year old daughter.  Three of us on one elephant was a tight squeeze so my husband got to ride on the elephant's neck/head.   They said my daughter was too young to do it and we were OK with their decision.  There was another girl in our group who looked about 13 years old and she was allowed. Just mentioning this so you can manage your expectations. At the end we bought bananas for 50baht to feed the elephants. There was instant coffee and tea available before the trip back to the hotel. Our mahout was great and made a grasshopper sitting on a branch out of foliage he picked up along the trek for our daughter.. how sweet.  In between the canoeing and lunch we made a short trip to a rural temple filled with hungry monkeys.  Our guide had picked some small  leafy branches so that we had something to feed the monkeys.  You just need to hold the food low and let them have it otherwise they will literally jump on you to get the food.. The kids in our group got a bit of a scare from this..wish the guide had warned us better in that regard.   Overall a really enjoyable trip and would recommend it.
reviews.title: Khao Sok canoeing and elephant trek

reviews.text:
Our day trip with KLLD a was a wonderful and memorable experience.  Annie was our very friendly and informative tour guide and she ensured that we had a beautiful day. 
The trip from Patong into Khoa Lak was an enexpected 1 hour drive but when we got there we felt that it was totally worth it.  We thoroughly enjoyed the bamboo rafting and elephant trekking plus there were a few extra stops along the way like to the turtle project, fruit stalls, pineapple farm etc. 
A very memorable part of the day was stopping at the Tsunami Memorial Park.  

I would advise anyone looking for a day trip into Khoa Lak to book with KLLD.  Their service was efficient, professional and personalized.
reviews.title: Memorable experience

reviews.text: would recommend this tour , elephants brilliant , feed them bananas, pineapple etc , went to water falls for lunch which was the best
reviews.title: great day guides very good

reviews.text: Went with Khao Lak Land Discovery for a day out on an Elephant trek and Elephant Bathing,,,,,,,,,what a fantastic day , it was a once in a lifetime experience,, to be able to get into the water and swm with these magnificent animals was something I will never forget,, we were picked up from Phuket island (which cost a little extra, but not a lot, considering),, and driven up to the Elephant camp, which took about an hour and a half, so we didnt mind the extra cost,, Soon  after we arrived we were  loaded on to the Elephants for a trek through the area for about 40 minutes,, after that the chairs were taken off and the Elephants were taken into lagoon and we were invited to jump in with them and swim for about half an hour,,,,great fun,, after that we were again loaded into the Elephants for a ride back to camp,,,from there it was off to a little restaurant for lunch and then driven back home,, would highly recommend this tour and the company,, definitely would do it again,,
reviews.title: Fantastic day

reviews.text:
Booked the trip directly through KLLD office at Khao Lak and were given a discount. A couple of days later a mini bus turned up at 8am Dirk jumped out and introduced himself, gave a brief overview and into the bus where four others were seated none of which spoke English, by the time we arrived at the stop-point at Khao Sok Dirk had to explain everything twice in English and German, he did a fine job explaining & answering all questions, besides speaking many languages (at least 4) his wealth of knowledge about Thailand eco and culture, elephants was outstanding. The stop point was the Rock & Treehouse where units were beautifully located amongst the trees this is where we also ate lunch (very nice Thai lunch) Our group headed off canoeing first (another group was also at the stop-point, looks like whilst one group is canoeing the other group is elephant trekking then swaps over after lunch) the canoe start point was only 5-10 minutes down the road, were given life-jackets seated in a canoe with a paddler and off we went, the scenery was great, saw a number of wildlife, locals fishing on the banks of the river, besides a brief shower (ponchos were provided before taking off) overall it was very relaxing (especially when someone else is paddling), after lunch it was our turn for elephant trekking, after crossing a river in the back of a ute we met up with the elephants and their trainers (Mahouts) once on the elephant, no time was wasted, we're off trekking other elephants in-tow, about 40 minutes later we reached a water fall, time for a photo shoot, then turn around the elephants, remount and headed back, on the return trip I was asked if I wanted to ride the elephant sitting on its neck, well I gave it ago and rode it all the way back...this added to the enjoyment and the experience well worth it, back at camp we fed our elephant bananas and then left. On the return trip we called into a temple fed monkeys and further down the road we stopped at a local market and returned to out hotel around 4:00pm. 

All and all we had a great day, thanks to a pleasant canoe ride, great lunch, a very friendly elephant and its mahout and a BIG thanks to Dirk our guide...
reviews.title: Khao Sok Elephant Trekking & Canoeing with guide Dirk

reviews.text:
I liked River Rafting,Elephant Ride,Turtle Farm,Fountain.
Didn't like Fruit Market,nothing only you go ordinary fruit shops near highways.
reviews.title: River Rafting Adventure

reviews.text: Me and my girlfriend went on this tour and it exceeded my expectations despite it rained great part of the day. We went with a french group and the guide made a big effort to make us part of it explaning everything in english to us. He also buys some tai food and gives you for try. The elephants are great but we enjoyed even more the canoes, amazing trip along the river were we even saw some snakes and lizards.
reviews.title: Elephant treking and canoe

reviews.text:
We went on a James Bond island tour with Khao Lak Land Discover and it was a great day. Picked up in a minibus from
Hotel reception and driven to the boat where we spent the day exploring Phang Nga Bay on a long tail boat and sea kayak. Also visited a temple on the way back. A very enjoyable day.

We also used this company for our airport transfer home and it was faultless. Driver was early on the day of pickup and took us to the airport door in a large air conditioned vehicle for a very reasonable price. Would definitely use again-well recommended.
reviews.title: Excellent Company-Highly recommended

reviews.text:
It was our 2nd time in Khao lak and as we had last year a great experience with them going to Khao Sok we made it again this year for an Elephant trekking and bathing tour with our daughter! 

You can see that they care about service and security! Jeeps were new and well kept! 👌 And their partner companies are on the same level! The bamboo houses we stayed at Khao Sok were great and the Elephant camp as well! 

It was great!!! We loved our Thai girl tour guide, unfortunatly I don't remember her name but she was funny and gave us a great time! Driver was also cool and even made some pictures of us while we were bathing with the Elephants! 👍 

We highly recommend this company! 

Tks a lot for the great time! 

Greetings from Germany,

Giovana, Martin & Giulia
reviews.title: 2nd great time with them!

reviews.text:
We used Khao lak for every excursion & even airport pick ups (which I would highly recommend). They were always on time, provided drinks and lunch and always spoke English.

They picked us up from the airport by holding a sign and we got into an air conditioned mini bus which was great.

We also went to Phi Phi, Patong & elephant trekking... Highly recommended!!

A fab company and would use again / recommend.
reviews.title: Excellent company & service

reviews.text: A great 1/2 day trip. We were picked up from our resort, Mai Khao Lak, it was only a short drive to the elephant camp. We went on a short elephant walk(1/2 hour) to a picturesque waterful, then we returned to the camp where we were given light refreshments, fresh pineapple and cool drinks. We feed our elephant bananas. After that we headed to the tsunami memorial and onto our lunch at a quaint thai restaurant and had an enjoyable meal. This tour gives you a good experience if you restricted on time or if it is your first time
reviews.title: Half day tour, Khao Lak Nature Discovery

reviews.text: WOW!! the elephant trek was pretty much as expected and was most enjoyable for the reactions of the teenagers we were with; and our daughter and son-in-law. The crowning glory was being able to accompany the elephants into the water for a swim; what an experience. Then we rode out of the water on the elephants neck.
reviews.title: Elephant trek with swimming

reviews.text:
Wow- I was picked up from PATONG at 615 am. It was a 3 hr car ride and I am so grateful that they offered to pick me up!

We promptly left at 9am to go to the islands (which took about an hour). They provide sea sickness pills, breakfast and coffee and tea before also so don't be nervous about the ride!

The guide and staff are really friendly and funny. They offered us refreshments and multiple snacks every single time we went back to the boat. 

I was skeptical about the lunch after seeing other reviews but LUNCH WAS DELICIOUS, and there was plenty!!! And then some.

I was really impressed. I used a different and expensive company for the phi phi islands (3600 baht) but I felt like I got the same amount of service and had just as much fun with this company and only paid $2600. I think it's more expensive when you live in phuket rather than Khao lak. WORTH IT

My ride back was also great, I got to talk to the driver about my travels and his life. 

THANK YOU SO MUCH!!!
reviews.title: Day trip to Similian Island

reviews.text: We booked this trip through Kuoni. The Canoe and Elephant trek were brilliant. But we felt that the added extras to the day were not very good. We were taken to a hot springs where our guide took ages agreeing the price and before our elephant trek we were taken to a monkey temple and were told we could pay 20 bahts to feed bananas to them. But were warned to be careful as the monkeys carried the HIV virus and not to let the monkeys bite or sratch you! We were amazed to be told this and frankly don't think this is somewhere tourists should be taken to. Put this aside the canoe and elephant trek was an amazing experience and something will will remember as part of our honeymoon to Thailand.
reviews.title: 1 day Canoe & Elephant trip

reviews.text:
Hotel pick-up was bang on time and it took about 20 minutes from the south of Khao Lak to reach the jetty. Our guide gave a detailed briefing in german (majority of guests spoke this) and a less detailed one in english. I think he just missed out the cheesy jokes, but all the relevant information was there. Seasickness pills were provided. Then it was off to the boats, via a large bucket where we were asked to leave our shoes. So, don't bother about what to wear on your feet, as the answer is flippers or nothing.

Water was offered throughout the trip and all snorkel gear was provided, plus there were life jackets available for less confident swimmers. One negative  - I reckon with a couple of people less in the group the boat journey would have been a lot more comfortable. We spent a rather cramped hour zipping over the waves to our first stop, where we climbed Sail Rock  - a bit tricky with nothing on your feet. If you have mobility problems or suffer from vertigo, enjoy a swim from the white sand beach. This wasn't a scheduled snorkel stop but there were still fish to observe.

Then it was on to our first snorkel stop - imagine spending 45 glorious minutes with your head in an aquarium. Magic. The sun is strong so make sure you slap on the waterproof sunblock or even better, wear a t-shirt when you're in the water. 

We then moved on to our third stop where we had lunch (average at best) and hung around for an hour waiting to go to the next snorkel stop. But hey, the beach is pretty and there are plenty of shady trees. It was nice to chill in the cool Andaman breeze. 

The second snorkel stop was amazing, not least because a green sea turtle decided to join us. There were even more fish than at the first stop, and though there were a couple of other boats there when we arrived, they moved off and we had the place to ourselves for about 45 minutes. An hour passed very quickly and then it was time to head for home. 

So, bring yourself, swimming gear, a towel, a t-shirt, high factor sunscreen, your camera and a smile, plus water. We were offered water regularly on the boat but it's still worth bringing extra.
reviews.title: Amazing snorkelling - Similans

reviews.text:
We were determined to ride elephants while in Thailand and this trip certainly didn't disappoint. 
While we would have preferred to have gone on some of the full day trips on offer, because we have 2 young children (a 4 year old & 6 year old with special needs) we opted for the half day elephant bath.  It was INCREDIBLE. We loved it and the children loved it.
We had a charming guide with excellent English, the elephant ride & bath was lovely,  feeding the elephants lovely and the lunch absolutely delicious. Oh, and the waterfall was pretty nice too.
Plus they picked us up from our hotel (which was just outside khao lak) and dropped us back again.
We gave given our children memories to last a lifetime - highly recommend!
reviews.title: One of the best days of my life!

reviews.text: We visited Tachai Island with Khaolak land discovery last week. Hotel pick up was a little chaotic but we finally left to Tachai at 9.00am. The boat was full and the sailing time is relatively long. When we reached Tachai, there was many boats already on the beach. Snorkeling was good but not as impressive as we expected.
reviews.title: Day trip to Koh Tachai

reviews.text:
My wife and I and two teenage sons did the Three Temple Tour and half day Elephant Bath tour. The fellow who led the temple tour was an exceptional guide (Dirk I think). Swedish by birth but fluent in German and English.  My son is a student of world religions and found him very informative (he's a very critical audience to play to). The 3 temples are very different with hardly anybody at the first 2 and these are in very picturesque locations. The final temple had crowds but the monkeys made up for this. The lunch stop is at a really pretty site and the food excellent. My wife got car sick. Dirk pulled over and arranged some motion sickness medicine that did the trick. The Germans we shared the bus with were very good natured about this little delay. Its was only a small group tour and so it mattered that the people we shared the day with were decent folk. We were blessed by a Monk at the final temple and even if you don't place most credence in the teachings of the Buddha, I think it's a respectful exercise to go through. Anyway it might have worked for us because the rest of the trip was fantastic.
As for the elephants, well whats not to like about taking a dunk with an elephant. Be prepared to get wet and have a lot of fun. As one is inclined to take photos of other elephants and hence the people on them it pays to get the email address of your fellow travellers. Another good meal concluded this trip.
Can't praise this company enough and I'm agitating for a return so I can perhaps do one of their longer 2 or 3 day trips.
Thanks Khao Lak Land Discovery!
reviews.title: 3 Temples Tour and Elephant Bath

reviews.text: My husband and two sons aged 19 and 16 went on the three temple tour. Our guide Dirk was so informative he knew the Buddhist religion and culture. On the tour was five Germans and us Australian, he was fluent in both languages and gave equal time to both groups. The temples were amazing and lunch was a feast in a great spot by the river. I would highly recommend this tour especially if you are interested in Buddhism.
reviews.title: Three Temple Tour

reviews.text:
I did the Tree Temples tour with this company on the 12th of february of 2014.

Our guide was Wendy, and we had a bilingual (English and German) group of tourists, so all information was provided in both languages. We got a lot of background information on Thai culture, buddhism and of course the temples themselves. Wendy was very informative and took the time to answer all questions (again in both languages). Pick up from the hotel was exactly on time in a comfortable minivan. This tour is highly recommended.
reviews.title: Three temples tour: very good and informative

reviews.text: We booked the Khao Sok Tour with Hughes and were very content with our tour leader. We were a mixed group of french and english speaking people and at all times he was doing everything to translate every bit of conversation in both languages. He had plenty of information on sights and culture and was very friendly. I’d book with Land discovery and Hughes again :)
reviews.title: Great trip!

reviews.text: A great experience all way through - smooth booking process, pickup on time, great tour-guide (Andy), super treetop bungalow and food.... and memorable experiences, especially evening and night in the treetops watching and hearing the animals as well as the 4 hour jungle-walk on day 2. I would definately recommend this tour.
reviews.title: Khao Sok tour 2d/1n - Khao Sok Treehouse

reviews.text: Booked the trek and bathe with elephants via the KLLD office in Khao Lak - paid a slightly discounted 3500 baht for two of us. Hotel pick up on time and we went to the elephant sanctuary north of Khao Lak. On arrival we had a brief about the elephants and then went on an hour trek. Unlike the previous review, the experience was totally the opposite. The elephants were extremely well treated, never once hit or picked. The seats were all well padded out with quite a few layers of carpet/material. There were certainly no pressure marks or sores evident when we bathed with them with the seats off. They went their own way and were allowed to within reason and their handlers were helpful and friendly, offering to take pictures with our cameras. We then spent half an hour bathing with them, sitting on our own elephant, washing and playing with them. Yes, you could buy food to feed them and we did - fresh bananas which they loved. They also fed themselves around the trek with leaves (and were allowed to by the handlers). From there we went for a lovely lunch up by a waterfall south of Khao Lak before being taken back to our hotel. Our guide for the day - 'Gift' and our driver 'Sun' were both excellent and Gift explained everything about the elephants and indeed anything else we asked her. Well worth the money and we did not feel at all that we part of any exploitation of these gentle giants. If you want to do an elephant trek, I would certainly recommend this one as a stand-alone trip. Thank you KLLD.
reviews.title: Excellent day with the elephants

reviews.text:
We wanted to make sure before you read this review that the negative feedback in here is with no reference to Khao Lak Land Discovery, who provided an excellant service. 
We choose the day safari with KLLD, we were picked up promptly from the hotel and from the moment we got on the minibus, until the day ended, the guide and driver were very informative and helpful. They told us what was happening at each step of the day, and kept us feed and watered. The trip was good value for money.
There were 8 of us on the trip, of various nationalities.
We started with a bamboo raft trip, which was excellant. Make sure you take your insect spray though. All the guides were good and we really liked this part of the day. With an optional photo at the end, but not bad value.
The second part of the day was a short walk down to the "small beach" which was lovely and secluded , just the right amount of time to enjoy a swim or a relax.
Next to lunch, brilliant food, plenty of it, very tasty and a nice strong coffee after. The guides really looked after us here and we have no complaints at all.
A short walk to the waterfall for some pictures.
Next was the part of the day that unfortunately we did not enjoy. We choose to do an elephant trek to the waterfalls. We both felt very uncomfortable about this. The elephants did not appear to be very well treated, the elephant guides did not seem to have a rapore with their elephants. There was little cushioning on the elephant between us and them, we were comfy but not sure they were. The guide continued to whip or pik the elephant even though it appeared to be moving along at a steady pace. We were left to make a short walk to a waterfall on our own, and although this was beautiful but we didnt want to swim and so had to sit and wait for the guides to take us back. Again another very upsetting ride back. When we did get back we were offered, for a small fee, food to give the elephants, we felt that the elephant expected food so did buy some, but actually once we had brought it we felt that the elephant was almost made to eat the not very fresh produce. We have visited elephant sanctuarys before in Thailand, Africa and Bali but this was the most unpleaseant experience we have had so far. I dont want this to reflect badly on KLLD but my suggestion would be that they perhaps look to take the Elephant trekking part of the day to somewhere where the Elephants are treated as they deserve. 
Khao Lak Land Discovery were very helpful, informative and the trip was good value for money. We woudl highly recommend this trip less the Elephant trek.
reviews.title: Day was brilliant with exception of Elephants

reviews.text: We used land discovery in November and booked 5 trips, 3 Temples, Khao Sok overnight.James Bond Island, Khao Lak Elephant Trek and Phuket shopping.All pick ups were on time if not  early in new vehicles and guides were very good.Thanks to Isii who organised the trips and to Dirk, Leon and Mongkol our guides who looked after us.All trips were excellent and we really enjoyed them.would definetly use again
reviews.title: Khai Lak tours

reviews.text: While staying in beautiful Khao Lak we took advantage of seeing one of Thailands more famous islands James Bond Island from the Roger Moore "The Man with the Golden Gun". On the day of our tour it was a very early start with a 7am pick up a and we were excited for the adventure that was ahead of us . Our tour guide was a guy from Holland and was roughly presented and I felt there was no real friendly connection from the beginning.We proceeded to pick up another couples that were from Germany and they were all really lovely however nobody spoke English so we communicated through smiles , and friendly hand gestures . The guide then proceeded to talk in German which is fine but neglected to translate to us so we missed out on a lot of significant  info along the way. After a long drive we rendezvous with an old Thai Tail boat and we were heading down river towards the sea . The scenery was breath taking and camera in hand is a 'must'. Upon reaching  our destination which took an hour or so the guide muttered that it was too busy on JB island and we just sailed on by ! . Every one was disappointed and beginning to question what had we paid quite a substantial amount of dollars for ?. We then went further to another are for some sea kaiaking around some caves for an hour or so and back on the boat to which we all thought was headed to the main attraction. No , we sailed straight past again which was terrible as everyone needed a toilet stop on the boat which was ignored even though two ladies on the boat were in tears as their bladders were on the brink of bursting . We were disgusted . The tour guide then took us a floating village where we manage to dock after 45 mins or so to see every lady scramble off the boat in desperate search of a toilet . The guide casually gestured they are over there and we all made a bee line toward them to then realize this vast village was a labyrinth . We all finally found them but they were closed !. There were little girls selling souvenir's so we told them we would by lots if they took us to a toilet which thank god they promptly did ! . Then comes the interesting part of the day , the tour guide had left us for dead and proceeded to take the other couples onwards . We searched and searched the labyrinth of markets and stalls for our group to find ourselves hopelessly lost and no tour group or help insight which is very distressing  considering how remote we were from land . All in all were saved by one thing............. my mobile phone , don't leave home without one ! . If we didn't call the head office and have them call the tour guide we would of been clearly in deep trouble . He came back to retrieve us with a load of pathetic excuses and walked us back to the group which mind you was on the other side of the island . There was no way we could of dreamed of finding them on our own . On arrival to the restaurant for a scheduled lunch apparently we found all had eaten and we were offered left overs and scraps which we plainly said " are you joking?!". We couldn't wait to get back to our beautiful hotel with the view of trying to forget the whole day and wasted funds for the tour from hell . After another long boat ride and long drive i asked the driver to stop in town , we got out and told the guide we will make our own way back to the hotel via taxi , but not before a good stiff drink and some delicious food as we were ill from not eating since 6am and it is now 6pm .All in all this is our 6 th trip to Thailand and we adore the country and its beautiful people and kind hospitality , so you can say we if you are a first time traveler you might have found our experience a bit more perplexing !!. So in conclusion , travelers beware of this company and do your research to ensure a wonderful experience instead of what we all do usually and take a leap of faith on what you assume to be a reputable company with a professional well planned tour . In this case not . I take refuge in the idea of sweeter adventures ahead !. Good luck fellow travelers and hope this may help you ensure a more successful tour of "James Bond Island !" .
reviews.title: James Bond island tour

reviews.text:
We did the bamboo rafting, small sandy beach and elephant riding combination.
We never spent too long at one place, the price was fair and the guides were nice.  A great option to see lots of stuff in a day!
reviews.title: Nice staff and good way to see lots in one day

reviews.text:
Really good service, the guys from the office - Isi, Chan, Edwin - stars! 

The Bamboo rafting and elephant trekking tour was excellent as was Phi Phi Island and the Similan! The one thing I would say is...you guys need more English speakers on staff! Everyone was very nice but because the guides were German and the group was predominantly German, we felt a little excluded as English speakers, not getting as much interesting info.
reviews.title: Great tours!!!

reviews.text: The bamboo rafting down the river at Phang Nga was an excellent experience for entire family. The river water was cool and refreshing and the ride is very safe for kids too. Enjoyed the scenic ride down the river and wish it was a longer ride. Be prepared to get wet but is worth it.
reviews.title: Bamboo rafting along scenic river

reviews.text:
I booked the airport transfer to JW resort together with the Khao Lak Safari day trip, with some discount online in advance. The driver was on time and friendly. 
For the day trip, we have six people and occupied for the whole car without other tourists. The guide was nice. We had elephant trekking to a small waterfall, but it return on the same way. Then we feed elephant with bananas. We went to a larger waterfall, so bad that we didn't bring the swimming suit to enjoy a swim. We had our lunch in a restaurant next to another small fall, not bad.
The guide advised to have the bamboo rafting first as he afraid it's gonna to rain. The bamboo rafting is exciting. Finally have the visit of turtle reservation center. The trip supposed to have a beach visit, too, but the guide knew our resort already with a private beach and we all agreed not to go there. We started at 9am and returned resort at around 3:40pm.

Later we saw some of the day trips with snake shows / monkey shows ...... um.....it seems that forest day trip would be the only option in Khao Lak when diving in Similan Island is not available during this period.
reviews.title: Day tour

reviews.text: This company picked us up from the airport and also drove us back. We arranged everything by email and we payed the driver. The organization went pretty well and the drive was at the airport on time and also the way back he was on time. The bus was very new and well equipped. So from the organization point and friendliness it was perfect. The only worry was the drive both time they went much too fast for example you were allowed to go 60 and he was 100. I would have preferred to pay a bit more but somehow arrive a bit more relaxed.
reviews.title: Good Company

reviews.text: We had a really good day on the Three Temple Tour - Dirk the tour guide was very friendly and his knowledge of the temples, their history and Buddhist background was excellent. He made the tour so interesting and kept feeding us other pieces of information as we travelled along the whole day tour. We were accompanied by a group of 4 Germans from another hotel and Dirk kept the information flowing in both languages and at no time did we feel ''left out' . The Germans were friendly too and we managed a flow of 'banter' despite each group having limited knowledge of the others mother tongue. Cold drinks and cold towels provided during the day were welcome and the lunch included was also very good. would thoroughly recommend the tour to anyone wishing to gain a better understanding regarding the culture and beliefs within this lovely country.
reviews.title: Three Temple Tour - a really interesting and enjoyable day

reviews.text:
We were looking forward to  fantastic day trip but we were very dissapointed with Land Discovery. Our elephant ride was o.k. nothing very eventfull happened - you walk for 20 minutes up a mountain stream and then 20 minutes back along the same route onm the back of the elephant. The canoe trip was fantastic - they arranged it, but our canoest, a local, who could speak some English, made that part very memorable by showing us all sorts of wildlife that we did not see at first as we floated down the river. The waterfall was also pretty at the end. 

We sat in the back of their vehicle which felt like it had no suspension; every bump could be felt. We were sitting with a wet towel, after the canoe trips in the back of the car wiping ourselves. We feel that the non-native Thai guide lacked professional traing in customer care. Where we are from, you don't wait for the customer to complain; you watch them closely to see that they are comfortable, etc. At the last attraction the question was asked, "Can you hear me clearly at the back of the car?" Surely that should be the first question when starting out. Also, from going on another tour with another company with a native Thai guide who could speak beautiful English with a surprising British accent, we discovered that the Land Discovery guide was not correctly informed about Thai culture. We also felt that the Land Discovery guide had a lack for respect for the Thai culture as she seemed to belittle aspects of Thai culture/religion. We would prefer not to go with tours arranged by Land Discovery again - other companies can take us to similar places in a much more caring way.
reviews.title: Biggest/oldest is not always the best

reviews.text:
Booked a half day tour with Khao Lak Land discovery and was not disappointed. Our tour guide turned up early so we were able set off before the peak hour traffic and we were briefed about what we were going to see and do. We experienced a thirty minute elephant trek as well as two trips to awesome waterfalls that we were able to swim in for about twenty-thirty minutes each and was loads of fun. It's completely safe, no leeches, no fish, no bugs, just pure and refreshing water and nothing too deep. Lunch is provided and was well worth it; a restaurant beside a river and excellent food and service and a great opportunity to bond with the family that had come along with us. You're also given a complimentary picture after the elephant trek which we loved. 

Great for families, couples, anyone really and definitely well worth it! Would do it again in a heartbeat!
reviews.title: Waterfalls and elephants!

reviews.text: Today we got out of bed at 4 am to travel with Khaolak Land Discovery to Phang Nga Bay by car, on a longtail boat and in canoes through the beautiful limestone caves to the inside of wonderful secret islands. It was like visiting the land before time. We had a small mishap with our longtail who's propellor seized and so we had an extra hour to explore on foot a different cave inside another island. This world heritage area is a must see and this tour company is brilliant. We had Jess as a guide. A multilingual Dutchman with a great sense of humour and who really knew an awful lot about the area and Thailand. We had lovely Thai guys paddling for us and operating the longtails, fantastic food at the floating fishermen's village and the best weather we could have wished for. It was also great value for money including food, drinks, tour transport and insurance.
reviews.title: Hong Island Discovery

reviews.text:
We have  booked by internet the elephant trek and bath .And on date and time they picked us in Phuket to go to Khao Lak. The driver and the tourguide were very kind and very profissional!
And the boy who guide the elephant was also very kind!
The lunch was very good ! 
It is an amazing experience to be in the river with the elephant!
We enjoyed everything and we recomend " Khao Lak Land Discovery"!
reviews.title: excelent!!

reviews.text: Great experience, good value and the scenery perfect. Tour guide Charin was very accommodating and speaks good English. Elephant ride was great and bathing with them vas very personal. Transfers and visit the waterfall, lunch after included for $1850 baht
reviews.title: Elephant 1/2 day tour

reviews.text: Did the full day - visited turtle farm, bamboo rafting and elephant ride - all with a very friendly guide & driver. The elephant ride was not rushed as I have had before with plenty of time to feed and talk to the handlers afterward. Lunch was provided and was near a small waterfall - reasonable food and beautiful location.
reviews.title: Great day out

reviews.text: Despite the rainy weather, we went with the kids riding the elephants. The tour itself is not really great (up and down the same trail) but the guide told us a lot of interesting things about these kind giants and you got to have a lot of respect for the men who work with them. Afterwards we had lunch by the waterfall and being vegetarians we were happily surprised by the standard and choice of the food. Thank you Jessey!
reviews.title: half day elephant trip with guide 'Jessey'

reviews.text:
We booked a full day tour with Khao Lak Land Discovery for elephant trekking followed by river rafting.

As it turned out it was raining on the day of the tour. We were picked up from our hotel right on time and went first to the elephant trekking. We were provided with ponchos to protect us from the rain, but the rain stopped before we reached the waterfall which was our destination. The waterfall was quite lovely, but when we began our return journey the heavens opened with heavy rain. The trek was still enjoyable, however.

After feeding the elephants bananas, we drove to another waterfall where we were supposed to be able to swim. However, this was now a raging torrent after the heavy rain, so we instead went directly to lunch in the restaurant above the falls. Lunch was delicious, and we were pleasantly surprised how good the food was.

We were then supposed to go rafting on the river, but when we expressed reservations with the water flow following the rain, the guide was happy to change it to a half day tour and refund the difference in cost between the half and full day tour. They then took us to the Turtle Conservation facility as a bonus, before depositing us back at the hotel.

We were very happy with the tour guide Charlie and their flexibility in looking after us. The only criticism I would have is that the driver drove very fast in heavy rain, and it was a hair raising ride over the mountain.
reviews.title: Elephant trekking in the rain

reviews.text: We booked the shopping and sightseeing Phuket trip with KL Land Discovery after seeing the good price of 1000 thb.  The condition of the minibus was good, and although we were the only English speaking people in the group of 8, the tour guide spoke good English and provided enough info on the local places of interest on the tour. The issue we had with this company was the driver.  We were sat in the back row of the minibus and the driver made no allowance whilst going over speedbumps,  sped continuously and there were several near misses. We felt the driver was in a rush all of the day. There was an older woman sat next to us also and she was also being thrown about In the bus and looked very uncomfortable. My husband did speak with the tour guide during the first stop to request she ask the driver to slow down, however there was absolutely no changes made by the driver. I feel that the driver had no respect for his passengers and would not recommend this company because of this.
reviews.title: Good trip but driver was not good

reviews.text:
Deciding on which company to use when you flit from one to the other is something I find a little daunting. Get it right and you're a bit smug. Get it wrong and the day is ruined.

This was the 2nd company we visited having left our details with another company, KLLD was able to offer us the Hong Island tour immediately whereas the 1st company simply took our details (as it turned out we never heard from them again).

We discussed the different tours available and settled on Hong Island. (definitely better than the James Bond island which to be honest on its own is a compete waste of money).

They're not that cheap at £154 for a couple but you get a whole day package. They pick you up at the hotel. In our case 5am so that we could get to Hong island at low tide to canoe through bat cave and into a completely hidden little lake.

The taxi takes you to a Longtail boat which travels for about an hour before being transferred to canoes. You can simply sit back and relax as there are two people per canoe plus the rower which is one of the staff members. The sunrise was amazing.

Although they don't provide breakfast, they provide an ample supply of water and soft drinks as well as sticky rice and either banana or coconut served in a banana leaf. Very tasty.

Having done Hong Island we made our way to James Bond island which is a small rock sticking out from the sea. There is an island adjacent to it which consists only of lots of shops all selling identical tourist tat. The tourist are literally shipped in at an amazing rate. We arrived early and within half an hour 6 speedboats had arrived and another 4 longtail boats. You're lucky if you can get a photo without some tourist appearing in your pic. 

From here we traveled past some mangrove swamps and did a tour of some other islands in the canoes. All very relaxing.

As we headed slowly back we stopped at a Moslem fishing village which is on stilts. The lunch (included in the price) was very tasty and there was more than could be finished. It consists of about 5-6 different dishes including a vegetarian dish and some non  seafood dishes.

Personally I didn't enjoy the village and was praying that the restaurant had their own kitchen because that which I saw as we walked through the village wasn't appetising at all.

It's a fisherman's village on which about 500 people live. At low tide the whole place smells of sewage. Somehow they have managed to shield the restaurant from this.

The guide spoke several languages and his English was fluent with a slight accent. He provided useful titbits of information without turning the trip into a field trip. He was keen to offer to take photographs of couples.

We were 4 in our group which worked out well.

Although this trip isn't suitable for the physically disabled there is no reason why those with limited mobility can't get out there. The couple with us were both pensioners and the lady had limited movement in her arm and couldn't take big steps but they accommodated her without any fuss at all.

They offer various trips but most of them require a minimum of 4 participants.

I wpuld certainly recommend them.
reviews.title: Well organised

reviews.text:
I used Khao Lak land discovey for Phuket airport transfers and a day trip to Similan. They were atleast 25-30% more expensive and had some fabulous ways of convincing you ..... quality of vehicles, special food cooked for you, we get you there before others, we have more snorkeling stops than others, unlimited drinks and snacks etc etc... Ultimately it was all Crap !!!
I paid thru my nose and got nothing out of it. Just one snorkeling Stop and the reason they gave us was this is the "2nd time in my 75 trips this has happened". Sounded like a overplayed recorded track.
I cancelled all my further plans with them and hired another local travel company at 2/3rd the cost and had a fabulous time.
reviews.title: Overhyped to charge you a bomb !

reviews.text: Bon was our guide for our family of 6 and he was fantastic. We were joined by one other couple from Croatia so the small group was lovely. We started with a fantastic raft then off to the elephant trek then waterfalls and a lay on the beach. I really fantastic day and I am only sorry we didn't book another tour with these guys as they were booked out and couldn't get us on the Similan Tour.....so we ended up with another tour company and two didn't really compare. Make sure you book in advance so you don't miss out on going on your tours with Khao Lak Land Discovery because we were pretty unhappy with the other tour company.
reviews.title: Elephant and rafting safari

reviews.text: Did the half day elephant ride tour. We were the only ones and the tour was a treat. Rode the elephant to a beautiful waterfall and then taken to lunch. A little restaurant built over a waterfall. Exquisite views and delicious food.
reviews.title: Elephant ride

reviews.text: It wasn't particularly nice weather when we did our tour which was a shame but we still all thoroughly enjoyed our day. Our guide was absolutely fantastic, very friendly and informative. James Bond Island was not a highlight, it was extremely busy with people and boats everywhere. The canoeing at Hong Island however was fantastic, 2 people to a boat along with the guide who is rowing. Some parts of the caves were quite narrow but it was a great/fun experience and in the 2nd place we went to we saw monkeys which kept us all entertained. The rock formations are stunning, and at certain times we were the only people which was very relaxing and peaceful. Our lunch at the floating village was lovely, the food was delicious and the service great. We definitely recommend this tour with Khao Lak Land Discovery, excellent service when booking and as well as our wonderful guide, the other 3 men on the boat were great and really helped us to enjoy our day.
reviews.title: Excellent tour to Hong Island

reviews.text:
The tour started bad as the transfer was late. To compensate for that the driver drove like crazy - just to get us to the honour where we had to wait 15-20 mins for another group of guests. 
Got on the boat for the 80 mins ride. It was bumping very badly and very loud. If you had a bad back or were bringing children it would have been a nightmare. Got to snorkle which was beautiful but you have to know that about 60-70 boats arrive each day so you will never feel like you got the beach on your own.
reviews.title: Similan islands day tour

reviews.text: We truly enjoyed touring with this company.  With a family of 4 kids, it is never easy to find the perfect tour but land discovery delivered on three separate occasions.  We the tour guides to be very helpful, especially Juli and willing to jump right in with our adventurous family.  We noticed with other companies that they tried to pack people in, sometimes as much as 70 people, but with land discovery the tours were no more than 12 people in well equipped vehicles.  The exception being the similan tour when the boat held 24, but even then the other tourists were personable and really madethe experience worth it.  We are planning another trip a year from now and will definitely choose this company again.
reviews.title: Perfect size groups!

reviews.text:
We went on the similan island snorkelling day trip. It was an early start but well worth it. Khao lak land discovery are the only tour operators that take you out first to beat the crowds. The trip was exciting and was a good mix of beach time, mini jungle walks and snorkelling. We saw moray eels, turtles, sea cucumbers, octopus, millions of tropical fish and of course nemo! Our guide Ron was fantastic and snorkelled with us finding the underwater treasures. All in all would recommend this trip!
 The morlands - 27/11/12
reviews.title: A great trip, with a little bit of everything!

reviews.text: This tour was just an average tour.  The guide we had spoke some English but was hard to understand.   The first stop was a pineapple farm.  That is what it was a stop to be able to buy pineapple.  There was a farm but no pineapples growing.  The second stop was elephant trekking.  The elephants here don't look we'll cared for.  Nothing was for free here.  They did not take photos so you for free you had to pay for them.  Then the waterfall, you had go go trekking down a path and then climb over around big rocks to get to it.  Not as advertised.  Then,  it was a very small waterfall and found out that there was actually a place at top that our guide could have driven up to.  Then lunch at a seafood restaurant.  Not great food. Next bamboo rafting.  Had to wait around for our turn for awhile.  Bamboo rafting was fun.  Then to the tusnami museum and sea turtle farm.  The sea turtles were actually the highlight of the tour for me.  They are taken really good care of.  Then a fruit stop to buy fruit.  I definitely would not recommend this tour.  Maybe hire a driver and pick the places you want to visit.
reviews.title: Just an average tour

reviews.text: We decided to popped into their office and the lady was so helpful and we booked a trip to Phang Nga Bay (James Bond Island) with the Sea Cave canoe trip (lunch at the gypsy village was delicious).  We also booked the 3day Khao Lak Discovery trip .. everything was included and we have nothing but praise for our guide "Charlie" and driver "Kok" (hopefully I spelt it correctly) as they looked after us and we could not have asked for better company .. the accommodation at the new Tree House was beautiful and our visit to the Khao Sok Lake raft houses were experiences we will not forget in a long time.  I am so thankful we went to Khao Lak/Khao Sok and experience Southern Thailand before we ventured in Patong, Phuket.  Hoping to travel to this beautiful area again and visit these places again with this tour company and would highly recommend
reviews.title: Fantastic Experience

reviews.text:
Myself and a friend did three tours with this organisation. Must thank the gentleman in the office, very patient, listened and made great suggestions.
The tours: Elephant Trekking with Bamboo Rafting - terrific experience with the elephants & the Bamboo Rafting was a blast (water slightly faster due to rain- yippee), then a visit to the Turtle farm at the Navy base - fantasitc how they increase the turtles chances of survival.  Hong Island & sea cave canoeing (with visit to Bond Island) - OMG the views, the experience, the natural beauty and lunch at the floating island was fantastic.  Only downside was the stalls at Bond Island - spoilt the place for me.  Three Temple tour was beautiful and inspiring (the history is amazing & how did they find these caves).
We had the same driver for all three tours - cheeky smile and very friendly (not sure of name).
All tour guides were very knowledgable and happy to answer any questions. Special mention has to go to Mongkul (guide) - thank you for an amazing experience and sharing your knowledge with us. I will definitely be looking out for you on my next trip.
Did I mention the food, lunch is included on all tours - yummy and way too much, bottles of water are supplied on all tours.   Prices for all tours is very reasonable.
Thank you Khao Lak Land Discovery & hopefully see you again.
reviews.title: Fantastic experience

reviews.text: We did 4 tours in 3 days with these guys and had a ball. The guides were informative, funny & enthusiastic, Priska was our favorite. They really care about the experience that you have while ensuring that you respect and appreciate all that is to offer throughout Khao Lak and surrounding areas. Tour price includes all admission fees, refreshments, lunch and insurance. Do yourself a favor and experience this!
reviews.title: Water is life Nature is home

reviews.text: We did a day tour with Land Discovery in late June. The organisation was excellent and our guide Mark (from Austria) was very friendly. Lunch was very nice and the whole day was interesting and good fun. Would definitely recommend this tour organisation. We also organised our transfers with them and apart from our first driver obviously being late for dinner (which seems to be a trait with Thai drivers), they were reasonably priced and reliable.
reviews.title: Bamboo rafting, turtles, rainforest and elephants tour

reviews.text: We were on a holiday in Phuket and decided to take the day tour to Khao Lak!  I'm so glad we did!  This adventure was truly the highlight of the holiday for me!  The drive is approximately one and a half hours from Phuket... But it was so lovely to leave Phuket behind and move into lush green surroundings.  We drove through little towns that were so clean... And not a tourist in sight! The adventure itself was amazing!  The kids saw and experienced so much!  Elephant trekking through dense jungle full of wildlife, bamboo canoeing and swimming in clean fresh water waterfalls!  The lunch provided was out of this world scrumptious... All overlooking the national park.  A truly memorable day!  Was so nice to move from the hassle and bustle of Phuket to Thailand's renowned beautiful country side :)
reviews.title: Highlight of our holiday!!

reviews.text: Great day out- pick up from motel, entertaining guide and a full cultural experience. Groups were small and our guide was very knowledgeable and friendly. Trip into Hong Island is very quick (but simply stunning!) due to the tides. Lunch at a gypsy town was a very interesting setting and nice food. Would recommend!
reviews.title: Phang Nga Bay & Hong Island Discovery

reviews.text: Elephant treking not all that flash as you just go around a farm not really through any rainforest. Beautiful animals though. The waterfall we went to was just up the road from the resort anyhow.
reviews.title: Just Average

reviews.text: I've been to Kholak at Pang Nga province in about last 2 weeks. It was so fun and good memory. In the last trip I was with friends and stayed at the Briza Beach Resort. This resort is little resort by the sea side that made me feel like in a peaceful area. I could completely relax in friends in the resort at the day time and also having extremely fun at the night time. Back to my current trip. I planned to go sightseeing in Takuapa district by driving. It is said that, in this district has so much attractive chino-Portuguese building style. The architecture style can be seen in many provinces around Southern Thailand. So I think these all buildings will drive me be back in the period time. My slow driving trip  had started in the early morning til afternoon then stop driving for lunch. There is so many restaurant and stall stores had been recommended to try. Finally I choose Khaolak Seafood restaurant because here serves lot of tasty traditional food. Normally, food in southern area is so spicy if anyone likes spicy food you can try them. In the evening I went back to my comfy room in The Briza Resort again for having some rest.
reviews.title: ฺBack to my place

reviews.text:
We did the Phang Nga Bay and Hong Island tour in May 2012 and it was great!

We were picked up on time from our hotel and made our way to Phang Nga Bay which was about an hour away. We then went by Long Tail boat to the Muslim village on stilts where we had lunch which was lovely! We then had some time to explore before we headed back to the boat.

The sea cave canoeing was awesome, though at times, a little bit scary when you go through the low caves! But Hong Island's lagoon was beautiful and so peaceful so is definitely worth going through the tight squeeze of the caves for!

We got some time to ourselves to paddle our own canoes and also to swim if you felt like it before we headed to James Bond island! As we got there late, there was hardly any tourists there which was great - it was well worth the visit as it was beautiful! Our tour guide even spent time picking up perfect little shells to give us as a present!

All in all, it was a great day and we would recommend to anyone!
reviews.title: Wonderful day trip!

reviews.text: We did 2 tours with Khao Lak Land Discovery and would definately recommend them.  Easy to deal with 1st tour was booked within 10 minutes in the office in Khao Lak city and the 2nd with the link on the internet site which was extremely easy and received a confirmation email with 10 mins.  We went on the full day Phuket tour which was good and the send trip was the Elephant Trek and visit to a waterfall then lunch at a little restaurant next to the waterfall which was fantastic.  Chocke was our guide that day and he is a local and very informative and friendly.  Would highly recommend this organisation.
reviews.title: High Recommend - very easy

reviews.text: Very easy to deal with. Went in the office and booked the Similan snorkelling trip for two within 10 mins and agreed on a small discount too! Unfortunately the next day weather was not permitting so trip had to be cancelled and we were given the choice of full refund or organise another trip or day. We took the refund as we had other plans for the next few days already. Will definitely book with them again in the future for snorkelling trips.
reviews.title: Easy peasy

reviews.text:
During a week long stay in Khao Lak I did 2 tours with Khao LakLand Discoveries: the snorkelling day trip to the Surin Islands and the Elephant Bath half day tour. 

SURIN ISLANDS: The trip to the Surin islands was fantastic. I was lucky enough to fit into the last one for the season so there were about 30 of us in total but we were the only boat there so it wasn't crowded. I was picked up promptly from the hotel and driven to the pier. I had my own mask and snorkel but was provided with a set of fins which were of excellent quality. I was also given a towel and a bag to store everything in. We boarded the boat which turned out to have a broken engine so had to return to the pier and change boats (a delay of approximately 40mins but we were given refreshments whilst we waited). We then boarded again and headed to the islands. We did 3 snorkels and were given a briefing before each. I noted (pleasingly) that the boats used line ropes rather than anchors and there was an emphasis on keeping the environment pristine. After the first snorkel we had a wonderful lunch at a beautiful beach. After the final 2 snorkels, we returned to shore at 5pm before returning to the hotel. The staff were great and made sure we remained hydrated throughout the day and answered questions anyone had. I would highly recommend this tour.

ELEPHANT BATH: A highlight of my trip, we were collected from the hotel and taken to the elephant camp. We met the mahout and his elephant then got up into the seat and were taken for a 50min ride through the camp. The mahout was very caring to his elephant, often letting her stop to munch on grass and even pick the odd coconut. He never hit her even when we was naughty and didn't want to walk anymore. Our mahout also got down at one stage and took photos for us as well which was nice. Upon returning to the camp we dismounted and gave the elephant a hand of bananas (purchased for 50 baht) and then stopped for a snack of watermelon and pineapple. We then headed to the lake to give the elephant a bath which was just that. We were waist deep in our swimmers with scrubbing brushes and the elephant splashed herself (and us) with water. It was wonderful. The bath lasted about 20mins and then we headed off to a lovely waterfall and restaurant for lunch. It was a local restaurant (I like supporting local business) that had excellent food, including massaman curry. We were returned to the hotel at 12:30pm which leaves time for afternoon activities. I would recommend this tour highly. The staff were nice and informed us about Thailand during the drives (including details about the tsunami) and answered all questions. The elephants are well cared for and appear happy. the tour was 1,850 baht (approx AUD$62) and is definitely money well spent!!
reviews.title: Nothing but positives to report!

reviews.text: This was a really nice half day trip. We went on a short trek on a beautiful big elephant. We then got to swim with and bathe the elephant after the trek which they apparently really like. That was one of the coolest things ive done. We fed her a basket of bananas which was also great fun. We then went to a nearby waterfall and had a lovely lunch which was a really nice end to the tour. Highly recommend this experience.
reviews.title: Unique lovely experience

reviews.text:
We just arrived home from a week in Khao Lak and while there we did three day tours with Khao Lak Land Discovery. From the moment we were picked up from our hotel on day one we were impressed with how professionally everything was run and how well looked after we were.

We went on the Hong Island / Phang Nga Bay, Similan Island and Khao Sok full day elephant tours and all three were fantastic fun, well run and the food was amazing. 

We highly recommend this tour company and have nothing but good words to say about them.
reviews.title: Fantastic tours - great guide, sights and food!

reviews.text: Just spent a week in Khao Lak and I must say the experience was made so much better thanks to Khao Lak Land Discovery. Their tours were very well organised and the staff were so friendly and accomodating. I did three tours - phang nga bay discovery & sea canoe, full day jungle safari and a snorkelling day trip to the similan islands. Very well planned and managed and I highly recommend them to anyone visiting Khao Lak!
reviews.title: Absolutely Stunning!!!

reviews.text:
We did the Khao Lak Nature Discovery Full Day and were very happy with our experience.  We got picked up in the morning and went to an Elephant camp where we rode an elephant through a jungle like setting.  It was very pleasant and the mahouts were informative and kind.  We then went to have a bath with the elephants...not everyone participated but we loved it!  It was a unique and cool experience getting in the water with the elephants.

Afterwards we went to a waterfall and explored that area.  We then had a delicious lunch at a nearby restaurant.  

All of the other passengers left at this point since they were only doing the 1/2 day tour...but my boyfriend and I signed up for the 2-3 hour jungle trek.  This was amazing!  An incredible hike...somewhat technical but very well paced and enjoyable.  We saw lots of cool bugs, waterfalls and beautiful nature.  We wore Keen hiking sandals with Vibram soles, but I'd recommend closed-toe shoes because of all of the leeches on the trail.  The tour company did provide protective foot and leg coverings so we were safe.  :)  It was easy to do the uphill but the down hill was tough (very steep) and I'd say it would have been impossible in the rain.

Throughout the day, our tour guide was Charin and he was very informative and great to hike with!  

A very enjoyable day.
reviews.title: Great day trip in Khao Lak!

reviews.text: We had a nice guide and a good day in the out back. We enjoyed the swimming and bathing with the elephants. Can recommend this trip.
reviews.title: Nice and well educated guide

reviews.text: very cute, very local and friendly.  after the group left waterfall we had it to ourselves and then lunch at local restaurant overlooking waterfall.  very nice.
reviews.title: Half day elephant wash

reviews.text:
We went on the Similan Islands snorkeling tour.
Punctual hotel pickup / drop-off and good safety demonstration at the dock pre-departure.  They even give you anti-motion sickness medication if you want it!
Pretty nice gear but make sure that your flippers don't have any cracks (one of mine did!).
The motor boat was nice and had two entry / exit ladders so you could get in and out of the boat quickly.  They also pack snacks and drinks (non-alcoholic) for you on the boat.
Our guide, Mark, spoke excellent English, German, and Thai.  He really considers all the factors that make a snorkeling trip successful like the tides, current, and crowds at each reef location.  He told us a lot about the places we saw and things to look for there.  Couldn't have asked for a better guide so ask for him!
Luck was on our side when we saw two sea turtles and an octopus at the first location!
Lunch was pretty elaborate for being a "packed lunch".  It featured three different dishes with rice as well as fried shrimp and watermelon!  We ate on this great little island.  It felt like we were in a movie it was so picturesque.
At the end of tour, you will be offered a really cool dvd featuring lots of pictures and videos taken of you during the tour.  They edit in shots of you swimming into a bunch of Similan Island wildlife footage too so it's like a National Geographic film- my husband and I love ours!
Although we were on a pretty tight budget, we decided to spurge for a Similan Island tour.  I'm so glad I booked it with Land Discovery!

note of caution:
-  don't forget to bring lots of sun protection!
-  things get wet on the boat when everyone gets out of the water so make sure you have a waterproof bag or a couple of plastic bags for your belongings
reviews.title: Good service, good guide and good equipment!

reviews.text:
Upon arriving in Khao Lak I booked 3 tours with Khao Lak Land Discovery, 1/2 day elephant trek, Phi Phi Island & a Phuket day trip. The staff were wonderful, always arriving on time with an air conditioned mini-bus or van. 
The elephant trek was great but nothing like I expected! Riding an elephant is not smooth, i was holding the back of the seat to prevent sliding off. The jungle was beautiful & lush & feeding the elephants back at the camp was fun. 
The trip to Phi Phi Island was great. The bus drops you at another tour company in Phuket and you join other tourists. The boat ride is fast, talking is almost impossible. The beach is stunning! I suggest walking through the island to the viewpoint on the other side, its a lovely walk through the jungle. This company does not let you off at Monkey Beach to see the monkeys however with the amount of people who were on the beach, I doubt you would see them if you could. We saw the ones up high on the cliff from the boat. Lunch was delicious and the snorkeling was wonderful. Beware there are plenty of sea anemones so watch your hands and feet! A great day all round!
The trip to Phuket was a great way to see Phuket on limited time. The views form the lookouts on the drive are amazing. The shopping is great and the beach is nice.
I strongly suggest using Khao Lak Land Discovery for your tours. The staff are friendly and efficient and the tours are great.
reviews.title: Fantastic tour company

reviews.text:
we went on the elephant safari on the 4.1.2011. it was well organised and the guides very very good the best part of the whole safari was taking a bath with the elephants this was an experience I shall never forget.it is only when you are this close(scrubbing them) that you realise how soft and inteligent they really are.
a wonderful experience
reviews.title: elephant bath

reviews.text:
We took the Jungle Safari Day trip - which included a bamboo raft trip, a stop at a waterful with lunch, some time at a beautiful beach, and an elephant ride.  They picked us up at our hotel in the morning.  We had a nice, small group.  The transportation was in an air conditioned van.  Our guide spoke fluent english and was very knowledgable and nice.  The highlight was the elephants.   After riding them, we could feed them bananas (purchased for 50 B).  You can interact with them and see how they interact with their handlers.  Was a fun, day.  
While on the elephant ride - we took a hike into the jungle and climbed some steep ladders.  Not for the elderly or handicapped!  Beautiful setting though.  
Be prepared to be wet from the raft trip, and swimming on the beach.  Best to wear swim wear under shorts for the day.
reviews.title: Incredibly fun day

reviews.text: Simply brilliant, well priced, polite, friendly, safe driver ... Thankyou James for a great day .   Oliver
reviews.title: Phang nga bay tour 2

reviews.text: We have just returned home from an amazing trip to Khao Lak, Thailand.  Whilst in Khao Lak it rained alot, but that didn't dampen our spirits.  We were keen to explore despite the weather and we were determined to ride an elephant before we went home.  Khao Lak Land Discovery allowed us to do this.  They were one of the only companies willing to take us out on a rainy day, from the moment Charin picked us up from the resort we knew we were in for a great experience.  We were the only two people on the tour that day and Charin rolled out the red carpet.  He happily informed us about what the morning would involve and gave us loads of information about the elephants.  Things were so well organised and the experience was great, not just a 'tourist attrcation' so to speak.  We got to ride through the reserve and then through the jungle for around an hour on the elephant and then it was feeding time which was awesome!  We then went to a small seaside restaurant for lunch followed by a visit to the waterfall.  Overall we give this experience a 10/10.  Charin we thank you, you were a fantastic and knowledgeable guide who made this experience fantastic, we can see you are passionate about your job!  We look forward to seeing you again later in the year :)
reviews.title: 10/10 for Khao Lak Land Discovery!

reviews.text:
The temples were very memorable the history and religion behind them.
Unfortunately, our Guid was too unresponsive to serious questions about the female side. Although he meant if he didn't know something he would say it too that he didn't. Also as far as wildlife is concerned, the statement that they are lower life forms and have no standing in religion but you are punished more if you kill an elephant than a human. In this day and age a no go sorry
reviews.title: Temple tour in German

reviews.text: We booked the tour through booking.com. On the drive to the boat we were with four adults and 5 children. That was a good group size. Our guide - Ivan - not a local himself, has been at our side - especially the participating children - in German at any time with advice and deed and gave some tips for the further holiday. That was very good. The whole day was very well plated, the individual spots well selected. Especially the rich and very good food in the stilt village should be highlighted here. We can recommend the tour - with our guide.
reviews.title: All-round successful day
reviews.review_of: James Bond Phang Nga with canoe Full Day Tour

reviews.text:
Very ambivalent!
We have booked the "3 days Khao Sok tree house and lake" (not listed here) for the price of 12,500BHT per person. Sounded very good in the prospectus and also in the advice. Unfortunately, the facts were quite different. First day with market visit to Takua Pa, elephant camp, visit to Wat Tam Panturat Temple, canoe trip in the wilderness of Sok River and overnight stay at Rock an Treehouse Resort with lunch and dinner just PERFECT! 
On the second day we continued to Cheow Land Stau-See. As the journey on the lake towards the Floss House grew, the impressive surrounding scenery became more and more positively impressed. The control of the raft house was still visually beautiful. Then followed the disillusionments:
- 
The accommodations were simple (no objection to that), but if these "chambers" are then clearly dirty, in the sanitary area also mold was still present, the "enjoyment" stops suddenly.
-
The meals at lunch and dinner were largely identical, but in the evening there was another, let's call it "fried" fish, which was on its way to the Dörr fish in the processing by the chef (?).
In the morning there was a frugal breakfast consisting of a slice of toast, a cold fried egg and 3 pineapple particles and two small melon slices. IT WAS NO LONGER THERE!
-
And for the use of the canoes in the lake "resort" we had to pay 200 BHT on site, which was not mentioned in the brochure and also the personal advice was not mentioned.
-
The stay was complemented by EXTREMELY unfriendly staff.

We and also our 2 fellow travellers were happy when we went back to the starting point and we were able to establish for ourselves: 
- beautiful panorama
- and having seen 3 wild elephants at dinner on one of the boat game drives,

and were now away from the accommodation.

The program of the third day was, not necessarily, to remember it. 
All points, such as simple accommodation on the lake and additional payment for the use of the canoes were then the subject of a letter to the agency / organizer. I was not concerned with any refunds due to poor performance(s). I just wanted information about how it could have happened,

I received an answer which my questions answered only rudimentarily, at least with the realisation that the author of the answer (including our consultant) admitted that he had perhaps, possibly, not argued quite objectively in the tender. I then wrote to the author again with the request to go into the exact points and how the topic with the 200BHT is to watch. I'll wait until today for the answer.

We did not realize our reaction in the form of "planned other additional booking" due to the experience and behavior of the agency at the agency.

Worth mentioning is the good tour guide, the equally good driver and the vehicle used.
reviews.title: At / with the agency not again

reviews.text: We had already booked the tour through Khaolak-Adventures.de in Germany. At the hotel in Khao Lak we were picked up on time. As a guide we had Anton?, he called himself the Flying Dutchman as he ran from shadow to shadow. He was able to give us very good insights into Buddhism, as he himself lived as a monk for 12 years. This tour was completely successful. Whether it was about the history of temples, Buddhism or the life of Thais, he could tell us a lot and entertainingly about it. The food was also very good.
reviews.title: It was an all-round successful day

reviews.text: Unfortunately, neither the offroad ride, nor the waterfall were really great... the waterfall was just a little trickle in the bushes and the offroad ride were 10 minutes over an agricultural gravel road.... Apart from potholes, there was nothing with off-road... and I must also say that we were really unlucky with our guide. There were 4 children who actually only disturbed him and questions from them did not answer. He was very disrespectful to them. He was also not motivated to take children's food requests into account. But we had already done some tours with khao lak Discovery, and knew that there is food for children. only on several requests, he ordered it... even the guides from the raft trip were not bothered to show us anything... so for us the trip was a real shame for the money...
reviews.title: Only partially recommended

reviews.text:
We had a very good tour with a very good guide named Heiko. He showed us a lot and explained, you could tell that he loved the country and the people and liked to pass on his knowledge. We booked the tour Khao Sok 2 days with an overnight stay at Rock&treehouse Resort. We saw a market, were allowed to feed and bathe elephants, saw a temple, visited the dam, hiked through the jungle, visited a cave and still received information about the land, people, wildlife and plants in between. 
Highly recommended.
reviews.title: Wonderful tour Khao Sok with overnight stay

reviews.text: The tour Khao Sok Jungle Trekking was a wonderful experience thanks to our tour guide Aris. She planned the process so that we could discover the Jungle away from the other visitors. Your information was very interesting. For me as a hobby photographer, she was always looking for the best possible locations for my photos. She also paid attention to our well-being. The pace was adjusted, breathers were taken and the bathing opportunity has cooled and wonderful. She chose the paths off the beaten track so that even less experienced people could still keep up. I can fully recommend this tour and especially tour guide Aris.
reviews.title: An eventful day in the jungle with many exotic animals

reviews.text:
We did the 3-temple tour with Volker and were absolutely thrilled. Volker was mega entertaining and at the same time very informative – he was just incredibly happy to be listened to.

The tour was super planned, there were always chilled drinks and refreshing towels, and we felt in good hands at all times. It was an excursion that we not only enjoyed a lot, but also had the opportunity to learn a lot about the country, people and culture.

Thank you for this great experience!
Elisabeth & Mario 🌺
reviews.title: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Khao Lak Discovery – Highly recommended!

reviews.text:
We did a tour to the elephants, it was just breathtaking to see the animal, feed them and end up bathing with them. 
Many thanks to our guide Ivan who explained everything to us great, explained a lot of interesting things about the elephants and took beautiful pictures of us!
reviews.title: Stunning close to the elephants

reviews.text: An excellently organized tour with a knowledgeable guide. It gave a little insight into everyday religious life in Thailand.
reviews.title: Worth seeing

reviews.text:
Everything worked perfectly and we had a sensational day.
Picked up at the hotel in 7:15. We were a small group together with another family. After an hour of driving in a very comfortable MiniVan we switched to the longtail. It was a very nice drive directly to James Bond Island. Depending on the water level, the canoe tour can also take place beforehand.
